title: GTC 2026 Keynote
abstract: In this keynote, NVIDIA founder and CEO Jensen Huang looks ahead to the future of accelerated computing and AI, and how they will shape the next era of computing across every industry.

title: Open Models: Where We Are and Where We’re Headed
abstract:
Doors open at 11:30 a.m. Session begins at 12:30 p.m.


One of the most important shifts in AI over the past year has been the rapid advancement of open frontier models. Open innovation is accelerating progress across every company and every industry, making it clear that AI will proliferate everywhere.


NVIDIA founder and CEO Jensen Huang hosts industry leaders for a candid discussion on the state of the art in open frontier models and what comes next.

title: Safety in the OpenClaw Era
abstract:
AI has crossed a threshold. Autonomous agents like OpenClaw, no longer just assist but hold credentials, persist context across sessions, spawn subagents, and execute continuously against production systems. That's a fundamentally different threat model than anything enterprise security was built to handle. The attack surface is real. Every prompt injection is a potential credential leak. Every third-party skill is an unreviewed binary. Every major enterprise platform is becoming an agent platform, and the governance infrastructure to match that reality doesn't exist at scale yet.
This panel brings together security and AI leaders to ask the hard questions: what does a real security model for autonomous agents look like? 
The agents are ready. The infrastructure to trust them is what we're here to discuss.

title: Advancing to AI's Next Frontier: Insights From Jeff Dean and Bill Dally
abstract: In this 60-minute wide-ranging discussion, NVIDIA Chief Scientist and GPU architect Bill Dally engages in a focused dialogue with Google's Chief Scientist Jeff Dean, co-instigator of TPUs, overall Gemini co-tech lead, and pioneer in large-scale ML systems. The conversation explores the critical intersections of hardware innovation, systems scaling, and algorithmic advancement needed to propel AI into the 2026–2030 era of agentic systems, ultra-low-latency reasoning, and energy-efficient scaling.

speakers.name: Bill Dally
speakers.jobTitle: Chief Scientist and SVP of Research

title: Automotive Special Address: Advancing Level 4 Autonomy, the Path to Scalable, Safe AVs and Robotaxis
abstract: Autonomous vehicles (AVs) are emerging as the first mass-deployed physical AI agents, reshaping mobility and driving new demands in AI, safety, and scalable development. This special address explores the industry’s path to Level 4 autonomy and how NVIDIA is helping accelerate it. With the DRIVE Hyperion architecture, manufacturers (OEMs) and AV developers can build scalable, safety-certified autonomy solutions. NVIDIA will present its cloud-to-car platform—spanning training, simulation, and in-vehicle compute—to support developing and validating next-generation drive-policy models. The session will also touch on how agentic AI is poised to transform AVs end to end, from automating data-driven development and validation to enabling more intelligent, adaptive in-car experiences. Gain insights into L4 software architectures, the DRIVE Hyperion platform, and ecosystem collaboration across OEMs, AV developers, mobility providers, and robotaxi companies.

title: Building the Future of Music and AI, With Sir Lucian Grainge From Universal Music Group
abstract: Join leaders from NVIDIA and Universal Music Group for a conversation exploring how technology innovation accelerated by artificial intelligence is reshaping the future of music and creative industries. The history of music transformation through technology over the course of one of the music industry's most influential careers will set the stage, with details about Sir Lucian’s experience engaging with artists on tech innovation and a focus on his vision for an AI partnership where technology and creativity can work together at scale. Moderated by Richard Kerris, general manager of Media2 at NVIDIA, former CTO of Lucasfilm, and previous global lead for Apple’s entertainment industry developer ecosystem, the discussion will offer candid insights and real-world use cases on how collaboration between tech and culture leaders is defining the next era of entertainment.

title: Diffusion Unlocked: Advanced Techniques for Training, Inference, and Deployment
abstract:
Diffusion models have exploded from research labs into the mainstream, powering everything from photorealistic image generation to creative co-pilots. But moving these models from a notebook to a scalable, low-latency, production-ready service is one of the greatest engineering challenges in modern AI. The "magic" of generation hides a complex stack of optimization and infrastructure hurdles.

We will bypass high-level concepts to debate the concrete architectural trade-offs required for low latency and high throughput, covering unique training challenges, up-and-coming model architectures, inference and runtime optimization, low-precision and NVFP4, and scaling topologies.

title: Disney’s Robotic Characters: From the Screen to Reality via Physical AI
abstract: The translation of animated characters like Olaf, or the design of characters like our BDX Droids, requires balancing artistic fidelity with the constraints of the physical world. This talk explores how these robotic characters achieve that balance through the integration of modular mechatronics and deep reinforcement learning. Leveraging GPU-accelerated simulation to handle complex mechanisms, we train control policies that enable our characters to self-balance and self-regulate temperature. We further demonstrate progress in believable, autonomous navigation and human-robot interaction driven by onboard perception.

title: How Open Models, Agents, and Physical AI Are Fueling a Flywheel of Healthcare Innovation
abstract: Healthcare is entering a once-in-a-generation leap as AI, accelerated computing, and robotics converge to turn data into decisions and accelerate breakthrough discovery. At GTC 2026, NVIDIA will showcase how open foundation models, agentic AI, and physical AI are creating entirely new categories of applications across care delivery, drug discovery, and laboratory operations. AI for science is closing the loop between digital dry labs and autonomous wet labs, catalyzing biology’s transformer moment. Healthcare robotics that combine digital and physical agents are closing care gaps around the world at historic speed. Together with industry leaders, NVIDIA is building the AI-powered platforms that are enabling this next generation of medicine.

title: Inside the NVIDIA AI Platform and Ecosystem
abstract: A deeper dive into key announcements from the keynote with a focus on enabling technologies, predicted effects, and opportunities across industries.

title: Open Source AI Shaping the Next Era of Intelligent Digital Workers
abstract: Open source AI is redefining how we build and scale intelligence. In this session, we explore how AI factories unlock a new generation of digital workers who learn continuously, collaborate with humans, and drive breakthroughs across every industry.

title: The Future of Industrial Autonomy
abstract: This panel brings together industry leaders from Honeywell Industrial, KION Group, Caterpillar, Medtronic, Hitachi Rail and GXO to explore how advanced AI and edge computing are reshaping the future of industrial and healthcare systems. Together, they will discuss the major trends accelerating edge intelligence—from autonomous operations and multi-modal sensor fusion to real-time decision-making and digital twins—and share high-impact AI use cases driving measurable business growth and societal value. The conversation will highlight challenges in deploying scalable, safety-critical edge AI and reveal how these global innovators are architecting their next-generation solutions using NVIDIA’s accelerated computing and end-to-end edge platform.

speakers.name: Deepu Talla
speakers.jobTitle: Vice President of Robotics and Edge AI

title: Accelerate the Physical AI Era With Digital Twins and Real-Time Simulation
abstract: Join Rev Lebaredian, NVIDIA’s VP of Omniverse and simulation technology, as he shares how autonomous, OpenClaw-based agents, digital twins and real-time simulation—powered by OpenUSD and NVIDIA’s accelerated computing platform, libraries, and ecosystem—are accelerating breakthroughs across industries in the era of physical AI. Discover their impact on everything from product design and manufacturing to robotics, autonomous vehicles, smart cities, climate science, and space exploration.

speakers.name: Rev Lebaredian
speakers.jobTitle: VP of Omniverse and Simulation Technology

title: Artist to Audience: The Developer Platform for Real-Time AI in Media
abstract: In this session, we’ll show how real-time AI is becoming the new fabric of modern media—connecting artist workflows to studio pipelines to distribution at global scale. You’ll learn the reference architecture behind an AI-native media stack: accelerated compute, low-latency networking, and developer-ready SDKs and APIs that let teams build, integrate, and ship AI into real production systems. We’ll walk through practical examples across creation, live production, enhancement, understanding, and delivery—focusing on what it takes to move from demos to deployed workflows, with measurable gains in speed, quality, and cost.

title: Build Trust and Discovery Through Open‑Source AI in Research
abstract: Open-source AI is essential for trust, learning, and discovery in education and research, but only if we can inspect, reproduce, and improve how models are made. In this session, Professor Hanna Hajishirzi will present Olmo 3: a fully open language and “thinking” model, plus complete model flows—the full life cycle of checkpoints, data, and dependencies—enabling customization and scientific inspection beyond final weights. Professor Percy Liang will introduce Marin, an open lab for frontier AI where experiments happen in public, with community review and contributions via GitHub, advancing reproducibility, benchmarking, and data-efficient training. Together, these approaches outline a path to verifiable, improvable AI systems that broaden access and accelerate open scientific progress.

speakers.name: Percy Liang
speakers.company: Co-Founder, Together AI, Co-Founder, Simile AI
speakers.jobTitle: Associate Professor of Computer Science, Stanford University

title: CUDA: New Features and Beyond
abstract: The CUDA platform is the foundation of the GPU computing ecosystem. Every application and framework that uses the GPU does so through CUDA's libraries, compilers, runtimes and language—which means CUDA is growing as fast as its ecosystem is evolving. Presented by one of the architects of CUDA, at this engineering-focused talk you will learn about all that's new and what's coming next for both CUDA and GPU computing as a whole.

title: Empowering Human Creativity: A Discussion With Paramount Skydance’s Dane Glasgow
abstract: Whether it was the transition from black-and-white to color or from linear to streaming, the entertainment industry is well-versed in innovation, transformation, and disruption. As content produced by AI models becomes more widespread, media companies face a unique set of issues as the industry works to protect its intellectual property and maintain the trust of its creatives and audiences. In a fireside chat with Paramount’s Chief Product Officer Dane Glasgow, we will hear how he and his company leverage new and emerging technologies in support of their employees and the creative community, while taking definitive measures to protect its IP and storytelling.

title: Enterprise AI Platforms and AI Agents to Business Value: How Enterprise AI Leaders are Scaling AI
abstract: The era of agentic AI is upon us. Generative AI and agentic architectures are no longer novelties—they’re rapidly becoming core infrastructure in leading enterprises. In this panel, moderated by NVIDIA’s vice president of enterprise AI, leading chief AI officers, CIOs, and AI platform leaders from NVIDIA, customers will share candid perspectives on building, scaling, and governing AI platforms and agentic systems in real-world settings. They will discuss how to move beyond pilot projects to large-scale adoption, manage infrastructure and inference pipelines, integrate agents into business workflows, and maintain trust, transparency, and resilience. Join us to hear from those who are navigating the challenges of enterprise AI transformation at scale.

title: From Concept to Production: Humanoid Robotics at Scale
abstract: Humanoid robots are evolving from prototypes to production-ready systems, powered by breakthroughs in reasoning AI, large-scale simulation, and real-time edge computing. But to get there, the industry must bridge the gap between digital intelligence and physical reality. Hear from luminaries at the world’s leading robotics companies on the future of physical AI, and what it will take to unlock truly capable humanoids.

speakers.name: Amit Goel
speakers.jobTitle: Head of Robotics and Edge Computing Ecosystem

title: How NASCAR Uses GPU-Accelerated Simulation for Safer, More Exciting Racing
abstract: Go under the hood of NASCAR’s rigorous engineering workflow, where computational fluid dynamics (CFD) now accounts for 80% of all aerodynamic development. We'll explain how simulation drives critical decisions regarding driver safety, thermal management, and the racing package itself—specifically through "liftoff safety" studies and multi-car traffic modeling. This is made possible by NASCAR’s transition from CPU-based CFD to the CUDA-X accelerated Siemens Simcenter STAR-CCM+ package, in partnership with TotalSim and the Ohio Supercomputer Center. Leveraging the latest NVIDIA GB200 GPUs, this workflow delivers a 10x improvement in turnaround time, enabling larger, higher-fidelity multi-physics models.

title: How Open World Models are Powering the Next Breakthroughs in Physical AI
abstract: Generating the photorealistic data and robust reasoning capabilities needed for machines to understand real-world complexity remains one of the biggest bottlenecks in physical AI. NVIDIA Cosmos world foundation models are at the core of AI models and agents that can perceive, understand, and adapt to the real-world like never before. Learn how developers can use Cosmos open models and tools to accelerate training, customize pipelines, and deploy faster across robotics, autonomous vehicles, and vision AI agents. This session will also explore how the latest Cosmos updates advance physics-aware learning, unlock simulation-to-real capabilities, and enable long-running agents and frameworks like OpenClaw to orchestrate continuous synthetic data generation and reasoning workflows at scale.

title: How Simulation and AI are Giving Life to Digital Twins for Patient Care
abstract: Simulation has evolved from a niche research tool to a cornerstone of modern healthcare innovation. Early breakthroughs focused on GPUs to accelerate physical interactions for device design and testing. Today, the scope is much broader—incorporating physiological processes such as respiration, cardiac motion, and electrophysiology. These comprehensive simulations are already being used to conduct virtual clinical trials and train autonomous surgical robots. We're helping to build the future in which AI is used to design, accelerate, and interpret these simulations, enabling digital twins that guide the care of each and every patient. Throughout the day, as your smart watch records your activities or during surgical procedures when unexpected events occur, your AI-enabled digital twin will be there to detect, diagnose, and suggest the treatment that provides the best outcome for you.

speakers.name: Mark Palmer
speakers.jobTitle: Field CTO and Lead Chief Technologist, Healthcare

title: How We Scaled Kimi K2.5
abstract: Explore how we scaled Kimi K2.5 by pioneering the Muon optimizer to double token learning efficiency and maximize training throughput through Day 0 infrastructure co-design. Gain insights into AI-native training and linear attention architectures to help you unlock the potential of longer-running agents.

title: Open, Trusted, and Observable: Deploying AI Agents at Enterprise Scale
abstract: As AI agents move from prototype to production, enterprises face a critical challenge: how do you deploy agentic systems at scale while maintaining trust, visibility, and control? This session explores how organizations are building production-grade agents using LangSmith—the commercial platform for agent observability, evaluation, and deployment from the LangChain team—combined with the NVIDIA AI-Q blueprint. Learn how an open, trusted approach, paired with end-to-end tracing, enables teams to debug complex agent behaviors, drive optimizations across the full stack from model selection to infrastructure, and confidently deploy agents in safe, governed production environments.

title: Practical Context Engineering: Eliminate Bugs With High-Signal AI Code Reviews
abstract:
AI-assisted coding has sped up code generation, but human-only code reviews are now the bottleneck. Too many pull requests (PRs), not enough reviewer bandwidth. This session shows how you can build the right context-engineering architecture that helps LLMs deliver high-signal AI code reviews. 

Catch logical or functional bugs, memory leaks, security vulnerabilities, code refactors, edge cases, and more. You'll see how bringing in the right context from external datasets (such as code graphs, PR history, issue tickets, MCP servers, and linters) yields better-quality reviews of PR diffs. You will leave with a practical playbook to pilot and scale AI code reviews that increase your release velocity with fewer bugs.

title: Scaling AI Agents Globally Across Brands, Use Cases, and Restaurants
abstract: Scaling and deploying AI into restaurant operations requires unique technology and partners. Learn how Yum! Brands, the world’s largest restaurant company, is using NVIDIA software to scale intelligence into their restaurant technology, expanding use cases, applications, and partners.

title: Securing Coding Assistants
abstract: As AI-powered coding assistants become more capable—and begin interacting directly with developer environments—their security risks grow alongside their utility. This session offers a practical demonstration of how vulnerabilities can emerge when these assistants gain computer-use capabilities, exposing new attack surfaces inside the software development life cycle. We then dive into Cursor’s real-world mitigation strategies, showing how hard security controls around critical functions create a flexible balance between capability and risk. Finally, we connect lessons from AI security research, secure application design, and red-teaming exercises to illustrate how organizations can safely unlock higher levels of automation. Together, these practices chart a path toward powerful AI development tools with far lower operational risk.

title: Security for the AI-First Enterprise
abstract: As AI transforms every layer of modern computing, security must evolve in parallel—shifting from a separate function to an embedded capability woven into every stage of innovation. Today’s CISOs and security leaders are moving beyond reactive defense, redefining their roles as proactive business enablers who accelerate safe adoption of AI, cloud, and emerging technologies. This talk explores how that shift is reshaping security architectures, operating models, and organizational influence. Finally, it highlights why meaningful collaboration across industry, academia, and government is no longer optional. Addressing global cyber threats at AI scale demands shared intelligence, coordinated standards, and partnerships that blend cutting-edge research with real-world deployment. Together, these forces define the future of secure innovation.

title: Shaping the Next Era of Autonomous Driving Through Safety-Centered AI
abstract:
Achieving Level 4 (L4) autonomy at global scale requires both technical mastery and a verifiable safety case to earn public trust. This panel brings together engineering, mobility, and regulatory leaders from across the autonomous vehicle ecosystem to examine the breakthroughs enabling trustworthy autonomous vehicle deployment. 


We’ll explore the core challenges: integrating large foundation models, using reasoning vision-language-action models to handle edge cases, generating billions of validation miles using physically accurate simulation, and using unified safety frameworks like NVIDIA Halos to achieve certification and regulatory compliance. You’ll learn how teams are translating abstract safety mandates into verifiable software and hardware constraints to deliver truly trustworthy autonomy.

title: Simulating Reality Inside General World Models
abstract: This session explores how world models are emerging as a critical next step in advancing AI, enabling applications across physical AI, entertainment, and beyond. We'll go inside Runway's GWM-1: its architecture, capabilities, and why we believe video diffusion-based world models are the most direct path to general-purpose simulation. We'll also discuss the research and infrastructure challenges involved in deploying world models that run in real time.

title: Supercharging AI With Multi-Gigawatt AI Factories
abstract: Join NVIDIA experts and industry leaders for a deep dive into the hard-won lessons from building massive AI infrastructure, the newest strategies for scaling network performance across clusters, and forward-looking insights into the next generation of AI networking. This session cuts through the noise with practical guidance and bold predictions shaped by real-world deployments at hyperscale.

title: The 50-State Plan: Public-Private Models for AI Infrastructure and University Transformation
abstract:
As AI becomes the definitive engine of economic and academic growth, the race is on to democratize access to high performance computing across the United States. This panel explores "The 50-State Plan," a strategic roadmap for building robust, statewide AI infrastructure through innovative public-private partnerships. The session brings together visionary tech-industry pioneers, leaders from the National Science Foundation (NSF), and executives from top-tier research universities to discuss the shift from isolated pockets of innovation to unified strategies for university transformation and mission success in the age of AI.

Join us to learn how your organization can participate in this nationwide effort to bridge the digital divide and accelerate the next era of discovery.

speakers.name: Jack Wells
speakers.jobTitle: Director of Higher Education and Research Computing

title: The AI-XR Co-Scientist Lab: How Stanford - Princeton's LabOS is Accelerating Advanced Science With XR
abstract: What if we could dramatically shorten the path from discovery to lifesaving therapy? Discover how Stanford Medicine’s LabOS, an AI extended reality (XR) operating system, makes this a reality. Learn how LabOS drives CRISPR GPT, a groundbreaking AI agent to design complex experiments and deliver step-by-step instructions through augmented reality glasses. LabOS unites computational reasoning with physical experimentation, built on NVIDIA Cosmos-powered XR/AI system. By connecting multi-model AI agents, smart glasses, and robots, LabOS allows AI to see what scientists see, understand experimental context, and assist in real-time execution. From immunotherapy to stem cell engineering, LabOS turns any lab into an intelligent environment where human and machine discovery evolve together—not just accelerating research, but delivering personalized, accessible therapies at unprecedented scale.

title: The Creative OS: AI Built With Human Imagination at the Center
abstract: Explore how Canva engineered AI systems with foundational models that understand creative context; preserve artistic control; and amplify, rather than replace, imagination. We'll share technical approaches for building AI platforms and products where AI becomes an invisible enabler, letting designers focus on their work, from editable designs to video, from web apps to interactive experiences.

title: The Era of GPU Data Processing: From SQL to Search and Back Again
abstract: This session delivers a technical state of the union on GPU-accelerated data processing across SQL/DataFrames, vector search, ML, and decision optimization. Learn how GPU-native engines enable interactive analytics on massive lakehouse-scale datasets, real-time semantic and vector search over billions of embeddings, and makes the hardest ML and decision science workloads tractable, cost-efficient, and energy-efficient. The talk highlights the implications for high-impact scientific and enterprise computing, then looks ahead to what’s in flight for 2026 and beyond, outlining concrete architectural patterns and practical guidance for building the next generation of GPU-accelerated data platforms and using them in your day-to-day work.

speakers.name: Joshua Patterson
speakers.jobTitle: VP, Solutions Architecture - Accelerating Data Processing

title: Turning Models Into Engines: The AI Factory Era
abstract: Step inside Mistral’s journey to industrial-scale AI as we share lessons learned and the principles guiding our approach in creating Mistral Compute. Built on four key pillars—open-source foundations, security by design, sustainability at scale, and digital sovereignty—we’ll share how our approach unlocks bare-metal GPU performance, why that matters in building trusted, European-hosted future-ready AI systems, and how we’re bringing collaboration to the next level by giving innovators access to the same infrastructure powering our models.

title: Accelerating Industrial Engineering: From Product Design to Manufacturing in the AI Supercomputing Era
abstract: Accelerated computing and AI supercomputing are redefining industrial engineering, spanning computational engineering (CAE) to semiconductor design and manufacturing. Together, these fields drive how the world conceives, simulates, and builds everything from advanced machinery to next-generation chips increasingly in the digital world before physical production begins. In this address, NVIDIA’s Tim Costa, general manager for Industrial and Computational Engineering, will show how CUDA-accelerated platforms, AI physics, DGX and cloud-scale AI infrastructure, and NVIDIA Omniverse are reshaping workflows across computer-aided engineering and semiconductor design and manufacturing. He will demonstrate how customers use GPU-accelerated solvers, AI physics models, and agentic AI to cut turnaround, broaden design exploration, and boost yield and reliability, revealing a unified accelerated-computing blueprint spanning classical, AI, and emerging quantum acceleration.

speakers.name: Timothy Costa
speakers.jobTitle: General Manager (GM) for Industrial and Computational Engineering

title: Advancing Autonomous Vehicles With World Models
abstract: Simulation remains a cornerstone for accelerating robotics and autonomous vehicle (AV) development, enabling policies to be evaluated ahead of real-world deployment and drastically reducing development time. Achieving meaningful results requires simulators that capture both the environment and the world’s response to AI agents with high fidelity, across diverse and challenging scenarios. NVIDIA Cosmos has been specialized for autonomous vehicles, incorporating novel architecture changes and optimizations to deliver real-time simulation for the first time. In this talk, we explore the breakthrough that powers closed-loop evaluation of AV policies, empowering developers to iterate faster, test more rigorously, and bring safer, more robust autonomous systems to the real world.

title: Agentic AI 101
abstract:
Agentic AI is evolving rapidly, shifting AI systems from passive responders to active systems that can plan, reason, and take action. In this session, we break down what agentic AI is, how the space has changed in the last year, and how both frontier models and open models are enabling a new class of AI applications.

You will learn the core components of agentic systems, where different model types fit, and practical ways to get started building agents today. This session is designed to give developers and technical leaders a clear mental model for agentic AI and a concrete path from experimentation to real applications.

speakers.name: Erik Pounds
speakers.jobTitle: Sr. Director, Product Marketing, Enterprise AI

title: AI Research Breakthroughs from NVIDIA Research (Hosted by Karoly of Two Minute Papers)
abstract: Don't miss this exciting live panel discussion, hosted by Károly Zsolnai-Fehér (better known as the creator of Two Minute Papers), featuring top researchers from NVIDIA Research. They'll dive into the latest breakthroughs in AI, spotlight the most promising emerging technical trends, and candidly explore the biggest open challenges facing the field today. Join us for an interactive, high-energy conversation packed with cutting-edge insights!

title: An Introduction to the Newton Physics Engine for Robotics
abstract: Robots learn faster and safer in simulation, but complex joints, balance, and contact-rich motions challenge current physics engines. Developers need high-fidelity simulation so virtual training transfers safely to real robots. This session with NVIDIA, Google DeepMind, Skild AI, and Disney Research introduces Newton, an open-source, GPU-accelerated physics engine built on NVIDIA Warp and OpenUSD to advance robot learning. Newton’s flexible design and multiple solvers enable realistic simulations—like walking through snow, or handling cups and fruit—and reliable deployment in the physical world. Explore Newton’s architecture, integration into robot-learning pipelines, and ways to develop new solvers for safer, more capable robots across diverse environments and tasks.

title: Autonomous AI Factories: A Technical Preview of Agent-Native Production
abstract:
For two decades, the "self-healing system" was just a theory. Today, it's an architectural reality. This session unveils the blueprint for the fully autonomous AI factory—a cognitive system where fleets of specialized agents replace static operations such as issues detection and troubleshooting to deliver true self-governance.

We'll move beyond the hype of simple chatbots to deconstruct a multi-agent architecture capable of complex reasoning, predictive failure analysis, and continuous self-improvement. But we won’t just tell you about it; we will show you.

This session features a high-stakes LIVE DEMO of an autonomous multi-agent swarm connected to a large-scale production system. Watch in real time as agents detect (and predict) anomalies and use advanced tools for troubleshooting as the first self-healing AI platform for autonomous AI factories.

speakers.name: JP Vasseur
speakers.jobTitle: Sr. Distinguished Engineer and Chief Architect

title: Beyond Guardrails: Best Practices Across the Industry for Trustworthy AI
abstract: Join Margaret Mitchell, Hugging Face chief ethics scientist, and Nikki Pope, head of AI and legal ethics at NVIDIA, in a conversation about how their respective companies are delivering trustworthy artificial intelligence products and tools.

speakers.name: Nikki Pope
speakers.jobTitle: Sr. Director, AI and Legal Ethics

title: Bringing Physical AI to Smart Spaces
abstract: Creating truly scalable AI solutions for cities is a massive challenge, one that requires many AI models and platforms (and tons of data) working in harmony to make it possible. Announced at GTC Paris, NVIDIA Blueprint for smart city AI leverages 3-computer to activate physical AI and apply to cities for a unified, real-time intelligence solution for creating a smart city. Learn about the latest workflow updates. Linker Vision will share how they've been able to apply the blueprint with U.S. cities, from training models with synthetic data and NVIDIA Cosmos to deploying video analytics AI agents using NVIDIA Metropolis Blueprint for Video Search and Summarization (VSS) that leverages the fleet of camera sensors deployed in cities—an end-to-end approach to processing tens of thousands of camera streams for proactive automation of city operations, safety, and planning.

title: Building Shopify’s Commerce Foundation Models
abstract: Modern commerce platforms must support millions of entrepreneurs while serving billions of buyers with personalized recommendations across massive product catalogs. To bridge this gap, we introduce Shopify’s Commerce Foundation Models: sequence autoregressive models built on the HSTU encoder to represent the journeys of merchants and buyers. Key innovations include generalizing to multiple tasks, large-scale contrastive learning, temporal encoding that fuses multi-scale time signals with commerce-specific features, and the scaling to petabytes volumes of data training and on-demand inference via CUDA kernels developed in collaboration with NVIDIA. While results are promising, challenges remain in handling non-stationary preferences and multi-objective optimization. We discuss our roadmap toward truly foundational commerce models that generalize across domains and market conditions.

title: Building the Future of Manufacturing
abstract: The manufacturing industry is entering a new era, driven by AI, robotics, and simulation. Join distinguished leaders from the semiconductor and electronics, industrial and process manufacturing, and robotics industries as they share their pioneering use cases and how these technologies enable them to innovate, reimagine production systems, and transform the design and operation of advanced manufacturing and warehouse facilities.

speakers.name: Rev Lebaredian
speakers.jobTitle: VP of Omniverse and Simulation Technology

title: Building the Next Wave of AI-Enabled Robots
abstract: Building generalist robots is a complex AI challenge—developers must ensure these machines can safely adapt across industries, from manufacturing floors to hospital corridors. Join this lightning session featuring founders and technologists from robotics startups in the NVIDIA Inception program as they share real-world lessons from tackling these problems at scale. Hear how they're leveraging NVIDIA CUDA-accelerated libraries, foundation AI models, and simulation frameworks built with Omniverse to build and deploy AI-driven robotics products—what worked, what didn't, and why. Gain insights on architectures, deployment patterns, and measurable customer impact across manufacturing, healthcare, retail, and food service.

title: Building Towards Self-Driving Codebases with Long-Running, Asynchronous Agents
abstract: Aman Sanger, co-founder and CTO at Cursor, will share how Cursor is building long-running coding agents that can autonomously execute more ambitious software tasks.

title: Charting a Course for the Next Decade of Gaming With AI
abstract: AI is fundamentally transforming real-time applications, reshaping both content creation and player experiences. Join John Spitzer, VP of developer technology and performance at NVIDIA, for an industry panel featuring AI leaders from Creative Assembly, Decart, RIOT Games and Krafton. These panelists will explore the current state of the art—from AI digital humans to generating entire worlds instantaneously—and offer their vision for the future of AI-powered gaming.

speakers.name: John Spitzer
speakers.jobTitle: VP of Developer Performance and Technology

title: Deep Dive: Streaming Immersive Robotics Teleoperations
abstract: Join NVIDIA expert Jiwen Cai for a technical deep dive on architecting scalable teleoperation pipelines that bridge simulation and reality. Learn how to leverage NVIDIA CloudXR to stream high-fidelity, low-latency visuals to wireless headsets, unlocking operator comfort and precise dexterity for industrial robotics deployments and data collection at scale.

title: Expression About Expression: When AI Looks in the Mirror
abstract: Despite fears of replacement, we may be entering a new wave of human creativity. AI systems increasingly generate content that comments on themselves or reveals emergent behavior. What happens when human judgment and machine intelligence blend together? This panel looks at emerging forms of expression that arise from human–AI collaboration, including outputs that feel unfamiliar or self‑reflective.

speakers.name: Shari Reich
speakers.jobTitle: Manager of Developer Relations for Audio and Music

title: Fast-Tracking the Launch of L4 Robotaxis Across the Globe
abstract: Uber and NVIDIA are joining forces to accelerate the next generation of Level 4 robotaxi and autonomous delivery fleets, built on NVIDIA’s end‑to‑end AI architecture. This fireside chat will unpack Uber’s long‑term vision for deeply integrating robotaxis into its global marketplace to deliver safer, more reliable, and more sustainable urban mobility.

title: From Benchmark Silos to an Interoperable AI Evaluation Ecosystem
abstract: As agent benchmarks multiply, the AI research community is paying a growing integration tax, with each new benchmark requiring custom infrastructure and tooling. This session introduces Common Unified Benchmark Environments (CUBE), a universal benchmarking protocol built on MCP and Gym that defines shared APIs for tasks, benchmarks, packages, and registries. We explore CUBE’s design and show how it integrates with existing evaluation workflows, including early reference integrations validated in collaboration with NVIDIA using NeMo Gym and NeMo Evaluator. Learn how to adopt CUBE, integrate it into your own tooling, and contribute to a more interoperable and scalable benchmarking ecosystem.

title: From Prototype to Playable: Bringing Scalable ML Into "World of Tanks: HEAT"
abstract: This session explores how World of Tanks: HEAT plans to transition ML from research notebooks to live combat using NVIDIA Triton. We begin by defining the "strategic recommendation system" within a gaming context—covering data prep, model selection, and player-centric evaluation. From there, we dive into the infrastructure: why traditional in-process inference fails in high-intensity battles and how Triton standardizes deployment across CPU and GPU. Learn how we utilize ONNX, TensorRT, and dynamic batching to optimize high-frequency models and use Triton’s Model Analyzer to ensure peak performance before players hit the battlefield.

title: GRAVEX: GPU-Accelerated GAN-Diffusion Representation for Counterfeit Pattern Analysis With MONAI-Based Validation
abstract: We demonstrate how systematically reusing and fine-tuning discriminators from generative models yields robust deepfake detection across diverse synthesis methods. By integrating scale-adaptive features from ProGAN discriminators with temporal-consistency features from diffusion models, our GRAVEX framework achieves 74.33% F1-score across nine state-of-the-art generators—outperforming conventional ViT-based detectors by over 30% F1, on average. Implemented using NVIDIA MONAI for streamlined training and deployment, this approach addresses the critical challenge of detecting AI-generated faces as generative AI becomes increasingly sophisticated. On challenging datasets like CIFAKE, where baseline detectors fail completely, GRAVEX maintains 88% F1-score, demonstrating exceptional robustness and validating discriminator reuse as a promising path toward production-ready detection systems.

title: How to Build Planetary-Scale AI Infrastructure
abstract:
Gigawatt-scale AI facilities are pushing data center infrastructure beyond the limits of traditional design and operations workflows. Siloed approaches to buildings, power, cooling, and compute make it difficult to scale efficiently while meeting energy and sustainability requirements.
This panel explores how simulation-based co-design and physically accurate digital twins are being used to plan and operate large-scale AI infrastructure. Industry leaders will discuss how these methods help evaluate tradeoffs earlier, improve efficiency, and better align facility, power, thermal, and compute decisions.

title: Integrating AI and Quantum Computing to Accelerate the Future of Supercomputing
abstract: The path to fault-tolerant quantum computing is an AI and supercomputing challenge. This talk outlines a vision to accelerate scientific discovery for everyone, everywhere, through the complete convergence of AI with quantum hardware and systems. We will present the blueprint for a collaborative, open quantum ecosystem that transcends boundaries. By leveraging open standards, CUDA-Q, and NVQLink, we are creating a unified, hybrid infrastructure where academia, labs, startups, and industry can collaborate to drive the future of supercomputing

title: Market Primitives for an Agentic Economy
abstract: AI agents are emerging as significant economic actors, yet today's markets are designed for human participants, burdened by cognitive load, irrational behavior and biases, and a high-friction legal paradigm. Illia introduces a formal model for agentic markets where autonomous AI agents directly express and match intents. He proposes a new set of agentic market primitives including programmatic escrow, agentic dispute resolution, and intent‑based matching and settlement. This paradigm shift necessitates revising classical economic problems for agentic systems.

title: MLOps 101: Platforms and Processes for Building AI
abstract:
As AI adoption accelerates across every industry, teams are discovering that model quality alone is not quite enough to deliver real value. Scalable and reliable AI systems depend on robust MLOps that streamline the entire life cycle, from data to deployment. At the same time, the field is undergoing a major transformation driven by agentic AI, advanced reasoning, multi-modality, and new patterns for large-scale, disaggregated inference. 

This session introduces the foundational patterns of MLOps while also exploring how these new capabilities reshape AI design and operational strategy. We'll examine several use cases to break down the process and ideal platform for MLOps in this new era. Whether you're launching your first AI initiative or modernizing your infrastructure, this session provides the technical blueprint and best practices you need to operationalize AI at scale.

title: Optimizing Speech-To-Text Pipelines for Real-World Voice Agents
abstract: Every voice AI agent starts with the same problem: turning raw audio into something useful. This session shares what we've learned at Decagon building a production speech-to-text pipeline for customer service voice agents. We'll walk through the core components, including voice activity detection, turn detection, and transcription, and the failure modes we hit when deploying on real phone calls. Along the way, we'll cover the statistical and learned approaches we developed to handle these challenges, and how we think about evaluating ASR in a way that actually reflects agent performance.

title: Self-Coding Agents: Architectures, Data Flywheels, and Autonomous Code Repair
abstract: AI agents are swiftly evolving toward true autonomy using self-coding, feedback-driven, and adaptive architectures. This session explores frameworks where agents self-improve via data flywheels—constantly refining their models through feedback and learning from every interaction. We'll spotlight data designer agents that not only process, but actively generate and curate training data, enabling ongoing evolution without routine human coding. Critical topics include self-repairing code, reinforcement-driven data loops, and orchestration of agent collectives for collaboration and scale. These advances are powering scalable, reliable, and collaborative autonomous systems for enterprise and research, ushering in agents that can continually adapt and co-create with humans in real time.

title: The Blueprint for a Modern Resource-Responsible AI Factory
abstract:
Hudson River Trading (HRT) is at the forefront of automated trading, processing vast amounts of data at lightning speeds. But their most impressive metric might be their efficiency. In this session, HRT engineers reveal how they are leveraging NVIDIA infrastructure to build the ultimate "AI factory."

Discover how to break the trade-off between speed and sustainability. We will cover the end-to-end journey of deploying AI-driven trading models that are faster, smarter, and greener. Learn how innovative data center design, renewable power strategies, and NVIDIA-optimized workloads combine to create a trading infrastructure that is resilient, autonomous, and environmentally conscious.

title: The Road to Intelligent Mobility: How In-Vehicle GenAI Is Redefining the AI-Defined Vehicle
abstract: The automotive industry is rapidly pivoting toward a future where intelligence inside the vehicle defines brand differentiation, customer experience, and long-term value. Breakthroughs in GenAI, cloud–edge orchestration, and accelerated computing are enabling automakers to deliver safer, smarter, and more intuitive experiences—turning every vehicle into a continuously improving digital product. In this panel we will share how leaders across different companies in the ecosystem - AI infrastructure, cloud, Tier-1 platforms, and next-generation EV OEMs - are shaping the in-vehicle GenAI landscape. The session offers a view of where the industry is headed and what it will take to bring truly intelligent, AI-powered vehicles to market at global scale.

speakers.name: Sri Subramanian
speakers.jobTitle: Global Head of Generative AI, Automotive

title: Transform Content Delivery With Generative AI at the Edge
abstract:
As AI reshapes digital experiences, content delivery needs a major transformation. Traditional networks built for static caching can’t meet real-time, personalized expectations. Modern applications require generative AI inference at the edge, close to users and data, to meet strict performance, privacy, and efficiency needs. Learn how edge AI redefines how content is created, delivered, and monetized. 

By combining generative, agentic, and future physical AI with NVIDIA accelerated computing and software, you can synthesize content in real time and tailor experiences to users and markets. Learn how advanced edge compute and intelligent data systems reduce data movement, maintain sovereignty, and deliver adaptive, low-latency experiences across augmented/virtual reality, internet of things, and autonomous systems. Join us to see how Gen AI at the edge unlocks new value and innovation.

title: Transforming the Automotive Industry With Industrial AI and Digital Twins
abstract:
The automotive industry is undergoing a major shift as AI, simulation, and digital twins redefine how vehicles are designed, engineered, manufactured, and validated. As automakers move to AI-enabled, data-driven development pipelines, they’re unlocking new levels of efficiency, quality, and innovation across the vehicle life cycle.

In this panel, leaders from NVIDIA, Hyundai, Lucid, and Schaeffler will explore how industrial AI, virtual development environments, large-scale digital twins, and advanced simulation are accelerating automotive innovation. They’ll share real examples of AI-powered design, automated engineering, factory optimization, and robotics training converging into a unified end-to-end pipeline, along with insights on the technologies and collaboration driving industry transformation.

speakers.name: Roberto Henkel
speakers.jobTitle: Senior Vice President Digitalization & Operations IT

title: Visual AI Agents for Real-Time Video Understanding
abstract:
The next generation of visual AI agents starts with NVIDIA Blueprint for video search and summarization (VSS), which enables the creation of interactive analytical agents that reason through massive amounts of video, assess physical processes, and provide decision-making insights to streamline operations. The blueprint combines powerful vision language models (VLMs), advanced search capabilities with large language models, and Cosmos Reason VLM into a full suite of software for building,

We've made it easier to customize and deploy at the edge, on-premises, or in the cloud. Be the first to hear about the new features of VSS to accelerate your AI agent development.

speakers.name: Adam Ryason
speakers.jobTitle: Product Manager, NVIDIA AI Blueprint for Video Search and Summarization (VSS)

title: Accelerate Power Grid Interconnection for Load and Generation Using Agentic AI and Digital Twins
abstract: For AI and data center growth, time-to-power is most critical bottleneck. In the United States, average time for interconnection is between three to five years. This panel with Southwest Power Pool, GE Vernova, Southern California Edison, and ERCOT will discuss how they are using agentic AI and digital twins to better understand existing capacity, peak loads, and accelerating the interconnection process.

title: Ozaki Scheme: Addressing the Accuracy Challenge in the Era of Low-Precision Accelerators
abstract:
This talk explores the Ozaki Scheme, including its two variants, as a practical approach for recovering high accuracy from low-precision computations in numerical linear algebra.
Participation is important as it sheds light on how we can effectively respond to the accuracy challenges posed by next-generation accelerator architectures.

title: UK AI Minister: AI Growth Zones and the UK’s AI Opportunities Action Plan
abstract: Join this fireside chat with Kanishka Narayan, MP and U.K. AI Minister, and Bea Longworth, Director of Government Relations, EMEA to examine how the U.K. Government is reshaping the country's global competitiveness in AI through massive infrastructure investment and innovative policy frameworks. You will leave with a clear understanding of the U.K. government's roadmap for AI leadership and how countries can unlock new economic value for enterprises, governments, scientists, and citizens around the world.

speakers.name: Kanishka Narayan
speakers.company: UK Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

title: Accelerate Video Workloads Using NVENC/NVDEC Across Gaming, Pro-Viz, Streaming, and the Data Center
abstract:
Stop by our session to explore video workflows accelerated by NVIDIA's dedicated video encoders (NVENC) and decoders (NVDEC). GPUs with dedicated video codecs power AI video pipelines, training, and inference alongside gaming, professional video production, streaming, and data center applications. 

Product managers, engineers, solution architects, and DevTech experts will be on hand to dive into our video feature sets, troubleshoot pipelines, or answer any video-related questions you have.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod C (L2)

title: Advance Physical AI Safety With NVIDIA Halos
abstract:
Discover how NVIDIA’s Halos safety tools, methods, and principles, from chip to cloud, accelerate safe physical AI development. Understand how safety innovation and standards compliance converge to enable the next generation of autonomous vehicles and robotics.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Suhas Hariharapura Sheshadri
speakers.jobTitle: Director of Product Management - Robotics & Edge AI

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod F (L2)
Connect With the Experts Pod F (L2)

title: Building Liquid-Cooled AI Cloud Gigafactories (Presented by Lenovo)
abstract: AI cloud providers are under pressure to deploy next‑generation AI infrastructure faster, more efficiently, and at multi‑gigawatt scale. In this session, Lenovo introduces the AI Cloud Gigafactory program, co-developed with NVIDIA to industrialize hyperscale AI cloud buildouts. See how this framework, with Lenovo's global manufacturing, integrated solutions and services, enables rapid, energy-efficient deployment of NVIDIA Blackwell Ultra (GB300) systems and provides a seamless path to the next-generation Vera Rubin architecture. Leveraging real-world AI cloud deployments with NScale, this session demonstrates how Lenovo’s Neptune liquid cooling has evolved from niche HPC roots into a scalable foundation for multi-gigawatt AI clouds, utilizing global manufacturing and end-to-end services to accelerate time-to-first-token and de-risk the industry’s shift to liquid-cooled infrastructure.

title: Next-Generation Containment: Keeping the Data Center Cool (Presented by DDC Solutions)
abstract: As racks become denser, cooling the data center presents unique challenges. In this session, learn the benefits of containing and optimizing the environment for each individual rack. See how others are using this data center cooling architecture to optimize their power usage effectiveness and deploy the latest NVIDIA GPUs.

title: 2025 Gordon Bell Winner: Forecasting Tsunamis in Real Time With Digital Twins
abstract: The Cascadia subduction zone is capable of unleashing a magnitude 9 earthquake with tsunamis as high as 30 meters, endangering millions in the Pacific Northwest. While it has been quiet since 1700, scientists place a 37% probability of an 8.2+ earthquake in the next 50 years. In response, the 2025 Gordon Bell Prize was awarded to UT-Austin, UC San Diego, and Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory for the world’s first digital twin for real-time, full-physics-based tsunami early warning. Leveraging exascale-class HPC systems, it delivers unprecedented accuracy and speed, achieving a ten-billion-fold performance improvement over existing state-of-the-art methods. This enables rapid, data-driven hazard assessment and actionable insights during critical windows. The work not only redefines the boundaries of real-time tsunami forecasting, but also sets a new standard for disaster resilience.

speakers.name: Omar Ghattas
speakers.company: The University of Texas at Austin
speakers.jobTitle: Professor and Cockrell Endowed Chair in Engineering

speakers.name: Stefan Henneking
speakers.company: The University of Texas at Austin

title: 800 VDC and Modular Data Center Solutions: Powering Next-Generation AI Infrastructure (Presented by Delta Electronics)
abstract: This session explores the convergence of 800 Volts Direct Current (VDC) power distribution and modular data center solutions as essential enablers for high-density AI and HPC workloads. Learn how 800 VDC architectures reduce power conversion losses, improve efficiency, and support scalable operations, while modular designs accelerate deployment and provide flexible power and cooling strategies.

speakers.name: Ralf Pieper
speakers.jobTitle: R&D Director, Delta Custom Design BU

speakers.name: Jason Agee
speakers.jobTitle: Director of Data Center Solutions & Architecture

title: A Collaborative Multi-Agent AI System for Scalable High School Transcript Processing
abstract: College admissions offices face a major challenge: processing millions of high school transcripts with varying formats, grading systems, and layouts. In this paper, we present a multi-agent AI system that automates this process through intelligent coordination among specialized agents. The architecture includes a Pattern Recognition Agent, Semantic Analysis Agent, Visual Intelligence Agent, and an Orchestration Agent that manages communication and result integration. Using GPA extraction as a coordination signal, the system ensures reliable collaboration and prevents information loss. Tested on 40 real-world transcripts from 13 U.S. states, it achieved 96.7% accuracy versus expert review, processing each document in 45 seconds. This demonstrates a scalable, collaborative AI solution that preserves human-level accuracy while greatly reducing processing time.

title: A Deep Dive Into Omniverse Libraries for OpenUSD and Physical AI Development
abstract:
Join this interactive Q&A session to get direct, actionable advice from NVIDIA's core engineering teams on overcoming the critical developer friction points in building large-scale, physically accurate digital twins for robotics, autonomous vehicles, and manufacturing. This is your unique opportunity to discuss your specific use cases and technical challenges directly with the experts who develop and maintain the core Omniverse libraries and technologies for world simulation, including OpenUSD, PhysX, RTX, and more.

Please Note: Mitesh Patel, Ernesto Pacheco, and Jay Axe will be joining us for the Tuesday 10AM session only. Neelakantan Mani and Natasha Anisimova will be joining us for the Tuesday 2PM session only. We encourage you to attend this session if you'd like to hear from a particular expert directly.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod D (L2)
Connect With the Experts Pod F (L2)

title: A Deep Dive Into Two Telco-Built Sovereign AI Clouds
abstract: More than 20 telcos worldwide have already built sovereign AI infrastructure powered by NVIDIA, with many more underway. These telco-operated national AI clouds are becoming strategic assets, combining local control and regulatory compliance with high-performance AI capabilities to serve priorities ranging from secure government workloads and public services to industrial clouds for sectors like manufacturing. This session brings together two leading operators who will dive deep into their vision and journey for sovereign AI in their markets: how they designed and built their infrastructure, what they are optimized for, how adoption is ramping, and where their roadmaps are headed. Gain a front-row view into how telcos are turning sovereign AI into real platforms for innovation, growth, and national resilience.

title: A Diffusion Model Framework for Solving Scientific Inverse Problems
abstract: A key challenge in many scientific tasks is inferring the underlying scientific phenomenon from sparse and corrupted measurements, also known as inverse problems. A canonical example is imaging a black hole using the Event Horizon Telescope, which collects sparse radio waves that have been corrupted by the Earth's atmosphere. We have developed a framework based on diffusion models that can solve a broad range of scientific inverse problems, with applications that span medical imaging, astronomy, seismology, and climate science.

title: A Modular Blueprint for Next-Generation AI Servers: Inside the MGX Architecture (Presented by MSI)
abstract: Explore how the MGX architecture is reshaping AI server design with its modular, building-block approach engineered to maximize performance for GPU-driven AI workloads, while also simplifying hardware reuse across multiple processor platforms to provide a scalable, future-ready foundation for evolving enterprise AI infrastructure. This session also showcases how optimized resource allocation, integrated internal cabling, and MGX-powered MSI AI platforms accelerate AI deployment, enhance efficiency, and support both on-premises and cloud environments.

speakers.name: Philip Maher
speakers.jobTitle: Director of Marketing, MSI Enterprise Platform Solutions

title: A Multi-Agent Debate Framework for Automated NVIDIA Cosmos Optimization in Surgical Robot Training
abstract: We present HMARCF-HILFOF, the first multi-agent agentic AI system integrated with NVIDIA Cosmos Physical AI for automated surgical robot training. By combining multi-expert debate reasoning with physics-based world simulation, we automate scenario design while ensuring clinical quality. HMARCF coordinates four agents — Thoracic Surgery, Robot Learning, Cosmos Engineering, and Imaging/Radiology — through structured debates to produce auditable, versioned Cosmos configurations (angles/tissue/dynamics/physics). HILFOF provides human-in-the-loop quality assurance with quantitative metrics (VACS >0.85, SBF >0.90) and feedback-driven regeneration. In preliminary phantom-based validation of thoracic needle insertion, the system expanded scenario diversity from 10 (manual baseline) to 30+ (5 angles × 3 tissues × 2 dynamics) while reducing prompt engineering time from hours to minutes.

title: A Multi-Step Approach to Customize Small Language Models for Enterprise AI Applications
abstract: Many enterprise generative AI use cases could benefit from more effectively utilizing user preference data. In this poster, we demonstrate an affective architecture for fine-tuning small language models using this preference data, built on NVIDIA NeMo, and share results from the model performance gained.

title: A Multi-Task Deep Learning Framework for Reliable Estimated Time of Arrival Prediction
abstract: Explore how we leverage deep learning to build a scalable, multi-task estimated time of arrival (ETA) prediction system that improves delivery time accuracy and reliability across millions of DoorDash orders.

title: A New Frontier for Vision AI with Expert Reasoning Agents
abstract:
This session is designed for companies already deploying vision AI applications with NVIDIA Metropolis (DeepStream, TAO). It will outline the next steps – how to integrate Vision Language Models (VLMs) into existing pipelines to unlock new reasoning and contextual understanding capabilities. Milestone Systems, a leader in video management software, will share how they have fine-tuned NVIDIA Cosmos Reason to create a specialized and highly accurate VLM using 150,000 hours of curated video.

Attendees will learn how they can use Milestone's VLM directly within their own applications and walk through their experience integrating VLMs into their video management system, XProtect. Through these use cases, we’ll examine how the next generation of VLM technology can transform video analytics into reasoning-driven applications.

title: A New Paradigm: Verifiable AI
abstract:
Autonomous AI requires a new security model. From AI agents to robots, we need to rethink how we should trust these systems to deliver the full promise of their automation. Can we verify their operations, security, and alignment as we grant them access to our most coveted data and delegate them critical tasks?.

As multi-agent AI systems span boundaries from cloud to edge, the challenge of maintaining trust and security across every layer of the stack—digital and physical—has never been greater. This session introduces EQTY’s verifiable AI runtime, a new approach that continuously ensures verified integrity from model training and inference to deployment in real-world, physical AI environments, including the verification of AI sovereignty.

title: A Playbook – Operating Cloud AI Factories at Scale
abstract: Discover how to build and operate AI factories that scale with your ambitions. In this session, NVIDIA shares hard-earned lessons from working alongside Cloud Partners and ecosystem ISVs to power massive AI infrastructure around the world. You’ll gain a practical framework—built on open, composable infrastructure software—for managing the full lifecycle of large AI systems, from reliability and performance to multi-tenant orchestration. Walk away ready to accelerate your time-to-production and deliver sustainable scale for your own AI operations.

speakers.name: Warren Barkley
speakers.jobTitle: VP of Product Management, DGX Cloud

title: A Practical Playbook for Making AI Real: Bring Physical and Agentic AI to Life (Presented by SoftServe, Inc.)
abstract:
As enterprises move beyond AI experimentation and into production, they face the growing complexity of modern AI across capabilities, technologies, and use cases. This session presents a practical, outcome-driven playbook for making AI real by translating that complexity into a clear, actionable framework.

By leveraging physical AI and agentic AI, organizations can deliver step-change improvements in efficiency, safety, and profitability. We’ll explore how enterprises connect real-time simulation, robotics, intelligent collaborative agents, and computer vision into scalable architectures that transform the end-to-end value chain.
Through real-world business use cases, you'll gain a clear framework to simplify AI adoption, articulate value to stakeholders, and accelerate the deployment of physical and agentic AI in production environments.

title: A Safe, Efficient, and Scalable Approach to 800 VDC Architecture (Presented by Eaton)
abstract:
The growing demand for high‑performance computing and dense GPU deployments is accelerating adoption of DC power distribution in next‑generation data centers. By eliminating rack‑level AC conversion, DC architectures improve efficiency, reduce space overhead, and enable higher‑voltage distribution (800 VDC), increasing overall system scalability and lowering losses. 

Within this landscape, Eaton plays an active leadership role in the Open Compute Project (OCP) community, and in the development of Underwriters Laboratories (UL) and International Electrotechnical Commission (IEC) standards, helping shape industry frameworks that enable the safe deployment of high‑voltage DC architectures at scale. This standards‑driven approach ensures that emerging DC ecosystems are interoperable, fault‑tolerant, and aligned with real‑world operational requirements for AI and HPC environments. 

Building on its deep power management expertise, Eaton focuses on enabling higher power densities safely. By combining active standards leadership, and a laser focus on safety, Eaton is helping establish the foundational power architectures required for the next generation of AI and high‑performance computing data centers.

title: A Vision Language Foundation Model for MRI Standarization
abstract: Magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) is widely used in both clinical and research settings. However, there is no standard system for naming or categorizing MRI sequences. This inconsistency is due to differences in imaging protocols, scanner vendors, and institutional practices. As a result, MRI sequences often vary in appearance, and DICOM metadata can be unreliable or conflicting. Clinician oversight is frequently required to verify the correct sequence type. This manual process is time-consuming and prone to error, especially when dealing with large datasets. The goal of this study is to create an efficient streamlined method to standardize the MRI sequences. In this project, we introduce the first foundation model that standardize MRI naming convention across nearly all sequence and anatomy in routine scans.

title: Accelerate AI Inference Using DOCA for Storage
abstract: Real-time AI inference at scale requires high-performance GPUs combined with efficient data movement, preprocessing, and data access from edge to cloud. Developers often struggle to eliminate bottlenecks that slow down inference pipelines and waste valuable compute cycles. The NVIDIA DOCA software platform enables high-performance AI inference processing by accelerating data movement and preprocessing tasks using RDMA, NIXL, and GPUNetIO. DOCA-powered storage solutions like the NVIDIA AI Data Platform design and S3-compatible storage enable seamless, high-performance data access for AI workloads. This session demonstrates how to design inference services that deliver responsive and reliable results for latency-sensitive applications in both AI factory and edge environments. It also shows how to apply DOCA-accelerated networking and storage for high-performance data access.

title: Accelerate AI Through Open-Source Inference
abstract: The open AI ecosystem is thriving—powered by a new wave of high-performance inference frameworks and community-driven model development. In this session, we hear from ecosystem leaders as they discuss the critical role of open models in progressing AI innovation. We’ll dive deep into the collaborative infrastructure enabling state-of-the-art generative systems—from tokenizer to transformer to GPU kernel. Whether you’re building your own language model, optimizing inference pipelines, or contributing to open AI research, this is your chance to learn about optimization breakthroughs, interoperability standards, and the future of deploying open models at scale.

title: Accelerate Apache Spark With GPU and AI: A Hands-On Workshop
abstract:
In this training lab, we'll introduce Project Aether — a set of automation and AI tools that speed up and reduce the cost of accelerated extract, transform, load and analytics workloads on the RAPIDS Accelerator for Apache Spark, built on NVIDIA cuDF. Aether combines automation along with advanced AI tools to automatically test and optimize your Apache Spark workloads with GPU.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Some experience with Linux and the command line needed.
  • An understanding of large-scale analytics and Apache Spark is not required, but would be helpful.

title: Accelerate Cloud Platforms for the Next Era of AI
abstract: AI advancement depends on cloud infrastructure to deliver massive compute scale, high-performance, high resilience, and robust security. Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) is addressing these challenges through an innovative architectural approach featuring the Oracle Acceleron fabric integrated with NVIDIA BlueField technology. This session explores how OCI’s accelerated networking, intelligent isolation mechanisms, and high-speed data movement capabilities collectively form a robust and secure platform for giga-scale. Speakers will share details of the Oracle Acceleron architecture and show how it enables the world’s most demanding AI workloads. Gain a deeper understanding of the infrastructure patterns shaping modern AI platforms and how organizations can leverage them as they accelerate their AI journeys, securely and efficiently.

speakers.name: Pradeep Vincent
speakers.jobTitle: Executive Vice President & Chief Technical Architect, OCI

title: Accelerate Data Compression and Decompression on GPUs with nvCOMP
abstract:
Join NVIDIA experts to discuss GPU-accelerated compression and decompression with nvCOMP and dedicated hardware decompress engines for data analytics, genomics, deep learning, and more. We’ll walk through best practices for offloading decompression to Blackwell’s dedicated engine, show how the latest nvCOMP APIs (including Python APIs) simplify integration into existing CPU and GPU pipelines, and talk through real-world performance tuning patterns.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod F (L2)
Connect With the Experts Pod F (L2)

title: Accelerate Drug Discovery With NVIDIA Libraries and Platforms
abstract:
The field of drug discovery is increasingly benefiting from AI, helping researchers build efficient predictive and generative workflows that drastically reduce time-to-clinic. NVIDIA accelerates this process with open-source solutions like BioNeMo Recipes, NIM, and Clara Open Models for Digital Biology, providing easy-to-adopt solutions for training and deploying foundation models and agentic AI systems for drug discovery. In this session, we'll demonstrate how the latest NVIDIA accelerated libraries and models streamline AI workflows, accelerate drug discovery, and enable more efficient biomolecular design and analysis.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Basic understanding of Python, Machine Learning, and Docker

title: Accelerate Enterprise AI Adoption: Secure Your Agentic Foundations from Day 1 (Presented by CrowdStrike)
abstract:
Enterprise AI adoption is accelerating through AI agents embedded in applications and workflows. While these agents drive innovation, they present unique security challenges as organizations move to production. Traditional security approaches weren't built for agent architectures, creating vulnerabilities in agent logic, prompts, model interactions, and infrastructure.

Learn how CrowdStrike is collaborating with NVIDIA to drive secure enterprise AI adoption across the entire AI life cycle, from governing agent behavior to protecting models, and delivering built-in protection for AI factories. By integrating security into agent design and runtime, organizations can confidently scale AI systems while managing risk.

title: Accelerate Geospatial Workflows for Planetary Insight
abstract: Learn how the integration of NVIDIA's accelerated computing and Planet Labs' cloud-scale imagery platform is driving a revolutionary shift in geospatial workflows at scale and in real time. This integration of Earth observation innovation pioneered by Planet, NVIDIA’s platform, and AI unlocks the full potential of petabytes of Earth observation data, providing detailed, actionable insights that address some of the world’s most pressing environmental and humanitarian challenges.

title: Accelerate GPU Scientific Computing With nvmath-python
abstract: In 2024, NVIDIA unveiled nvmath-python, a library designed to bridge the gap between Python scientific community and NVIDIA CUDA-X math libraries, and it’s now generally available. You’ll learn what makes nvmath-python a useful addition for GPU-accelerated computing with Python, along with how to use the library’s unique capabilities to reach extreme levels of performance to solve cutting-edge scientific problems previously unimaginable in Python.

title: Accelerate Grid Modernization for AI Growth Using NVIDIA Omniverse
abstract: Building physically accurate digital twins for electrical grid infrastructure is becoming critical to be able simulate and predict outages during extreme weather events. We'll discuss Southern California Edison's journey of adopting Omniverse as a platform to bring all its ecosystem partners to build digital twins of the grid. Presenters will showcase how SoftServe built the solution, demonstrating Southern California Edison's vision, and in parallel helped Siemens Energy build scalable product integrating physics and AI-based models from substation.

title: Accelerate Hybrid Quantum-Classical Research at Scale With NVIDIA CUDA-Q (Presented by Foxconn)
abstract: This talk explains why Hon Hai Research Institute’s integrated strategy for advancing quantum science across the full stack requires an integrated strategy, developing both better hardware and applications algorithms together. We'll outline Hon Hai Research Institute’s approach to this—including Foxconn’s ion-trap roadmap—from first-generation blade-type traps to the establishment of Taiwan’s first industry-based ion-trap quantum computing laboratory and the creation of multi-layer and chip-based architectures. We'll also highlight advancements from Hon Hai Research Institute in software and theory for areas including quantum error correction, efficient tomography and noise benchmarking, quantum machine learning, and quantum networking.

title: Accelerate Industrial Intelligence: Practice Across Design and Production (Presented by GIGABYTE)
abstract:
Deploying autonomous robots and smart manufacturing systems requires a computing architecture that extends far beyond the edge. This session explores how to build a converged "AI factory" infrastructure that prioritizes the low-latency demands of advanced robotics and intelligent production, while simultaneously supporting the massive compute requirements of upstream physical AI workflows.​

Learn architectural strategies to manage these mixed workloads on a single platform, ensuring real-time responsiveness for machines without sacrificing simulation performance. Finally, we will demonstrate how the GAIFA (GIGABYTE AI Factory Accelerator) ecosystem integrates specialized partners to power this end-to-end industrial autonomy.

speakers.name: Weiru Li
speakers.jobTitle: Deputy Director at the Software and Solutions Center

speakers.name: Huei-Ru Tseng
speakers.company: Information and Communication Lab., Industrial Technology Research Institute

title: Accelerate Minimal Residual Disease Detection: Real-World Performance and Clinical Impact
abstract: We implemented an minimal residual disease (MRD) detection pipeline optimized with NVIDIA Parabricks to accelerate computation and enable faster, scalable molecular analysis without compromising accuracy.

title: Accelerate Native Sparse Attention Kernels on Blackwell by CUTLASS Python
abstract: We dive deep into new techniques for accelerating native sparse attention (NSA) kernels on Blackwell using CUTLASS Python, showing how precise control over data movement and sparsity scheduling unlocks dramatic performance gains. Together, we'll explore practical strategies you can apply to push your model training efficiency to the next level.

title: Accelerate Open Science: Incorporating CUDA Into the SciPy Ecosystem
abstract:
As GPUs become central to scientific Python workloads, SciPy-ecosystem projects—including NumPy, SciPy, and scikit-learn—face the challenge of adopting CUDA without sacrificing usability, portability, or community values. This panel brings together open-source maintainers and ecosystem partners to discuss how tools such as CuPy, Numba, and CUDA Python are being used to accelerate core numerical routines while preserving Pythonic APIs.

Panelists will share lessons from real-world integrations, including managing build and packaging complexity, handling optional GPU dependencies, and supporting diverse hardware environments. The discussion highlights how collaboration across open-source communities, hardware vendors, and scientific users enables sustainable GPU acceleration aligned with the long-term goals of the SciPy ecosystem.

title: Accelerate Operational Excellence Through NVIDIA Resiliency Solutions
abstract: Explore the NVIDIA resiliency tools that keep AI systems reliable at scale. Learn how to match these solutions to your operational challenges to build a practical adoption roadmap.

title: Accelerate Oracle AI Database Embedding and Vector Index Creation With NVIDIA (Presented by Oracle)
abstract: As enterprise vector data grows, keeping vector indexes up to date becomes increasingly important for similarity search and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG). In partnership with NVIDIA, Oracle AI Database uses GPUs to accelerate vector workflows by speeding up large-scale embedding generation and vector index creation. With Oracle AI Database, users can run vector similarity search directly in SQL, as well as via APIs and SDKs. Join us for a walkthrough of using Oracle AI Database capabilities with NVIDIA GPUs, learn how they can reduce time-to-value for vector workloads, and see how Deutsche Bank is employing them.

speakers.name: Tirthankar Lahiri
speakers.jobTitle: Senior Vice President of Mission-Critical Data and AI Engines

title: Accelerate Physical AI with Simulation-Centric Data Engine (Presented by Lightwheel)
abstract: Physical AI is moving from research demos into real-world deployment.
