However, scaling Physical AI systems remains constrained by low-fidelity simulation without physics, limited data generation capacity, and the lack of large-scale, industrial-grade evaluation infrastructure. Lightwheel was built to close this gap, and trusted by 80% of leading Physical AI teams. Lightwheel is a simulation-driven Physical AI infrastructure company, providing the simulation, data, and evaluation platform required to train, scale, and deploy Physical AI systems in the real world.

title: Accelerate Physical AI: An Integrated Solution for Rapid Development (Presented by QCT)
abstract:
Explore how to accelerate physical AI deployments using the QCT Application-Ready Solution concept. By deep-diving into the QCT Dev Kit architecture, a toolkit that integrates high-performance QCT GPU servers with NVIDIA Cosmos, NVIDIA Isaac GR00T, and NVIDIA Omniverse, customers can deliver end-to-end physical AI solutions faster than ever.  

 
Learn how QCT works with TM Robot to overcome humanoid era challenges and achieve industrial workload validation. Through the TM Robot "Xplore I" collaboration, the companies manage to improve humanoid performance via specialized GUI designs and enable five key features (system, skills, datasets, models, and USD file management).

title: Accelerate Predictive Water Safety via Synthetic Data and Continuous Learning
abstract: We demonstrate how synthetic data generation and continuous learning solve the critical deployment barriers in water safety AI—reducing detection latency by 37% and cutting multi-site onboarding from two months to five days. Explore how generative AI mitigates model drift across diverse aquatic environments where labeled real-world data is scarce and costly.

title: Accelerate Robot Learning With NVIDIA Isaac Lab and Newton
abstract:
This hands-on lab introduces Newton, a GPU-accelerated physics engine. Then we'll integrate Newton with Isaac Lab, showing how they can work together in robot-learning workflows. You'll configure tasks to run on Newton, and train and evaluate policies in Isaac Lab with Newton providing high-fidelity, high-throughput physics. You'll leave knowing how to use Newton for more realistic robotics simulation.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Intermediate experience with Python, including working with packages, virtual environments, and basic scripting.
  • Familiarity with 3D content concepts such as meshes, materials, transforms, and scene hierarchies from tools like Blender, Maya, or similar DCC applications.
  • Understand command-line usage for running scripts and managing project files.
  • Have familiarity with robotics or simulation workflows (for example, using simulation tools or working with physics-enabled assets).

title: Accelerate Robotic Grinding With Physics-Based Simulation and Synthetic Data
abstract: To solve the slow, manual tuning and costly physical trials hindering robotic grinding, we present HolonCloud: a physics-based digital twin powered by NVIDIA Isaac Sim. This framework leverages synthetic data and material simulation to accurately predict results, eliminating physical validation and enabling an autonomous, reinforcement learning-driven sim-to-real workflow.

title: Accelerate Speed to Insight: Harness BERTopic and NVIDIA Technology
abstract: Explore how we accelerated insight extraction on Adoreboard by combining BERTopic with NVIDIA GPU technology. This approach reduces analysis time by 83% and theme overload by two-thirds, delivering faster, clearer, and more actionable insights. Using CUDA, RAPIDS, and containerized pipelines, we enable real-time topic modeling across massive datasets—empowering teams to move from hours of manual analysis to minutes of automated understanding.

title: Accelerate the AI Factory With NVIDIA-Certified Systems
abstract:
NVIDIA-Certified Systems provide the essential foundation for the modern AI factory, offering performance-optimized hardware validated to run demanding workloads from the desktop to the data center and edge. This session explores how the program’s rigorous testing—now including NVIDIA-Certified Storage for massive data demands—ensures enterprise-grade reliability, security, and scalability for generative AI and industrial digitalization. Join us to learn how these certified servers, workstations, and edge systems provide a trusted, best-practice pathway for enterprises to confidently deploy accelerated computing at scale.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Steve Stein
speakers.jobTitle: Sr. Product Marketing Manager, Data Center Platforms

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod A (L2)
Connect With the Experts Pod F (L2)

title: Accelerate the Path to In-Silico Life Sciences and Drug Discovery
abstract: Representing life in silico and designing compounds and therapies that modulate that biology are among the most foundational goals of modern life sciences R&D. The community is at an inflection point: increasing volumes of sophisticated data and foundation modeling efforts require fundamental new technology to accelerate discovery. In this talk, learn about NVIDIA's efforts in addressing these challenges head-on, with new efforts in data generation, data processing, and biological and molecular foundation modeling, and research. We'll cover genomics, multi-omics, foundation model scaling, molecular discovery, and end-to-end AI-scientist workflows in this comprehensive product talk.

title: Accelerate Topological Property Simulation of SSH–Hubbard Model via DMET-VQE With CUDA-Q
abstract: We demonstrate how the Density Matrix Embedding Theory with Variational Quantum Eigensolver (DMET–VQE) can be applied to uncover topological properties in the one-dimensional Su–Schrieffer–Heeger–Hubbard (SSHH) model, and how its computational performance can be enhanced through asynchronous simulation across multiple GPUs using NVIDIA CUDA-Q. In our simulations, the open-boundary edge-state signatures — that is, the signals of the topological states splitting away from half filling to one-and-three-quarters filling as onsite interaction increases — depend on the fragment size, which determines the number of qubits, revealing the significance of computational acceleration in high-qubit simulations.

title: Accelerate Video Frame Extraction and Labeling
abstract:
Learn how to reduce the cost associated with frame extraction for computer vision post-processing using free and publicly-accessible American Sign Language data. Use a novel, open-source framework and contribute to the world's largest dataset.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Have GitHub Account
  • Understand Python and Poetry
  • Have AWS Command Line Interface Configured (for S3 Operations)

title: Accelerate Your AI Infrastructure at Scale
abstract:
This session will be an extension to the session led by NVIDIA senior solutions architect Kristof De Brouwer, titled "Mastering AI Infrastructure At Scale — a DevOps Playbook." You can ask questions about your scaled AI deployments of our technical experts, explore deployment strategies in depth, and engage in discussions on optimizing NVIDIA AI infrastructure for your specific environments.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod E (L2)
Connect With the Experts Pod E (L2)

title: Accelerated AI Networking with DOCA
abstract: As AI models scale, data center networking must provide ultra-low latency, high throughput, and programmable data paths so training and inference workloads remain efficient. With the NVIDIA DOCA software platform, developers can move critical data-path functions onto NVIDIA BlueField data processing units (DPUs) using DOCA libraries and microservices to offload, accelerate, and isolate cloud infrastructure while enhancing observability. We’ll cover how DOCA enables centralized orchestration of BlueField‑accelerated services, dynamic traffic steering, and fine‑grained network and security controls that work together to deliver secure, high‑performance fabrics with predictable performance and efficient resource utilization for multi‑tenant AI environments at scale.

title: Accelerated Building Blocks for Next-Generation AI+HPC Workloads
abstract: Embracing next-generation technology like AI-infused scientific workflows involves blending familiar building blocks with innovation, timeliness, and purpose. Whether it's models pre-trained on petabyte-scale data for common forecasting tasks, or real-time digital twins, developers need building blocks to enable their vision. In this talk, we walk through developer software used to enable AI and HPC innovations, including recent enhancements that maximize the use of NVIDIA hardware and improve the developer experience.

title: Accelerated Computing in Financial Services for Banking
abstract:
Join this interactive session with NVIDIA experts to learn best practices and latest techniques for deploying AI applications and accelerated computing to banking use cases. We'll answer your questions, share implementation insights, and discuss architectural patterns for deploying AI reliably across on-premises, cloud, and hybrid environments. Gain practical guidance on navigating regulatory, data, and governance challenges, and learn how to design robust, efficient, and scalable solutions that unlock new products, services, and operational efficiencies for banks and financial institutions.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod D (L2)
Connect With the Experts Pod E (L2)

title: Accelerated HPC+AI Workflow Enables Live-Steering of Vera C. Rubin Observatory and X-ray Free Electron Laser
abstract:
The landscape of experimental science is being reshaped by the demand for real-time insight at the petabyte scale. With the Vera C. Rubin Observatory’s Legacy Survey of Space and Time (LSST) Camera and the Linac Coherent Light Source II (LCLS II) at SLAC National Accelerator Laboratory, data are arriving at unprecedented speed and volume.
 
NVIDIA-accelerated computing, coupled with GPU-accelerated Python libraries—CuPy, cuPyNumeric—are making possible what was previously out of reach. Now, by running these same scientific analysis pipelines on NVIDIA DGX Grace Hopper and Blackwell, Spark, and RTX PRO, researchers are gaining powerful new advantages for both performance and collaboration. Many months of data analysis compresses to a few hours through cleverly solved equations using distributed computation on GPUs.

title: Accelerated Networking for AI Infrastructure
abstract:
Gain a holistic understanding of AI factory networking, exploring how key communication methods between GPUs, racks, and storage shape overall AI performance and scalability.


Bring your own laptop and experience AI factory networking in action. In AI factories, the network is critical to maximizing performance. You’ll see how networking choices shape AI workload performance and scalability, then configure key pieces yourself in guided labs.

We start with a level set session that walks through essential communication paths in an AI factory deployments: east-west GPU-to-GPU communication across racks with InfiniBand, north south and storage connectivity over Spectrum-X Ethernet, and low latency, high bandwidth NVLINK communication between GPUs within the same rack.


By the end of the session, you’ll be familiar with the key networking technologies that underpin AI factories and have a clear view of how network design decisions translate directly into faster, more efficient AI workloads.

    Prerequisite(s):

    • Basic Linux administration skills, including directory navigation, file management, and text editing with nano or vim are required.
    • Intermediate TCP/IP networking experience is expected, with familiarity in L2/L3 protocols, IP networking concepts, and proficiency using tools such as iperf, wireshark, and the command line.

title: Accelerated Omics and Single-cell Analysis Using NVIDIA GPUs
abstract:
The explosion of biological data generation and innovations in AI have opened new frontiers in scientific discovery and biomedicine. With the ability to process vast amounts of biological data, accelerated analysis and foundation model approaches are transforming digital biology. This workshop provides hands-on experience for how accelerated libraries and deep learning models can be leveraged to process and analyze complex biological data. Gain an understanding of the end-to-end process, from how NVIDIA libraries support data generation, pre-processing, and analysis of single-cell data, to how foundation models are being used and evaluated in the life sciences. Get an overview of relevant solutions in digital biology, such as Parabricks, RAPIDS-singlecell (developed by scverse), NVIDIA CUDA-X Data Science Libraries, and Clara Open Models.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Basic familiarity with Python, Jupyter, and Bioinformatics workflows.

title: Accelerated Quantum Supercomputing: Build a Multi-GPU, Multi-QPU, Multi-User Environment for Hybrid Algorithms
abstract: Quantum computers can run some calculations faster than classical computers, but integration with classical computing is required. Our work is the first-ever demonstration of a multi-QPU and multi-GPU environment fully integrated into a data center.

title: Accelerating Enterprise AI with NVIDIA: How SAP Builds, Scales, and Runs Agentic Workflows (Presented by SAP)
abstract: In this session, SAP shares how it partners with NVIDIA to accelerate enterprise AI from model development to production-grade agentic workflows. This session shows how SAP combines NVIDIA AI with deep business context from SAP applications to move AI beyond prototypes into real-world impact. Learn how developers build and adapt domain-specific models, operationalize them using SAP GenAI hub and NVIDIA NIM, and apply them through SAP Joule for Developers to tackle complex challenges like code explanation and cloud migration. The session closes with a forward-looking view on agentic and physical AI, where live business context connects digital intelligence to real-world execution.

speakers.name: Ted Way
speakers.jobTitle: Vice President and Chief Product Officer

speakers.name: Mayank Shrivastava
speakers.jobTitle: Vice President of Generative AI & Foundation Models

title: Accelerating High-Fidelity Generative Models: Entangled Quantum Walks With CUDA-Q
abstract:
We introduce a Quantum Walk-based Adaptive Distribution Generator (QW-ADG), a hybrid quantum-classical framework designed for high-efficiency generative modeling. The key is using an entangled two-qubit coin within a variational quantum walk, generating complex 2D spatial correlations for tasks like image creation.

Implemented on the CUDA-Q platform and accelerated on an NVIDIA GPU, our model demonstrates a significant performance advantage over alternative quantum approaches. In a direct benchmark generating 8x8 MNIST digits, our QW-ADG is over 10x faster than a standard quantum generative adversarial network (QGAN) while achieving comparable or superior accuracy (L1 Error).

We establish the QW-ADG as a highly efficient and practical framework for near-term quantum generative modeling, highlighting the power of structured quantum dynamics combined with high-performance simulation.

title: Accelerating Innovation With AI Studio and Foundry: Cases Driving Customer Success (Presented by World Wide Technology)
abstract: Discover how organizations are accelerating their AI journeys with WWT’s AI Studio and Foundry—leveraging NVIDIA NIM, NVIDIA NeMo, and NVIDIA AI Blueprints to move quickly from ideas to validated prototypes and scalable, production-ready solutions. We will share real-world examples of how this approach is delivering meaningful business outcomes in industries such as global service providers and energy production.

title: Accelerating LLM Training and Inferencing With Reduced Precision Format
abstract:
Large-scale LLMs require enormous computational resources, making efficiency crucial for training. Learn how cutting-edge reduced-precision formats such as FP8, MXFP4 and NVFP4 can dramatically accelerate pre-training, post-training, and inference while maintaining accuracy close to full-precision baseline models. You’ll learn how NVIDIA's innovative NVFP4 format better captures dynamic range and reduces quantization error compared to MXFP4. We'll cover techniques including Random Hadamard Transforms, two-dimensional block quantization, stochastic rounding, and strategic placement of higher-precision layers to stabilize training. Gain practical skills with the software tools necessary for quantization-aware training and efficient inference deployment, helping your applications retain accuracy while benefiting from reduced precision.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Understanding of LLM training and inference
  • Basic understandings of reduced precision formats
  • Python, docker and shell scripting skills

title: Accelerating Quantum Chemistry on GPUs—Latest Advances
abstract: Gaussian-basis quantum chemistry on NVIDIA GPUs is primed to change the world. Join this session to learn about new technologies and offerings from NVIDIA that help accelerate this paradigm shift.

title: Accelerating Rack-Scale Connectivity for NVIDIA Blackwell and Rubin Platforms (Presented by Astera Labs)
abstract: Learn how advanced rack-scale connectivity solutions are revolutionizing data center infrastructure for next-generation AI workloads. We'll explore the technical innovations enabling seamless multi-GPU scaling and the performance breakthroughs achieved when integrating Astera Labs' purpose-built connectivity solutions with NVIDIA's Blackwell and upcoming Rubin architectures.

title: Accelerating Scientific Discovery Through Global Innovation
abstract: Join Undersecretary Dario Gil for a forward-looking update on the Genesis Mission—a bold initiative redefining how science, technology, and global collaboration drive scientific discovery. This session will showcase early successes of NVIDIA’s long-standing collaborations with the U.S. Department of Energy and ongoing work with leading research centers such as the Argonne, Los Alamos, and Oak Ridge national laboratories. Discover how these collaborations are advancing breakthroughs across energy, quantum technology, and materials science—and learn how developers, researchers, and business leaders alike can help shape the next frontier of scientific innovation.

title: Accelerating Small Molecule Drug Discovery for Hard-to-Target Proteins
abstract: Learn about the practicalities and challenges of developing a flow-matching sequence-to-ensemble model for intrinsically disordered proteins, and an evaluation framework for ensemble prediction. You’ll see how augmented training data, experimental biophysical constraints, and dedicated benchmarks enable robust conformational ensembles where conventional predictors fail, and how these ensembles plug into GPU-accelerated protein structure generation workflows for hard-to-target oncology, central nervous system, and immunology programs.

title: Accelerating the Path to Production: The Evolution of Enterprise Storage to Deliver AI-Ready Data
abstract: Roughly 70% of AI projects fail to transition to production due to challenges related to data compliance and security. To address this challenge, enterprise storage systems are evolving from data delivery to knowledge and intelligence delivery systems. Join us to learn how NVIDIA is collaborating with leading partners from the industry on architectures that deliver AI-ready data to accelerate the path from proof of concept to production.

title: Accessible Quantum Reinforcement Learning for Finance: Benchmarking on NVIDIA RTX GPUs
abstract: Explore how we make quantum reinforcement learning for finance truly accessible using everyday GPUs. This poster demonstrates how you can train hybrid quantum–classical financial agents on NVIDIA RTX 3090s in just 20 hours, benchmarked on 18 years of Taiwan stock data, revealing how GPU-accelerated quantum simulation unlocks faster, fairer, and more inclusive financial AI research.

speakers.name: Chisheng Chen
speakers.company: Harvard Medical School/Beth Israel Deaconess Medical Center
speakers.jobTitle: AI Research Assistant, Harvard Medical School, and AI Trainer, OpenAI

title: Achieve Peak Tensor Core Performance for GEMM on Blackwell via CUTLASS Python
abstract: We'll walk you, step by step, through how to achieve peak Tensor Core performance for GEMM. We'll provide tutorial examples to help you get started with the CUTLASS Python, and also demonstrate how to further optimize performance using the latest Blackwell hardware and software features.

title: Achieve Truly Serverless GPUs With libfuse, CRIU, and CUDA-Checkpoint
abstract: Unpredictable workloads make inference a natural fit for serverless computing (a.k.a. "functions-as-a-service"). But serverless execution only works if new function replicas spin up quickly. Learn the techniques we've developed at Modal to speed up the boots of GPU containers and achieve truly serverless execution for workloads running on thousands of GPUs.

title: Achieving 8x Lower Cost Analytics with GPU-Accelerated DuckDB
abstract: DuckDB has become the analytical engine of choice everywhere—from notebooks and embedded applications to production data workflows. At the same time GPUs have rapidly evolved into powerful and cost-efficient engines for general-purpose parallel compute. Sirius brings these two trends together by enabling GPU-native execution for DuckDB—without requiring users to change how they write queries. In this session, we’ll explore how Sirius offloads DuckDB workloads to GPUs, accelerating analytics by up to 8x at the same hardware rental cost. Learn how this new architecture combines DuckDB’s simplicity with the power of GPU compute, unlocking faster, more cost-efficient interactive analytics while preserving the elegance of a single-node engine.

title: Achieving Gigascale AI: Insights From Infrastructure Visionaries
abstract: For AI developers and infrastructure pioneers, achieving competitive advantage now demands more than raw compute—it requires unprecedented efficiency, scalability, and automation. This session brings together infrastructure visionaries advancing gigascale AI across leading organizations. They will share real-world architectures, optimization strategies, and operational best practices that enable faster training, smarter orchestration, and greater system autonomy at every layer of the AI stack.

speakers.name: Chris Lesniewski
speakers.jobTitle: Associate VP of Enterprise Infrastructure Services

title: Activate Always-On AI Defense Across Local and Enterprise Agents
abstract:
AI is rapidly transforming the cybersecurity landscape, with security operations centers (SOCs) facing growing AI-powered threats that overwhelm traditional tools and workflows. AI-driven defense is therefore no longer optional—it is a necessity. Organizations are not only re-architecting their SOCs but also their infrastructure, with the transition to AI factories. 


This session explores how organizations can move from experimentation to secure deployment of AI agents using an agentic security platform that streamlines analyst workflows and orchestrates adaptive defenses across dynamic environments. Learn how CrowdStrike’s agentic cybersecurity platform integrates with NVIDIA’s AI stack to secure AI across the enterprise, building a resilient foundation for AI-driven cyber defense at enterprise scale.

title: Adding New Knowledge to LLMs
abstract:
In today's AI landscape, even powerful Large Language Models (LLMs) face limitations when confronted with specialized business knowledge, technical domains, or cultural contexts absent from their training data. While retrieval-augmented generation can mitigate some gaps, true domain mastery requires deeper model adaptation.

This comprehensive workshop equips developers with hands-on skills to transform open-source LLMs into domain-specialized AI assets. Through five interconnected modules, you'll master the complete lifecycle of model customization. By workshop completion, you'll possess the complete technical skillset to develop, deploy, and operate sovereign AI systems tailored to your specific requirements - from data preparation to production scaling. Bring your laptop; we'll provide the GPUs.


Certificate: Upon successful completion of the assessment, you’ll receive an NVIDIA certificate to recognize your subject matter competency and support your professional career growth.


Prerequisite(s):

  • Intermediate Python programming skills.
  • Previous work with LLM-based applications and understanding of prompt engineering principles.
  • Experience with data processing pipelines and text preprocessing techniques.
  • Understanding of fine-tuning, training/validation splits, and basic ML metrics.
  • Basic knowledge of GPU acceleration for ML workloads (CUDA experience helpful but not required).
  • Familiarity with containerization and basic Kubernetes concepts (Optional but helpful).

title: Adding Safety and Perception to Industrial Robots
abstract:
Learn to implement functional safety for robots using an "outside-in" approach. You will gain deep insight into building collaborative, AI-powered factory environments where vision systems and robotics converge to deliver operational intelligence and safety. We will connect industrial robots to factory-wide sensor infrastructure using NVIDIA Metropolis to build intelligent systems dedicated to robot safety. Through practical exercises, you will create a video analytics AI agent that extends a robot's real-time perception. This agent will enable critical safety features, such as automatic speed adjustments (slowing down or speeding up) and risk mitigation, essential for safe co-bot environments. This lab showcases how NVIDIA Halos for Outside-In Safety Agents, powered by NVIDIA Metropolis technology, enable an outside-in approach to achieving advanced functional safety.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Intermediate experience with Python, including packages, virtual environments, and scripting

  • Familiarity with computer vision concepts, object detection, and AI inference pipelines

  • Experience with robotics workflows and sensor integration

  • Knowledge of data streaming, real-time analytics, and message-passing architectures

title: Advance AI Data Center Infrastructure Design and Construction to Accommodate GW Scale (Presented by EdgeConnex)
abstract:
Today’s data center industry is evolving faster than ever, with record demand for gigawatt (GW)-scale designs now paired with the accelerated delivery intervals needed to meet today’s ever-changing technology demands. This race to build large-scale data center infrastructure has fundamentally shifted the industry’s approach, from traditional design and construction methods to ones that are highly optimized for accelerated delivery at GW scale. 

In this session, explore the drivers and overall impacts of designing and building large-scale GW data center infrastructure that serves as the building blocks for AI factories of the future. Better understand the new challenges faced by the industry and the general path forward for designing and constructing large-scale data center architectures at accelerated delivery intervals to accommodate the technology innovations of tomorrow.

title: Advance World Simulation With 3D Gaussian Splatting for Large-Scale Environment Reconstruction
abstract:
Learn how to reconstruct a large scene for robotics testing using NVIDIA Omniverse NuRec Gaussian-based reconstruction technologies to perform multi-GPU training, object segmentation, and extraction. This lab will walk through core concepts with a step-by-step workflow for data capture, reconstruction, and object level-integration for robotics simulation in NVIDIA Isaac Lab.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Omniverse NuRec, Isaac Sim / Lab

title: Advanced Workload Orchestration With NVIDIA Run:ai
abstract:
NVIDIA Run:ai is a comprehensive platform that brings advanced, AI-native scheduling and resource management to GPU infrastructure, enabling enterprises to accelerate and scale AI operations efficiently, reduce costs, and accelerate innovation. In this lab, you will learn the basics of NVIDIA Run:ai and how to create AI workloads. Designed for AI practitioners, this lab guides you through the practical steps of creating, launching, and monitoring interactive, inference, and training workloads using NVIDIA Run:ai.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Familiarity with the NVIDIA Run:ai platform
  • Familiarity with containerized environments (e.g., Docker)
  • General knowledge of AI/ML workflows
  • Experience working with machine learning libraries and tools, such as PyTorch and Jupyter Lab

title: Advances in Decision Intelligence
abstract:
Explore the latest advancements in GPU-accelerated Mixed Integer Programming (MIP) solver, generating fast, high-quality feasible solutions, which is a critical capability for dynamic decision making. You’ll learn about the algorithms behind the cuOpt Linear Programming (LP) solver, which earned the top spot as an open source solver on the Mittelman LPfeas benchmark. 
We’ll explore how Nemo Agent Toolkit and cuOpt optimization are tackling some of today’s most complex real-world decision problems and share our latest work on a modular skill-based decision intelligence agent that enables users to generate optimized decisions and plans through natural language.

Additionally, you'll learn how Kinaxis achieved speedups of up to 14X on key supply chain planning use cases by integrating NVIDIA cuOpt Linear Programming (LP)​ on NVIDIA GPUs into the Maestro platform for scenario planning.

title: Advancing AI and HPC Competency in Higher Education Through Faculty Instructional Enablement
abstract: NVIDIA’s developer training programs support faculty teaching students and researchers through downloadable teaching kits, a cloud-based GPU-accelerated platform, and technical certification exams. Instructional content spans foundational topics—deep learning, accelerated computing, and data science—and advanced areas including agentic AI, natural language processing, and generative AI. Online courses and instructor-led workshops with project-based assessments help faculty align coursework with certifications and support students in earning recognized qualifications. We highlight ECPI integrating GPU-accelerated AI into student projects, and Lebanese American University using hands-on, faculty-led workshops to advance applied learning and prepare students for real-world AI applications and industry pathways.

title: Advancing Multimodal Systems: Alignment, Information Typology, Reasoning, and Cross-Modal Flow
abstract:
This lab explores key principles for advancing multi-modal systems, focusing on multi-modal information typology, alignment, multi-step reasoning, and cross-modal flow. We analyze redundant, unique, and synergistic information, and examine multi-modal alignment through coarse-grained versus fine-grained behavior. The lab then investigates multi-step multi-modality reasoning with external knowledge, where agents integrate images, text, tables, and diagrams to solve complex tasks. Finally, we study multi-modal information flow through cross-modal translation, cross-modal editing, and cross-modal querying, supported by NIM examples for text-to-image generation, semantic image editing, and visual question-answering.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Basic understanding of machine learning and deep learning concepts, including embeddings and neural network architectures.

  • Familiarity with multimodal AI concepts, such as combining text, image, and other data modalities.

  • Experience with Python programming and standard ML libraries (e.g., PyTorch, TensorFlow).

  • Basic knowledge of data visualization techniques (e.g., PCA, t-SNE) for interpreting embeddings.

title: Agentic AI for DAG Reasoning: Build Deterministic Data Engineering Pipelines Through Human-Machine Collaboration
abstract: Data engineering can dramatically benefit from agentic assistance. However, agents are not well suited to many standard data integration tasks, as hallucinations during pipeline execution can produce non-deterministic outcomes, and operations rarely merit the expense of AI at scale. We demonstrate a system that leverages agents to help design abstract data pipelines as Directed Acyclic Graphs (DAGs), then translate those pipelines into code that can be deployed in existing data platforms. This ensures deterministic, traceable, and affordable data workflows, while still benefiting from the huge advantages agents offer in accelerating building pipelines. A combination graph and chat based user interface is demonstrated allowing efficient human-machine teaming for pipeline build-out.

title: Agentic AI for Medical Discharge: Combining Machine Learning with Language Models for ICD Code Prediction- Department of Computer Engineering, Faculty of Engineering, Mahidol University
abstract: We show a new AI system that helps hospitals code medical records automatically. Our system combines machine learning with language models to make coding more accurate and save doctors' time.

title: Agentic AI for the Next Generation of Financial Advisors
abstract: In this session, we'll dive into designing an agentic system to support financial analysis and advisory. Inspired by NVIDIA AI-Q Research Assistant blueprint, and leveraging NVIDIA's multi-modal retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) pipeline, our system not only provides a tool that can take financial analysis—one of the core tasks in finance—to the next level by supporting internal experts, but also makes an important step toward building a sovereign agentic architecture.

title: Agentic AI II: Production Deployment, Safety and Scale
abstract: This session focuses on the deployment of agents in production, and hard engineering required to run models and agents at scale. Our experts will discuss the considerations of hosting open models using high-performance inference engines on your own infrastructure, as well as incorporating frontier models in your workflows. We discuss optimizing, inference speed, and reducing costs as well as implementing rigorous safety guardrails, along with evaluating and auditing models in production.

speakers.name: Neal Vaidya
speakers.jobTitle: Developer Advocate Engineer for Deep Learning SW

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod D (L2)
Connect With the Experts Pod C (L2)

title: Agentic AI in Financial Services: Model Use Cases
abstract: Hear from a panel of leading financial services innovators on how they leverage agentic AI for production use cases. Panelists will also discuss what it takes to get to production with agentic AI in the highly regulated financial services industry, and how they effectively measure return on investment (ROI) for new use cases.

speakers.name: Bobby Grubert
speakers.jobTitle: Head of AI and Digital Innovation

speakers.name: Jennifer St. John-Foster
speakers.jobTitle: Sr. Sales Manager, North America Financial Services

title: Agentic AI Using Open Source Models: Finetuning, Deployment and Chip Design Case Studies
abstract:
Open models have evolved from experimental alternatives to production-grade building blocks for agentic AI and enterprise applications. But deciding when to use open source models, and choosing and customizing them requires a clear framework. 

In this session, we'll walk through a practical decision framework for deploying open models at scale from picking the right model size for your agentic tasks to the software and practices for customizing, evaluating, and deploying them. We'll bring it to life with two case studies: how CodeRabbit runs open-source models in production, and how NVIDIA’s chip design team turned early failures into a repeatable flywheel: using a decision framework to pick use cases, then fine-tuning open-source models for the patterns that stuck. Whether you're building agentic AI, internal copilots, or cost-sensitive services, this talk will give you the framework and tools to ship with confidence.

title: Agentic AutoML with RAPIDS-Accelerated AutoGluon and NeMo Agent Toolkit
abstract:
We introduce NeMo-RAPIDS AutoML Agent, an agentic AutoML framework designed for tabular and time-series tasks. Unlike prior LLM-based AutoML systems (e.g., AutoML-Agent), our system integrates NVIDIA NeMo Agent Toolkit for multi-agent orchestration and RAPIDS for GPU-accelerated AutoML.

Through tool-augmented reasoning, LLM agents invoke high-level APIs (AutoGluon, RAPIDS) instead of writing raw code, enabling both autonomy and performance. Experiments on public datasets demonstrate up to 13% improvement in quality and 5–10x faster runtime, proving that agentic orchestration plus GPU acceleration can make LLM-driven AutoML efficient, scalable, and enterprise-ready.

title: Agentic Edge AI: Nemotron Training & Alignment Best Practices
abstract:
Discover how NVIDIA developed the Nemotron family of edge-ready models, setting new benchmarks for accuracy and performance. This session explores standard pre-training to advanced pruning and distillation for edge-scale efficiency. We will also detail the alignment techniques used to create state-of-the-art multimodal reasoning models. Finally, learn best practices for domain adaptation and the deployment of customized Nemotron models for gaming and AI applications on GeForce RTX platforms.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Jani Joki
speakers.jobTitle: Director, Benchmarking and GeForce Technical Marketing

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod F (L2)
Connect With the Experts Pod B (L2)

title: Agentic Engineering Development: Planning, Coding, Deployment
abstract:
Our Prometheus framework for agentic engineering coordinates planning, coding, and deployment agents to automate software development. Explore the unification of human oversight and AI agents, in which we offer a new approach to the software development life cycle. Discover the benefits of our framework and the lessons we learned while working on it. 

The system consists of a multi-pronged approach. Planning involves gathering project requirements, business insights, and creating epics and tasks. We combine this with coding, frontend, backend and testing efforts, deployment, and verification.

title: Agentic Systems for End-to-End Surgical Workflows
abstract:
In this presentation, we'll introduce the Surgical AI Copilot, an agentic assistant developed by NVIDIA and Orsi Academy to support robotic surgery in the operating room. The system combines MCP-based agentic control with NVIDIA Holoscan, enabling low-latency video processing, streaming, and real-time interaction with surgical workflows. This tight integration operated on a IGX optimized for healthcare application allows the Copilot to operate alongside robotic systems with the responsiveness and determinism required in safety-critical environments, while remaining invisible when not needed.
Through the lens of robotic surgery, this session highlights how agentic AI and fluent human–machine interaction can transform the operating room into a more intuitive, adaptive environment. One where AI augments surgical expertise without introducing friction, complexity, or distraction.

title: Agentic UI in XR Solutions With NVIDIA and Apple Vision Pro
abstract:
We’re at a pivotal moment to reimagine how we interact in extended reality (XR), shifting from complex, traditional interfaces to intuitive, conversational experiences powered by voice and AI. This evolution lets us focus on creativity and exploration, not learning mechanics. 

The Agentic UI architecture reshapes the traditional interaction model by introducing intelligent, conversational interfaces accelerated by NVIDIA's AI and rendering stack using the following design pillars:  

•	Natural Language Interface: Replace traditional user interface (UI) controls with conversational AI 
•	Context Awareness: Maintain rich understanding of user intent and application state 
•	Proactive Assistance: Anticipate user needs and provide intelligent guidance

title: Agility With the Speed of Light, Powered by NeMo Agentic Toolkit
abstract:
The Agentic Scrum Master (ASM) is a multi-service platform using the NVIDIA NeMo Agent Toolkit to orchestrate workflows autonomously.

Its intelligent core integrates five applications/systems that provide proactive insights like predictive risk assessment and Smart assignee recommendations.

ASM achieves true portfolio alignment via full bidirectional Jira-Jira Align linking. This autonomously manages epic-to-capability linking and feature hierarchies, solving a major enterprise coordination problem. ASM's development was lead by a Scrum master in the Agentic AI Hackathon at NVIDIA in 2025. A non-developer picked it up and enhanced it further, inspiring others use it to reduce the admin churn and move from reactive scrum mastering to proactive strategic leadership to deliver highest value to customers.

title: AI Agents at Work for Retail: How Leading Brands are Getting Real Results in Store Operations
abstract:
The retail AI hype cycle is over—now it’s about results. Leading retailers are moving beyond experimental chatbots to deploy AI agents that complete work end to end. From resolving maintenance tickets and managing in-store promotions to routing human resources cases and handling customer issues without human hand-offs, these agents drive bottom-line savings and top-line growth.

In this session, we’ll explore the inflection point retail faces with AI—why those who move now will lead, and what sets successful adopters apart. See how ServiceNow customers deploy AI agents, powered by NVIDIA’s AI infrastructure, to boost efficiency and deliver results. We’ll share what’s working and how AI agents drive workflow automation that solves challenges in store operations, promotions, and service—turning talk into tangible results.

speakers.name: Lorraine Bacon
speakers.jobTitle: Head of Retail and Hospitality Solution Consulting

speakers.name: Kylie Harris
speakers.jobTitle: Head of Retail Innovation and Solutions

title: AI Agents, Robotics, and Digital Twins: The Full Stack of Self-Driving Labs and Biomanufacturing
abstract: The pharmaceutical industry’s quest for self-driving labs demands convergence of intelligent AI agents and precision robotics to create biology data factories and new medicine foundries. Multiply Labs provides the physical execution layer, using modular robotic clusters and digital twins to automate complex cell therapy workflows, reducing variability and setup time while capturing high-quality data. Thermo Fisher is delivering the lab of the future with industry-leading instruments, automation, and digital platforms that enable fully connected, AI-ready labs and end-to-end automated workflows. HighRes Biosolutions powers the labs of top TechBio and pharma through advanced automation, autonomous mobile robots, and agentic orchestration. This connected approach is the blueprint for truly self-driving bio and manufacturing labs.

speakers.name: Olga Ovchinnikova
speakers.jobTitle: Director of Product Management, Digital Labs

title: AI Coding for the GPU: Build a Coding Agent to Help GPU Developers Write Speed-of-Light Code
abstract: Discover the evolution of AI-assisted development for accelerated computing as we move beyond general-purpose coding assistants to specialized agents designed for high-performance CUDA optimization. While frontier models have improved at syntax correctness, they often lack the domain-specific context required to utilize the latest hardware features and achieve "speed of light" performance. This session details NVIDIA’s "CUDA Intelligence" ecosystem—a transition from simple retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) pipelines to advanced agentic workflows using the model context protocol (MCP) and agentic orchestration. We will demonstrate how we are democratizing state-of-the-art code transformation by integrating cloud profiling, Nsight tools, and expert-level optimization skills into a constellation of AI services that can run locally or in the cloud.

speakers.name: Matt Frazier
speakers.jobTitle: Director Software Engineering, AI Technologies for Developer Tools

title: AI Digital Twin of the Climate System
abstract: Scientists use computer simulations to understand how different parts of the Earth’s system interact, or to explore how they respond to external forcing. These simulations have been carried out using numerical models (global climate models, or GCMs). Recently, modern machine-learning-based emulators have been used to reproduce the same dynamics using data. We will present the first generation of AI digital twin of the climate system, using data from state-of-the-art GCMs. These AI emulators can simulate the climate system 100x faster than typical GCMs with one GPU. We will discuss how we build, evaluate, and leverage these emulators to make discoveries about the climate system. For example, it offers strategies to improve simulations of extreme weather such as hurricanes, and forcing them with past ocean states reveals how and why hurricane counts have varied over the past 1,000 years.

speakers.name: Elizabeth Barnes
speakers.jobTitle: Dalton Family Chair in Environmental Data Science and Sustainability

speakers.name: Laure Zanna
speakers.jobTitle: Keller Professor of Applied Mathematics and Professor of Mathematics and Data Science

title: AI Factories in Europe: Building the Foundations for Scalable Intelligence
abstract:
As Europe accelerates its AI ambitions, the concept of “AI factories” has become central to scaling innovation and production. This panel examines the three critical components required for successful AI factories: a reliable and sustainable energy supply, advanced data center infrastructure, and strong customer use cases that demonstrate measurable business value.

Experts will discuss how Europe can integrate these elements to establish efficient, secure, and sovereign AI production systems. The conversation will highlight pathways to strengthen Europe’s competitiveness in the global AI landscape while aligning with sustainability goals and economic resilience.

speakers.name: Carlo Ruiz
speakers.jobTitle: VP, EMEA AI and Data Center Operations

title: AI Factory in Action:  Achieving Agile Automation With Gen AI Agents and Digital Twins (Presented by ASUS)
abstract:
As manufacturing shifts from mass production to high-mix, low-volume demands, flexibility becomes key. Traditional automation faces a critical bottleneck: rigidity. We'll explore the paradigm shift toward the "AI factory," a facility where intelligence is manufactured alongside products. ASUS will present a full-stack architecture with our solution partners that integrates agentic AI—powered by generative AI and vision language models—with digital twins built on NVIDIA Omniverse libraries and OpenUSD.

Discover how to build a high-performance, closed-loop system where AI agents perceive changes, reason through complex tasks, and validate strategies within a high-fidelity virtual environment using NVIDIA Isaac Sim before physical execution. By leveraging NVIDIA Omniverse for digital twins, manufacturers can simulate edge cases and optimize robotic workflows in a risk-free virtual space.

title: AI for Payments
abstract:
Learn from the NVIDIA financial services Solution Architecture team how artificial intelligence is being applied in the payments industry. We'll discuss techniques like tabular transformers, graph neural networks, and NVIDIA NIMs for large language models, and how they apply to critical payments workflows like fraud detection, anti-money laundering, and customer experience.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod D (L2)
Connect With the Experts Pod D (L2)

title: AI For Science: Driving Innovation in Research Labs and Academia
abstract:
AI is revolutionizing the way researchers approach their work and accelerating the pace of discovery. Join this interactive session to discuss the latest methods and models in AI for science, including domain-specific foundation models, research copilots, and autonomous science workflows with NVIDIA engineers and solutions architects. Come chat with us about NVIDIA’s latest research and enterprise products, including collaborations with partners. Some examples include training Nemotron models for data science tasks with NeMo-Gym and NeMo-RL, building agentic research assistants, developing hybrid quantum-classical algorithms with CUDA-Q, accelerating traditional simulations with NVIDIA Apollo and PhysicsNeMo, and AI-driven chemical and materials discovery using NVIDIA ALCHEMI.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod C (L2)
Connect With the Experts Pod D (L2)

title: AI Grid Explained: From Secure AI Factories to Distributed Intelligence
abstract: As AI adoption accelerates, isolated AI factories must scale across to a connected, distributed and secure AI grid that can run diverse workloads —from edge AI applications and telco network functions to internal AI services, digital twins, and RAN—while delivering the right performance and efficiency. This session defines what an AI grid is, why it matters, and how to build it.  The speakers will walk through its reference architecture, from the compute substrate and high-performance networking to an intelligent, workload-aware control plane and orchestration that spans data centers and sites of all sizes. They will show the types of workloads that are ideal for running on an AI grid and share concrete steps telcos can take to implement a secure AI grid, where to start that journey, and how to unlock new revenue and monetization opportunities today.

title: AI Grid for Robots: Applications and Infrastructure for Physical AI Powered by Telcos
abstract:
Physical AI is moving robots from labs into streets, offices, factories, and public spaces—where they must see, think, and act in milliseconds. Traditional cloud-centric architectures can’t meet the latency, reliability, and resiliency demands of these real-world uses, making a distributed AI grid essential. 

Two leading telecom operators will showcase physical AI use cases, demonstrating how AI-enhanced radio networks and distributed computing let robots offload heavy perception and decision workloads while maintaining real-time responsiveness and safety. The session will outline key architectural building blocks of an AI-native network, explore its evolution toward 6G, and explain why telecom AI grids are poised to become the default fabric for the next wave of physical AI.

speakers.name: Ryuji Wakikawa
speakers.jobTitle: VP, Research Institute of Advanced Technology

title: AI Grid in Action: Killer Applications for Distributed Inferencing That Telcos Can Monetize
abstract: This lightning session showcases three edge-native AI applications that only reach their full potential when deployed on a distributed AI grid powered by telecom networks and NVIDIA’s AI infrastructure. Through back-to-back talks, each presenter will showcase their application, the real-world challenge it solves, and the importance of network-proximate inference in enabling hyper-personalized, real-time use cases at scale—all delivered in a cost-efficient and compliant manner. See these applications in motion, hear how NVIDIA’s software stack helps to build and optimize them, and learn how telcos can unlock new revenue streams and return on investment by hosting and scaling these services on the AI grid.

title: AI Grid Tokenomics: Powering Distributed AI Inference at Scale
abstract: As AI usage explodes, optimizing economics and performance of inference is becoming just as critical as training the models themselves. A distributed AI grid makes tokenomics radically more efficient—improving cost per token, time-to-first-token, and token throughput while delivering deterministic performance for the applications that need it most, including highly interactive and latency-sensitive experiences. In this session, two companies advancing AI grids will share which applications they prioritize for distributed versus centralized deployments and how key workload key performance indicators (KPIs) drive those choices. See concrete examples, benchmark results, and economic models that illustrate how NVIDIA-powered distributed infrastructure can enable “least-cost, best-performance” serving at scale—and what that unlocks for the next wave of AI services and business models.

speakers.name: Chris Penrose
speakers.jobTitle: VP and Head of Business Development Telco

title: AI Grids: Why Telcos Hold the Keys to Distributed Intelligence
abstract:
Discover how telcos are leveraging NVIDIA’s platforms and partner ecosystem to transform their networks, AI factories, and infrastructure assets into a unified platform for distributed intelligence. Whether you're focused on sovereign AI, distributed inference, autonomous networks, or 6G innovation, connect with experts shaping how telcos are becoming the intelligent backbone of the AI era.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod D (L2)
Connect With the Experts Pod A (L2)

title: AI in the Age of Regulation: Build Trust Through Compliance
abstract: As global AI regulation accelerates, understanding and aligning with evolving compliance standards has never been more critical. This session brings together AI and legal experts to unpack what the EU AI Act means for organizations developing and deploying AI systems today. The panel explores how companies identify and manage high-risk AI applications and support transparency, accountability, and audit readiness through robust documentation practices. Gain a comprehensive understanding of the EU AI Act framework and the Code of Conduct, as well as the enforcement mechanisms and penalties associated with noncompliance.

title: AI Inference on NVIDIA Jetson Orin AGX GPU for Real-Time Arthroscopic Surgery Assistance
abstract:
AI is transforming medical imaging and surgical navigation by enhancing precision, reducing human error, and improving patient outcomes. Yet, its integration in operating rooms is challenging due to stringent real-time and safety demands.

In minimally invasive procedures such as arthroscopy, limited maneuverability and indirect visualization make tasks like ACL reconstruction technically demanding. AI-driven video-based surgical navigation in edge computing systems offer improved accuracy and efficiency without high-end workstations.

Our recent collaboration with Smith & Nephew features an edge AI surgical assistance pipeline built on the NVIDIA Holoscan framework and Jetson AGX Orin hardware. We optimized three AI subsystems — segmentation, classification, and lens mark detection — validated on cadaveric datasets, achieving real-time performance with dozens of frames per second.

speakers.name: Gabriel Falcao
speakers.company: Instituto de Telecomunicações, University of Coimbra, New York University Abu Dhabi
speakers.jobTitle: Associate Professor of Electrical and Computer Engineering

title: AI Literacy at Scale: K-to-Career Access That Delivers Real Student Outcomes
abstract:
AI literacy has moved from a "nice-to-have" to a readiness requirement. The open question is execution: how schools build access that scales, supports educators, and reaches students across different learning paths, geographies, and resources.

This panel brings together education leaders and practitioners shaping that execution from four angles: classroom and school implementation, learning science and workforce-relevant skill-building, platform-level delivery at scale, and institutional systems that connect education to long-term opportunity.

Together, the panel will share practical approaches to expand AI literacy and access without turning schools into pilot sites. You'll leave with a clear view of what to teach, how to equip educators, how to deploy tools responsibly, and how to build pathways that connect early literacy to real-world readiness over time.

title: AI No Longer Benefits From High-Capacity SSDs—It Requires Them (Presented by Solidigm)
abstract: Huge training datasets are not new. But massive context windows, extended workflows, and rapid iteration are causing a data explosion on the inference side that analysts have dubbed the “memory apocalypse.” Demand vastly outpaces supply, putting AI users in a crunch. The solution: modern SSDs that are fast enough to relieve the pressure while also offering storage in the hundreds of terabytes, enabling unprecedented scale and efficiency.

speakers.name: Ace Stryker
speakers.jobTitle: Director of AI and Ecosystem Marketing, Solidigm

title: AI on the Front Lines of Healthcare: Unlock Clinical, Operational, and Diagnostic Value
abstract: Learn from the digital health software leaders on the front lines of the agentic and ambient AI revolution. Panelists from a crosscut of the digital health ecosystem will share how they are deploying cutting-edge agents to solve critical healthcare challenges, such as transforming physician-patient conversations into structured notes, crafting diagnostic reports in real time, or interacting with caregivers to answer questions and guide them through receiving care.

title: AI Performance and Inference Economics for Engineers
abstract: This technical deep dive analyzes the cost drivers for AI systems, focusing on the interdependent relationship between total cost of ownership (TCO) and performance. We will show how the influence of extreme co-design, spanning silicon, software, and the rack-scale system, unlocks new efficiency levers. The session will detail how these integrated optimizations fundamentally improve operational metrics like cost per token and token per watt in modern deployments.

title: AI Planner: An Agentic Workflow for Supply Chain Optimization
abstract: AI Planner demonstrates an agentic framework for supply chain optimization and is aimed at democratizing operations research to business supply chain planners. We combined discrete optimization with the power of agentic AI and created an end-to-end decision management solution.

speakers.name: Anthony Noguera
speakers.jobTitle: Director IT - AI For Business Applications

title: AI Solves GPS Outages: Making Vehicle Navigation Robust
abstract: Kalman filters (KF) are the most successful tracking algorithm for vehicle navigation. Its accuracy drops when the process noise covariance matrix Q is fixed. Conventional approaches for updating the Qmatrix have stability issues due to aggressive updates. We propose a reinforcement learning (RL)-guided adaptive Q-update for robust vehicle navigation. The proposed RL-guided KF achieves stable state estimation under GPS loss and tough terrain.

title: AI Success Stories: Insights From the Frontlines (Presented by Dell Technologies)
abstract: How will you unlock the true value of AI? That’s the critical question organizations are addressing today. In this session, Honeywell and Southern California Edison will join us on stage to share the AI impact they’re delivering, their challenges and outcomes, and how they turned concepts into production-ready solutions.

title: AI Transformation in Healthcare: Helping Surgeons Help Patients
abstract: The next wave of surgical innovation lies in the intelligence behind robotic systems. Advances in accelerated computing—enabling performance gains on the order of thousands—are transforming surgical data into a powerful feedback loop for continuous improvement. This session will share how a massive leap in computational power—up to 10,000 times greater than before—is enabling continuous learning in the operating room. Real-time analytics, AI-generated technique insights, and digital twin simulations are reshaping how surgeons learn, collaborate, and refine their practice. Combined with remote connectivity and scalable compute, these technologies mark a shift toward smarter, more adaptive operating rooms and improved patient outcomes.

title: AI 与工业的融合：探索工业具身智能新路径
abstract: 我们开发了与英伟达AI生态深度融合的工业具身智能控制平台。该平台基于云边端架构，并采用虚拟化和容器化技术，使得工业控制不再局限于特定硬件和操作系统，进而可根据 AI 算力需求部署到不同的硬件设施上，加速推动了 NVIDIA AI 生态与工业的融合。基于该平台，我们开发了一套工业具身智能机器人拆码垛工作站，并已批量交付给客户，以此验证了上述技术方案的可行性和可落地性。

speakers.name: 晓辉 张
speakers.company: 思摩尔智能科技（广州）有限公司

speakers.name: Xianglin Mao
speakers.company: 思摩尔智能科技（广州）有限公司

title: AI 重塑影视产业：打造基于 NVIDIA 全栈的 AI 算力池，生成式 AI工作流与 OpenUSD 资产平台
abstract:
上海电影集团成立于76年前，是中国电影产业的重要先驱者。2023年，我们迈出了关键一步——投入建设AI算力体系。
我们围绕上海电影集团昊浦智慧产业社区，构建并规模化落地一套面向影视内容生产、产业协同与生态共创的 NVIDIA 全栈智能基础设施体系。构建面向 CG、AIGC、虚拟拍摄与大模型服务的统一GPU算力池，实现跨企业、跨团队的资源池化、服务化与精细化调度；基于 NVIDIA Omniverse 与 OpenUSD 打造影视行业级数字资产平台，将场景、角色与内容资产从“项目文件”升级为“可复用、可流转、可进化的产业级生产资料”；依托 NVIDIA AI Enterprise 与 NVIDIA NIM，将AI能力深度嵌入创作、制作、交付与运营全流程，形成可复制、可审计、可规模化推广的影视AI生产体系。

speakers.name: Yue Xi
speakers.company: 上海昊浦影视文化有限公司（上影集团）
speakers.jobTitle: 上影昊浦智慧产业社区常务副总裁

title: AI-Accelerated Design: From Hyperscale Data Centers to Molecular Discovery (Presented by Cadence)
abstract:
The pace of AI-driven design innovation has accelerated dramatically since early 2025. With the introduction of Cadence’s AI-optimized Millennium M2000 Supercomputer built on NVIDIA, engineers and scientists now have the unprecedented capability to tackle the hardest problems in semiconductor design, system integration, and life sciences.
 
In this session, discover how Cadence and NVIDIA are shaping a unified AI design ecosystem—integrating agentic AI workflows, physics-based models, and simulation-driven approaches to accelerate breakthroughs in chip design, advanced packaging, physical AI, and molecular discovery. Learn how next-generation infrastructure and tools are driving continuous innovation across every scale—from silicon and systems to data centers and molecules.

title: AI-Accelerated Materials Discovery: High-Throughput Virtual Screening of Novel Materials With NVIDIA ALCHEMI
abstract: Leveraging NVIDIA ALCEMI, which confers unprecedented AI-enabled batched simulation capabilities, we performed ultra-fast high-throughput virtual screening of catalysts for hydrogen generation and immersion coolant for GPUs. Our newly discovered material candidates are poised to unlock the potential for hydrogen-driven and AI-driven societies.

title: AI-Augmented RFP Benchmarking Suite: Intelligent Performance Prediction for Heterogeneous Supercomputing Clusters
abstract: We present an AI-assisted benchmarking method that predicts multi-node HPL performance using minimal measurements. The model achieves <3% error up to 32 nodes and detects uncertainty at higher scales, enabling faster and more efficient cluster sizing and request for proposal (RFP) evaluation.

title: AI-Based Software Quantifies Fatty Liver Disease From MRI-PDFF Images Using Feature Extraction to Reduce False Positives
abstract: Our system integrates neural networks with SVM-based feature extraction to automatically detect liver regions in MRI-PDFF images while minimizing false positives. By employing whole-liver quantification instead of traditional ROI sampling, it objectively computes mean PDFF values for more accurate and consistent fatty liver assessment and follow-up.

title: AI-Driven Digital Twins Optimize Facility Simulation and Autonomous Driving (Presented by Delta Electronics)
abstract: Delta has unlocked the potential of NVIDIA Omniverse with scenarios beyond data centers through building automation, autonomous driving, and advanced driver assistance systems (ADAS). Learn how you can maximize efficiency across all built environments.

speakers.name: Gary Harvey
speakers.jobTitle: Development Director, Building Automation Business Group

title: AI-Driven Discovery of Novel JAK3 Inhibitors Using NVIDIA NIM-Accelerated Computational Pipeline
abstract: We accelerated JAK3 inhibitor discovery from months to hours using NVIDIA NIM microservices with GenMol and DiffDock on RTX Ada 6000 GPUs. Our pipeline identified novel drug candidates with better binding than FDA-approved treatments. This end-to-end workflow runs on a single GPU and combines NIM-accelerated AI generation with physics-based validation. Pharmaceutical companies and academic researchers can deploy this modular NIM-powered framework to dramatically reduce drug development timelines and costs for any therapeutic target.

title: AI-Driven Industrial Safety Monitoring: Real-Time Detection and Physics-Informed Safety Risk Reasoning
abstract: We present an AI-based industrial safety monitoring system that performs real-time personal protective equipment (PPE) detection and worker action recognition. Using physics-informed risk reasoning, the system generates automated safety summaries and actionable recommendations, supporting evidence-based decision-making. Deployed on server and edge platforms, it significantly reduces PPE noncompliance and provides timely insights for operators and managers in complex industrial environments.

title: AI-Driven Rapid Diagnosis of Lymph Node Metastasis During Cancer Surgery
abstract: We developed an advanced intraoperative lymph node diagnostic framework that integrates rapid fresh pathology imaging with an AI-assisted analytical pipeline. The demonstrated approach enables accurate intraoperative diagnosis of lymph nodes, markedly improving diagnostic sensitivity and reducing the false-negative rate of intraoperative frozen section analysis.

title: AI-First Design: Build Smarter From Day Zero Through Every Funding Stage
abstract:
The decisions AI-first companies make on Day Zero will radically shift based on initial resources. Does a $5 million seed round change the fundamental AI strategy compared to a bootstrapped $0 start? Absolutely.
This session explores four distinct Day Zero scenarios, each representing a different starting capital: $0 (Bootstrapped), $500,000 (Pre-Seed), $5 million (Seed/Series A), and $50 million (Series B+). For each scenario, we'll highlight high-leverage AI-first tactics, illustrating how capital immediately dictates where to spend resources and how fast to build proprietary technology. You'll leave with a clear, resource-aligned playbook for your own AI-first launch.

speakers.name: Carlos Morais
speakers.jobTitle: Developer Relations Manager - AI Natives (France & Southern Europe)

title: AI-Orchestrating Platform for Drug Repurposing
abstract: We'll introduce a multi-model AI-orchestrated platform that integrates structure-based screening, knowledge-graph reasoning, and gene-expression analysis to accelerate systematic drug repurposing. By unifying multi-modal data and diverse AI models, the system enables cross-domain reasoning from molecular binding prediction to disease-gene inference. A case study on Bremelanotide illustrates how this approach identifies new therapeutic indications through end-to-end computational orchestration.

title: AI-Powered Digital Twin for Rapid Verification in Robotic Manufacturing: Accelerate Motion Planning With Isaac Sim
abstract: We present an AI-driven offline programming (OLP) and digital-twin framework for factory-level automation in automotive manufacturing. By integrating GPU-accelerated motion generation with NVIDIA Isaac Sim for simulation-based validation, we demonstrate how manufacturers can rapidly verify safety, cycle time, and precision before physical deployment, accelerating the digital transformation of production lines.

title: AI-Powered Digital Twin Transforms 7-Eleven Retail Store Design and Training
abstract: See how interactive 3D digital twins reduce retail store setup time by 70% and display costs by 40%, replacing expensive physical mock-ups with fully virtual environments. This self-improving platform integrates real-time inventory management, AI-driven planogram optimization, and demand forecasting — enabling over 7,200 stores to validate layouts, train employees, and test “what-if” scenarios safely before physical implementation.

title: AI-Powered Huge Ensembles for Portfolio Climate Risk Management
abstract: Discover how large‑scale AI‑accelerated ensemble forecasting is reshaping the way we assess physical climate risk in financial portfolios. This session dives into practical, data‑driven methods for quantifying asset‑level climate exposure—bridging advanced climate modeling with real‑world financial and energy use cases. You’ll learn how massive ensembles help uncover cross‑dependencies among temperature, wind, and solar patterns that drive volatility in energy commodities, grid stability, and portfolio performance under extreme conditions.

title: AI4S Machine Learning Potential: Integrated Workflow With Active Learning, Model Distillation, and Multi-GPU Simulation
abstract: Our GPU-accelerated pipeline bridges first-principles data generation, neural potential training, and large-scale atomistic simulation. Starting from automated data generation, representative chemical systems are selected and explored through DFT calculations under an active-learning strategy. Then neural interatomic potentials are trained from scratch or refined via multi-head fine-tuning and model distillation, leveraging GPU-optimized cuequivariant architectures for efficient learning. The trained models are deployed in LAMMPS-MLIAP for massive-scale molecular dynamics, achieving 300,000 atom simulations with full GPU acceleration. This framework unifies data, training, and simulation within a single reproducible ecosystem—transforming traditional materials modeling into a scalable, automated workflow capable of continuously improving its accuracy through iterative GPU-based learning.

title: AIBOX + AIOS：构建生成式 AI 在智能汽车的端侧落地新范式
abstract:
随着生成式 AI 的爆发，汽车正成为继手机之后的下一个核心载体。然而，当前车载系统仍面临端侧算力不足、架构碎片化、高延迟及隐私安全等严峻挑战。
本次演讲将深度解析 AIBox + AIOS 的全栈技术方案。该方案在不破坏既有座舱架构的前提下，通过即插即用的 AI 硬件底座与 AI 原生操作系统，结合 TS-EdgeLLM-Server 推理架构，实现了多模态模型（如 Qwen2.5-VL）的端侧高效部署。通过端云协同与多智能体（Multi-Agent）协作，我们将分享如何优化量化算法与 Eagle3 调优经验，将车辆从“被动响应”升级为“主动感知”的智能体，助力 OEM 构建低延迟、高可靠且可持续演进的车内 AI 体验。

speakers.name: Dongchao Xu
speakers.jobTitle: 智能汽车事业群副总裁

speakers.name: 晟 卢
speakers.jobTitle: AIOS 智能中心-高级软件工程师

title: Al + Quantum: Unlocking the Next Era of Scientific and Industrial Breakthroughs (Presented by Quantinuum)
abstract: The convergence of AI and quantum computing is not a distant vision—it’s unfolding now. Generative Quantum AI (GenQAI) is at the forefront of this shift, creating a feedback loop where quantum computers generate novel data that can enhance classical AI models, while AI systems learn from and guide quantum exploration. Quantinuum has been using the NVIDIA CUDA-Q platform to bring these two technologies together. Their convergence unlocks more than technical achievements—it redefines what’s possible. In pharmaceuticals, it makes the acceleration of drug discovery possible by exploring chemical spaces classical computing cannot reach. In materials science, it makes breakthroughs in superconductors and batteries possible that can transform energy and mobility. In finance and logistics, it opens the door to solving optimization problems that define competitiveness.

title: All Roads, All Rides: Technical Foundations for a Universal Autonomy Platform
abstract: Self-driving vehicles represent the most immediate opportunity for AI to transform the physical world. This talk presents the technical architecture of Nuro’s autonomy platform, designed to generalize across geographies, use cases, and vehicles. Discover how Nuro’s end-to-end AI foundation model learns to drive safely in complex conditions. Learn how the Nuro Driver is integrated into passenger vehicles and how it is validated. See how this approach comes together through an applied example: Nuro’s global robotaxi program with Uber and Lucid.

title: All the Precision, None of the Transistors
abstract: We challenge the notion that modern AI-era GPUs are unsuitable for high-precision computations, specifically double-precision (FP64), the cornerstone of scientific computing since 1985. While scalar and vector FP64 throughput in GPUs have continued to improve over generations, Tensor Core throughput for FP64 has not grown at the same rate as reduced-precision operations used by AI. This is alleviated with mixed-precision linear algebra algorithms and Ozaki schemes that utilize reduced-precision Tensor Cores delivering significant performance and efficiency gains while guaranteeing native FP64 accuracy. We will showcase recent advancements in these algorithms, how to use them, and demonstrate their effectiveness in real-world applications. We will also preview an experimental analysis tool designed to improve developer productivity in reasoning about precision choices.

speakers.name: Harun Bayraktar
speakers.jobTitle: Sr. Director of Software Engineering, Libraries

title: Always-On Autonomous AI Agents: Exploring the OpenClaw Abstraction
abstract:
As agents evolve from basic conversational interfaces to personal complex task navigators, the primary challenge shifts from writing context strategies to designing robust systems. This hands-on workshop explores the engineering needed to manage persistent agent state, secure tool execution, and complex workflow creation in a low-code, high-capacity context. 

Using OpenClaw, a popular open-source agent gateway, we illustrate how a single daemon can manage sessions across multiple applications, maintain long-term interpretable memory, and safely execute tools in a virtual user-centric environment. We emphasize low-code abstractions and out-of-the-box capabilities while maintaining enough rigor to motivate effective and secure best practices. You'll be able to expand on these ideas and improve your agents' capabilities as new innovations arrive and the field continues to evolve.

title: Always-On Innovation: How Nasdaq Deploys AI in Mission-Critical Market
abstract: In this session, we'll explore how an exchange moving hundreds of billions of dollars in trades each day evaluates and governs AI under strict latency, reliability, and transparency requirements. We'll discuss where AI can augment the market stack, and where deterministic behavior must remain non-negotiable.

speakers.name: Ioana Boier
speakers.jobTitle: Global Head of Capital Markets Strategy

title: An AI-Driven Autonomous Lab of the Future for Chemistry and Materials Science
abstract: Explore how agentic AI and automated laboratories are transforming chemistry and materials discovery by seamlessly integrating AI-driven atomistic simulations with experimental workflows. Learn from industry leaders how they're accelerating the path from computational prediction to lab validation, enabling faster discovery cycles and breakthrough material innovations that were previously out of reach.

title: An Ecosystem for End-to-End Multi-Modal Data Curation
abstract:
Multi-modal data from diverse domains is the lifeblood of the Gen AI revolution, but curating and discovering datasets is expensive and time consuming. With the scarcity of efficient tools to unify data discovery, debiasing, and blending multi-modal human-labeled datasets for reuse in dynamic downstream model development tasks, there is a tremendous opportunity to drastically reduce data annotation cost and data pre-processing compute.

We present inventions utilizing technologies such as agentic AI, synthetic data generation, and multi-modal embedding models closely integrated with the NVIDIA tool suite. Our end-to-end ecosystem exhibits downstream performance improvements on multiple task types (pre-training, post-training). These tools can rapidly cut down cycle times for foundation model teams, and power smarter and more aligned Gen AI models across numerous industries.

title: An Introduction to Robot Simulation
abstract:
Physical AI is transforming how robots interact with the world. Building these advanced systems requires three computers: compute for training, advanced simulation for validation, and efficient edge deployment. With a simulation-first approach, robots can train across millions of scenarios, generalize faster, and adapt to real-world complexity. 
 
 In this technical deep-dive session, learn core robotics simulation workflows, from building virtual environments to configuring robots, sensors, and physics, using NVIDIA Isaac Sim and Isaac Lab, built with NVIDIA Omniverse libraries. You'll also explore how to generate and leverage synthetic data with NVIDIA Cosmos world foundation models to train and validate robot perception and control policies before deploying them in the real world.

title: Architect for AI Breakthroughs: Co-Design Memory + Storage for What’s Now and Next (Presented by Samsung Semiconductor)
abstract: As AI workloads evolve toward complex agentic reasoning and large-scale physical intelligence, the integration between compute and the memory-storage hierarchy has become vital. This session explores collaborative architectural approaches co-designed to support the transition to the NVIDIA Rubin platform powering AI factories, alongside platforms powering AI at the edge and physical AI. We will discuss the technical integration of HBM4, HBM4E, and SOCAMM2 within accelerated CPU-GPU clusters to optimize system-level throughput and thermal efficiency, while also examining the role of high-performance PCIe Gen6 storage in emerging inference context management frameworks. Join us to explore how these synchronized memory and storage layers provide the robust performance and reliability required for the current state and next frontier of AI.

title: Architect the AI Factory for the NVIDIA Rubin Era: Scaling is Key (Presented by GIGABYTE)
abstract:
