With the incredible capabilities of large language models (LLMs), enterprises are eager to integrate them into their products and internal applications for a wide variety of use cases, including (but not limited to) text generation, large-scale document analysis, and chatbot assistants. The fastest way to begin leveraging LLMs for diverse tasks is by using modern prompt engineering techniques. These techniques are also foundational for more advanced LLM-based methods such as Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT). In this workshop, learners will work with an NVIDIA language model NIM, powered by the open-source Llama-3.1 large language model, alongside the popular LangChain library. The workshop will provide a foundational skill set for building a range of LLM-based applications using prompt engineering.


Certificate: Upon successful completion of the assessment, you’ll receive an NVIDIA certificate to recognize your subject matter competency and support your professional career growth.


Prerequisite(s):

  • Familiarity with basic programming fundamentals such as functions and variables.

title: Building Local, Sovereign AI in Practice (Presented by Nscale)
abstract: Europe has built the foundation for having domestic AI compute positioned within the region, with projects across the U.K., Norway, Portugal, Iceland, and beyond, all using NVIDIA-accelerated infrastructure to keep compute, data, and value on European terms. In this 40-minute session, you’ll get a fast, practical view of how public–private partnerships are structuring these deployments—what’s working, what’s hard, and how to apply the same playbook in your own country or organization.

title: Building Observable and Scalable Multi-Agent Workflows for Asset Lifecycle Management
abstract:
Learn to develop multi-agent workflows tailored for Asset Lifecycle Management use cases within industrial process applications. You'll be introduced to the NVIDIA open-source NeMo Agent Toolkit (NAT) and its key features, enabling the creation of a comprehensive and customizable reference solution for this use case. You'll also be guided in effectively utilizing other components of NVIDIA AI stack, including NVIDIA Inference Microservices (NIM), cloud endpoints, and NVIDIA Tesseract time series foundational models. These components are used to design agents capable of executing frequently performed tasks such as data querying and analysis, Forecasting, Remaining useful life prediction, and Anomaly detection. Furthermore, you will gain insight into NAT’s marquee features, such as workflow observability and sizing, prompt optimization, and third-party connections.


Certificate: Upon successful completion of the assessment, you’ll receive an NVIDIA certificate to recognize your subject matter competency and support your professional career growth.


Prerequisite(s):

  • Basic familiarity with Python.

title: Building Proprietary Multi-Agentic AI Workflows for a Consumer Banking Operational Use Case (Presented by Capital One)
abstract: Capital One leverages its own proprietary, multi-agentic AI framework to help several hundred bank customer service agents that specialize in complex fraud resolution to understand, analyze, respond to real-time customer queries with more speed/quality than ever before. Explore how Capital One uses multi-agent collaboration to process long-form conversational audio between customers and service agents—or across agents—to generate accurate, structured, and low-latency summaries. These summaries enhance the human agent experience—especially by reducing the large cognitive load required to accurately recap long-form discussions, which can last up to an hour for complex fraud scenarios—as well as the customer experience by advancing quality reviews, speed, and agent productivity, while meeting rigorous business standards around accuracy, reliability, and risk management.

title: Building Safer Physical AI: Certification, Cybersecurity, and Agentic Control
abstract:
Safety, cybersecurity, and AI safety are interconnected mission-critical properties of physical AI. As autonomous systems move into the real world, they require a foundation of trust spanning from sensors and hardware platforms to operating systems, reasoning models, and agentic functions. 

Join us to explore the NVIDIA Halos safety framework and learn how to architect certifiable systems for Level 4 autonomous vehicles and robotics. We’ll examine the design of robust hardware and software safety foundations—integrating hardware platform and operating system—to ensure rigorous safety and systematic cybersecurity by design across the entire stack.  You'll learn how to leverage NVIDIA DRIVE Hyperion for automotive, and NVIDIA IGX for robotics, to implement advanced inside-out and outside-in functional safety use cases for scalable autonomy.

title: Building Sovereign AI Systems Under Real-World Constraints (Presented by Radiant)
abstract: This session examines sovereign AI as a systems engineering challenge that emerges from the interaction of architecture, control planes, operations, and life-cycle ownership. Drawing on real-world experience designing large-scale AI infrastructure under persistent supply and operational constraints, the talk focuses on the long-term consequences of foundational design decisions and how they shape autonomy, resilience, and adaptability over the life of an AI system.

title: Building Sovereign AI: Scaling 100B+ Model Training on NVIDIA Blackwell Infrastructure (Presented by Lablup, Inc.)
abstract:
Dive deep into the real-world challenges of training 100 billion-plus parameter foundation models from scratch on NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs. We'll share hands-on experiences from a national-scale sovereign AI initiative, covering fault-tolerant orchestration, storage I/O optimization, and NVIDIA Collective Communication Library (NCCL) tuning strategies that keep massive training jobs running reliably across more than 500 B200 GPUs.

At this scale, GPU errors, NCCL timeouts, and storage slowdowns begin to appear. Learn how we built a resilient stack with automated failure recovery, resolved 10x storage degradation from NFS driver issues, tackled MXFP8 precision stability on Blackwell, and tuned RoCE and NVIDIA InfiniBand networks through cross-vendor collaboration.

title: Building the AI Stack of Tomorrow: IBM and NVIDIA’s GPU-Accelerated Full-Stack (Presented by IBM)
abstract:
Accelerating AI at enterprise scale demands a unified platform that seamlessly connects data, software, and infrastructure. This session examines how IBM and NVIDIA bring together GPU-proximate storage, open AI and data services, and accelerated computing to deliver production-ready AI at scale. Learn how IBM Fusion and watsonx integrate with NVIDIA’s platform to eliminate data bottlenecks, streamline pipelines, and enable secure, scalable deployments. We’ll also highlight how IBM Storage powered NVIDIA’s Velox project—advancing a new era of GPU-aware, data-intelligent infrastructure.

Also hear from Liqiang Wang, BioHPC Director at UT Southwestern, on how the medical center is deploying next-generation AI infrastructure with IBM Fusion and NVIDIA’s AI Data Platform to train larger models, process complex datasets, and power advanced research - from accelerated drug discovery to AI-driven clinical training and improved researcher productivity.

title: Building the Architecture of Advertising Intelligence: How GPU-Accelerated AI Agents are Reshaping Digital Advertising
abstract: Digital advertising now rivals high-frequency trading in both speed and complexity: tens of millions of auctions occur every second, each demanding a decision in under a millisecond. AI is transforming the industry as agents autonomously plan, negotiate, and execute media transactions in real time. This session details how PubMatic re-engineered its global infrastructure using NVIDIA-accelerated computing to power some of the world’s first agentic advertising transactions, offering a blueprint for how GPU-driven intelligence is reshaping not just programmatic advertising, but real-time digital markets of every kind.

speakers.name: Vasu Cherlopalle
speakers.jobTitle: VP of Real-Time Bidding and Analytics

title: Building the Enterprise AI Factory:  A Unified Stack With ASUS, IBM, and NVIDIA (Presented by ASUS)
abstract:
Most enterprise AI initiatives fail to scale—not because of models, but due to fragmented infrastructure, data bottlenecks, and weak governance. GPUs alone are not an AI factory.

In this session, ASUS, NVIDIA, and IBM present a validated, converged AI factory architecture that transforms raw data into trusted intelligence for agentic AI and retrieval-augmented generation at scale. This end-to-end solution combines NVIDIA Certified servers, NVIDIA AI Enterprise and NIMs, and IBM intelligent storage to accelerate inferencing while ensuring security and governance.

Learn how Red Hat OpenShift, NVIDIA GPU Operator, NVIDIA NIM Operator, and IBM Content Aware Storage enable containerized AI deployments from the data center to the edge—supported by validated blueprints and centralized management for faster, more reliable AI production.

speakers.name: Vincent Hsu
speakers.jobTitle: VP, IBM Fellow, CTO for IBM Storage

title: Building the Future of AI Infrastructure: The HUMAIN-AWS AI Factory Partnership (Presented by AWS)
abstract: Discover how HUMAIN and Amazon Web Services are pioneering sovereign AI infrastructure at unprecedented scale through their groundbreaking AWS AI Factory partnership. This session showcases a multi-gigawatt AI Zone engineered from inception to serve accelerating local and global demand for AI compute using NVIDIA GB300 GPUs.

speakers.name: Dr. Yaser Al-Onaizan
speakers.jobTitle: Deputy CEO & President of AI Products

title: Building the Intelligent Control Plane for Medical Imaging
abstract: A shortage of scalable clinical expertise is becoming healthcare’s limiting factor, not access to imaging hardware. In this session, Taha Kass-Hout, Global Chief Science and Technology Officer at GE HealthCare, will share architecture patterns for an intelligent control plane where multimodal models run at the edge to interpret images plus workflow context and guide real-time decisions with human oversight. Using autonomous X-ray as an early proof point, you will learn how accelerated computing enables clinical-grade latency and deployment, and how simulation and digital twins support safe validation at scale. Roland Rott, President and CEO of Imaging at GE HealthCare, will highlight real-world examples and measurable outcomes from AI adoption across ultrasound, mammography, CT, and MRI, and how these results inform the path toward increasingly autonomous imaging workflows.

speakers.name: Taha Kass-Hout
speakers.jobTitle: Global Chief Science and Technology Officer

title: Building the Next Frontier for the World’s Largest Sovereign AI Factories (Presented by Yotta)
abstract: India’s AI future will be defined by mass-scale inferencing, not just training—and sovereign AI factories will be the nation’s strategic engine for that shift. This session explores how Yotta, powered by NVIDIA, is building India’s first AI-inferencing-optimized cloud, and what the country must prepare for as demand accelerates.

title: Building, Measuring, and Using AI Scientists
abstract: Learn how AI scientists can automate generation and testing of hypotheses with experiments in a loop. See how to maintain long-running coherence required for their days runtime, and the nuances of benchmarking the process of discovery.  Explore their automated discoveries across domains like molecular biology, materials science, and chemistry.

title: Can Large Language Models Explain Themselves? A Study of LLM-Generated Self-Explanations
abstract: LLMs excel at diverse natural language processing (NLP) tasks, from sentiment analysis to reasoning and summarization. Because they are instruction-tuned to produce helpful responses, they often generate self-explanations — rationales accompanying their outputs. For instance, when analyzing a movie review, an LLM may identify sentiment-laden words like “fantastic” or “memorable.” We investigate such self-explanations in sentiment analysis, focusing on feature attribution explanations. We explore different elicitation strategies, assess their faithfulness, and compare them with traditional methods like occlusion and LIME. Our experiments show that LLM self-explanations are comparably faithful, differ notably from traditional ones, and are far cheaper to obtain. These findings invite a reconsideration of current interpretability practices in the era of instruction-tuned LLMs.

title: CAPA: Cross-View Automated Part Alignment via SAM-Guided Point Sampling for 3D Part Segmentation
abstract: The limited part-level understanding capability of existing 2D semantic segmentation models is a bottleneck in 2D-to-3D part segmentation. So we introduce cross-view automated part alignment (CAPA), a novel zero-shot multi-view framework that systematically unifies state-of-the-art 2D and 3D models to achieve superior, class-agnostic 3D part segmentation. It utilizes ControlNet and Semantic-SAM to generate and segment 36 geometrically consistent, high-fidelity 2D part masks across multiple views. Next, it employs PTv3 for feature extraction and a novel Wasserstein distance-based clustering approach for cross-view integration, ensuring consistent 3D part definition. CAPA achieved 15% class-agnostic mIoU improvement over existing methods on challenging benchmarks: PartObjaverse-Tiny and PartNetE. And it only requires 5-9 minutes per object on an RTX 3090 (vs. 15-20 min for other models).

title: Capital Group, Global Asset Manager, Transforms Investor onboarding with NVIDIA and Infosys AI Next platform (Presented by Infosys)
abstract:
Capital Group Investor Onboarding Transformation:

Infosys modernized and deployed a portfolio guidelines analysis solution on Infosys AI Next platform, powered by NVIDIA NIM for Capital Group. This AI-based solution streamlines the review of compliance language to speed up client onboarding, enable analysis and research, and reduce reputational and financial risk.

The solution automatically extracts compliance language from investment guidelines documents, categorizes it, and maps the language to applicable compliance rules. This implementation reduces manual work and enables portfolio guidelines team to review and approve onboarding of clients with significantly higher speed and precision.

The platform, in production today, has increased the efficiency of review and processing volume significantly across customers’ onboarding workflows.

speakers.name: Badri Devalla
speakers.jobTitle: AVP and Global Head of AI and Automation Solutions

title: Category Theory in Commodities: Applying Sheaf-Based Graph Neural Networks and Quantum Portfolio Optimization
abstract: We apply sheaf neural networks, a generalization of graph neural networks (GNNs), to model commodities and capture cross-curve, cross-sector, and macro linkages. These models are used to generate probabilistic forecasts of returns, volatility, and co-movements. The resulting signals can then be combined with quantum-based portfolio optimization techniques to explore risk–return trade-offs.

speakers.name: Yigal Jhirad
speakers.jobTitle: Portfolio Manager/Head of Quantitative and Derivatives Strategies

title: Characterizing Communication Patterns in Distributed LLM Inference
abstract: This work investigates communication dynamics in distributed LLM serving—analyzing how various parallelization approaches coordinate data exchange between GPU workers during inference. We study transformer-based models as representative examples of contemporary architectures widely used in operational deployments. Our work combines detailed profiling measurements with predictive analytical models to characterize communication behavior across different parallelization configurations.

title: China AI Networking Day - 迎接 10 亿瓦级 AI 工厂的时代
abstract: 当代理式 AI、生成式 AI 重塑产业边界，当数据中心成为 AI 工厂，海量算力、算存协同与能效优化成为新一代 10 亿瓦级（Gigawatt）数据中心的基石，一场定义未来的技术盛宴即将启幕！本场线上活动将围绕下一代 AI 工作负载对超大规模网络、无限互连能力和全数据中心综合能效来展开，从 NVIDIA、业界领先客户、合作伙伴以及科研学者的角度来探讨领先的 NVIDIA® NVLink™ ，NVIDIA® Quantum-X InfiniBand、NVIDIA® Spectrum-X™ 以太网、NVIDIA® BlueField® DPU/SuperNIC™ 、NVIDIA® ConnectX® SuperNIC™ 网络平台及其最佳实践，特别是专为大规模 AI 而设计的 NVIDIA® Spectrum-X/XGS 以太网络及其相应的软、硬件优化参考设计如何助力 AI 工厂实现数百万级 GPU 的无缝扩展，缩短部署时间，并优化整体性能和功耗。

title: Claim Your Free Self-Paced AI Infrastructure Training
abstract:
Get the most out of your GTC experience and claim up to $200 in free AI infrastructure training!

Visit the Technical Training Area on the 2nd floor of the Signia Hotel to see how NVIDIA can help you and your team boost AI infrastructure skills.

Join us in the Paseo Room to:

  • Connect with NVIDIA training experts
  • Engage directly with instructors
  • Select and enroll in your free self-paced course using available laptops.

Choose from the following courses that can help you prepare for AI factory deployments and NVIDIA certification:

    AI Infrastructure and Operations 

    • AI Infrastructure and Operations Fundamentals*
    • NVIDIA AI Enterprise Deployment on Bare Metal Kubernetes 
    • NVIDIA AI Enterprise for Azure Professionals 
    • Run:ai Platform Development^

    AI Networking 

    • Ansible Essentials for Network Engineers^
    • BlueField® DPU Administration*^
    • Cumulus® Linux Administration^
    • Data Center Management Made Easy With NVIDIA® UFM®*^
    • InfiniBand Network Administration*^
    • The Fundamentals of RDMA Programming*

    *Part of a learning path to prepare for NVIDIA Certification
    ^Part of the AI Factory Deployment Training Learning Path


Hours of Operation:

Monday, March 16 | 2:00 p.m.-6:00 p.m.

Tuesday, March 17 | 8:00 a.m.-5:00 p.m.

Wednesday, March 18 | 8:00 a.m.-5:00 p.m.

Thursday, March 19 | 8:00 a.m.-3:00 p.m.


Location: Signia Hotel Paseo Ballroom (2nd Floor)

title: Claim Your Free Self-Paced Developer Training
abstract:
Maximize your GTC experience and unlock up to $90 in free developer training!


Visit the technical training area on the 2nd floor of the Signia Hotel to explore how NVIDIA can help you and your team level up your Al and accelerated computing skills with hands-on technical training.

Stop by the Paseo Room at the Signia Hotel to select your course and enroll. Connect with training experts, engage with instructors, and choose your free self-paced course.


Laptops are available to enhance your learning experience, with courses designed to prepare you for NVIDIA's Generative Al and data science certifications, including:

    Accelerated Computing & Data Science 

    • Accelerating End-to-End Data Science Workflows*
    • Accelerated Machine Learning for Time Series Forecasting*
    • Getting Started with Accelerated Computing in Modern CUDA C++
    • Optimizing CUDA Machine Learning Codes with Nsight Profiling Tools*

    Generative AI, LLM Development & Deep Learning 

    • Building LLM Applications With Prompt Engineering*
    • Generative AI with Diffusion Models*
    • Getting Started with Deep Learning*
    • Introduction to Transformer-Based Natural Language Processing*
    • Rapid Application Development With Large Language Models*

    Agentic AI & Production Deployment 

    • Building Agentic AI Applications with Large Language Models (LLMs)*
    • Building RAG Agents with LLMs*
    • Introduction to Deploying RAG Pipelines for Production at Scale*
    • The Art of Compressing LLMs: Pruning, Distillation, and Quantization Demystified

    *Part of a learning path to prepare for NVIDIA Certification


Hours of Operation:

Monday, March 16 | 2:00 p.m.-6:00 p.m.

Tuesday, March 17 | 8:00 a.m.-5:00 p.m.

Wednesday, March 18 | 8:00 a.m.-5:00 p.m.

Thursday, March 19 | 8:00 a.m.-3:00 p.m.


Location: Signia Hotel Paseo Ballroom (2nd Floor)

title: Claws: How to Build Safe and Secure Long Running Agents
abstract:
Running today’s self-evolving AI agents is fundamentally insecure. Agents such as OpenClaw operate with real system access but lack a robust, infrastructure-level security model. Tool-level restrictions can be bypassed when agents discover alternate execution paths, and container or prompt-based guardrails are insufficient for long-running systems. In this session, experts will discuss infrastructure-level security and runtime platforms for deploying autonomous agents more securely by default.

Please Note: Ryan Angilly will be joining us for the Wednesday session only. We encourage you to attend this session if you'd like to hear from him directly.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod D (L2)

title: Climate Prediction and Smooth Navigation Use Cases
abstract:
This session will introduce two use cases of NVIDIA platform adoption—one in climate prediction, and the other in smooth navigation.

Climate Prediction: Accurate climate prediction relies on capturing small-scale processes, such as clouds and turbulence, which remain elusive in traditional models. This talk presents the CliMA solution: a physics-AI hybrid Earth System Model built in Julia to run natively on NVIDIA GPUs. Leveraging GPU acceleration, researchers generate large ensembles to drive a "calibrate, emulate, sample" workflow. 

Generalized Navigation: the CUDA-accelerated Delta-Corridor framework utilizes Delta-Corridor spatial modeling to achieve reliable and smooth generalized navigation with Isaac and Cosmos. By applying continuous safety corridors, it ensures reliable navigation for mobile robots, boats, and autonomous vehicles in dynamic environments.

speakers.name: Tapio Schneider
speakers.jobTitle: Professor of Environmental Science and Engineering

title: Close the Sim-to-Real Gap: Next-Gen Techniques for Synthetic Data Mastery (Presented by World Wide Technology)
abstract: Stop relying on limited real-world datasets. Join us to explore proven techniques to leverage NVIDIA Omniverse Replicator and NVIDIA Cosmos to create synthetic imagery for building robust computer vision models that recognize rare objects and challenging conditions. We will dive deep into synthetic imagery best practices, the NVIDIA hardware and software stacks that accelerate the process, and the critical decision points that maximize your synthetic data return on investment and accelerate your path to production-ready physical AI.

speakers.name: Paige Reiter
speakers.jobTitle: Technical Solutions Architect II, Cloud and Infrastructure

title: Closing the Sim-to-Real Gap in Physical AI With Real-Time Control Stack
abstract: We explore a real-time execution stack that unifies NVIDIA Isaac Robot Foundation Models, ROS2, and software-based motion control to achieve sub-millisecond, deterministic performance — enabling precise, synchronized actions in the real world and closing the sim-to-real gap in physical AI. We also introduce sensor-driven fine-tuning methods to improve real-world adaptability through residual policy learning and LoRA-based adaptation.

title: Co-Engineering Grace CPU Platforms for Hyperscale
abstract: Meta operates one of the world’s largest and most diverse computing fleets, spanning AI training and inference, web services, ranking, caching, analytics, and video processing. In this technical session, leaders from Meta and NVIDIA share how they co-engineer NVIDIA Grace CPU–based platforms end‑to‑end to meet the performance, efficiency, and scale demands of hyperscale fleets. You’ll see how joint work across silicon, platform design, and infrastructure integration improves performance per watt, rack density, and overall fleet efficiency, and how these platforms enable the next generation of hyperscale infrastructure for AI and large‑scale services.

title: ColonVR: Enhancing Colonography Through Artificial Intelligence and Virtual Reality
abstract:
Colorectal cancer is a leading cause of cancer-related deaths. While virtual colonoscopy provides a noninvasive alternative, current approaches depend on manual segmentation and lack immersive exploration. We present ColonVR, a hybrid AI and virtual reality framework that combines NVIDIA MONAI with TotalSegmentator for automated colon reconstruction from unprepared CT scans. Our hybrid pipeline was benchmarked against NVIDIA VISTA 3D, MONAI, CLEAR, and TotalSegmentator, showing improved anatomical accuracy and surface continuity.

In a study with 30 participants, users searched for polyps inside a reconstructed colon in VR using three navigation modes: Automatic, Flythrough, and Teleport. Automatic navigation delivered the best trade-off between efficiency, accuracy, and comfort, highlighting how AI-driven reconstruction and immersive VR can advance diagnostic training.

speakers.name: Joaquim Jorge
speakers.company: Instituto Superior Tecnico, University of Lisbon

title: Compress, Cut, and Distill: The Latest Gen AI Model Compression Techniques in Practice
abstract:
This training lab explores the art and engineering of compressing large language models to make them cheaper, faster, and easier to deploy while preserving practical capability. Designed for a broad audience that spans beginners to advanced practitioners, the workshop will introduce foundational concepts for newcomers, share implementation patterns and pitfalls for experienced engineers, and highlight cutting-edge research directions for specialists.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Familiarity with Python/PyTorch: Proficiency in Python programming and basic hands-on experience using deep learning frameworks.
  • Conceptual Knowledge of Deep Learning: Basic understanding of neural networks, backpropagation, and the model fine-tuning process.
  • Familiarity with LLM Architectures: Conceptual understanding of the Transformer architecture, including components like attention mechanisms and feed-forward networks.

title: Computing on the Road: Scaling the Waymo Driver for the Real World
abstract: Autonomous ride-hailing is increasingly a daily reality for more people with Waymo. As the most mature manifestation of AI in the physical world, Waymo’s path has been defined by 200 million miles and over 20 million trips completed without a human specialist present. Drawing on this unparalleled scale of experience, we will explore Waymo’s approach to developing its own custom hardware, the vital role AI compute plays in enabling it, and how this integrated foundation makes our demonstrably safe AI architecture possible.

title: Configure and Tune Robot Assets With OpenUSD and PhysX
abstract:
Learn from NVIDIA experts about OpenUSD best practices and physics tuning of mechanisms, using the example of a robotic hand. Starting from a URDF file, see how to structure, assemble, and optimize USD. Then learn about the physics tuning process, to enable dexterous hands to behave realistically in simulation. We will focus on best practices that make complex hand models performant, controllable, and ready for downstream learning and control tasks.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Intermediate experience with Python, including working with packages, virtual environments, and basic scripting.

  • Familiarity with 3D content concepts such as meshes, materials, transforms, and scene hierarchies from tools like Blender, Maya, or similar DCC applications.

  • Understand command-line usage for running scripts and managing project files.

  • Have familiarity with robotics or simulation workflows (for example, using simulation tools or working with physics-enabled assets).

title: Connect With Cybersecurity, Agentic AI, and Threat Hunter Experts
abstract:
Join NVIDIA cybersecurity and agentic AI experts to learn how NVIDIA technology and agentic systems can be applied to strengthen your cybersecurity posture.

Please Note: Rich Harang will be joining us for the Tuesday 3PM session only. We encourage you to attend this session if you'd like to hear from him directly.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod A (L2)
Connect With the Experts Pod A (L2)

title: Connect With the Experts on NVIDIA's Cloud Technologies
abstract:
Join NVIDIA's cloud team to learn about NVIDIA's portfolio of technologies for building and managing AI factories at scale, including components for monitoring GPU health, automated remediation and resiliency, provisioning and managing multi-tenancy, inferencing and deployment platforms, and more.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod D (L2)

title: Continuous Inference Benchmarking of the AI Frontier
abstract:
As large language model architectures evolve, traditional peak performance metrics are becoming less predictive of real-world efficiency. Effective inference benchmarking now requires a multidimensional approach that balances raw throughput against energy consumption, cost-per-token, and the critical "interactivity" thresholds required for user-facing applications.

This session introduces InferenceX, SemiAnalysis’ open-source benchmark suite designed to navigate this complexity by sweeping the latest software optimizations across leading AI accelerators. It moves beyond static datasheets to examine nightly, reproducible results that reveal how frontier models perform under variable production workloads. Learn how the InferenceX benchmarks are designed, how to run them, and how to interpret the results to make informed decisions.

title: Continuous-Time NODE in Transformers and Physics-Informed Loss as Soft Physical Constraints in Weather Forecasting
abstract: We develop a novel approach to integrate continuous-time Neural ODE dynamics to transformers and implement physics-informed loss as soft physical constraints to learn the spatio-temporal mapping of weather variables in the embedding space of the vision transformer. As climate volatility grows, this approach offers timely improvements in forecast quality without sacrificing transformer's learning capacity.

title: Conversational AI Agent for Transportation Planning
abstract: We present a hybrid AI agent that combines retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) and LoRA-tuned models to achieve 95.7% accuracy in generating transportation planning commands from natural language, reducing technical barriers and letting planners focus on analytical insights instead of memorizing command-line interface (CLI) syntax.

title: Crack the Code: Enable AI Assistants for Massive C++ Codebases
abstract: Most AI code assistants struggle with large, complex codebases because they lack reliable project-wide context, leading to shallow or incorrect suggestions. This talk presents a technical approach to improving AI code generation by combining NVIDIA NeMo Retriever NIM with the Model Context Protocol (MCP). We show how long-context embedding models from NeMo Retriever, paired with AST-based chunking that preserves logical code boundaries, enable higher-quality retrieval across massive C++ projects. By exposing this retriever as an MCP server, AI assistants can dynamically pull in relevant project-specific context, such as engine APIs, architectural patterns, and recent changes, at generation time. The result is more accurate, context-aware AI code generation that aligns with a project’s structure and conventions.

title: Create Generative AI Workflow for Design and Visualization in ComfyUI
abstract:
The latest image and video generation models, combined with LLM, are fantastic tools to build powerful agentic AI workflows, but they're hard to access through too many cloud access points to efficiently iterate on concepts and stay up to date. In this two-hour instructor-led workshop, you'll learn how to use GPU acceleration for ComfyUI on local workstation or servers, pulling the latest models to build complex generative workflows. Workflows will range from image generation and separation, artefact removal, 3D generation, Physically based rendering (PBR) material generation, 360 and HDR Image Based Lighting (IBL) generation, as well as video generation. Whether you are a developer willing to learn what models work best for what tasks, or an industrial designer or digital artist willing to understand how to drive AI with your inputs, this course demonstrates how ComfyUI can be used to power design and visualization workflows with Gen AI.

Prerequisite(s):

  • A desire to learn. Whether you are an expert in models, ComfyUI or a beginner, you should learn something if you are ready to drink the AI from the firehose.

title: Create Noninvasive Diagnoses: Adapting Digital-Twin Model for Hemodynamic Simulation
abstract: We are developing noninvasive diagnostic solutions for CAD to replace risk-bearing, high-cost invasive angiography with safe, efficient, and data-rich alternatives. By combining coronary computed tomography angiography (CCTA), AI-based lumen segmentation, and hemodynamics simulation, clinicians can assess both the anatomical and functional significance of coronary lesions — improving patient safety, healthcare efficiency, and clinical precision.

title: Create Vision AI Applications With Generative AI Coding Agents
abstract:
Computer vision is a cornerstone of the generative AI revolution, providing the essential visual metadata required by vision language models (VLMs) to extract actionable insights. In this two-hour instructor-led workshop, you will learn how to GPU-accelerate your video analytics workflow by combining the NVIDIA DeepStream SDK with the power of prompt engineering. Discover how to generate complete, GPU-accelerated DeepStream pipelines using simple natural language prompts. Whether you are a developer new to DeepStream or an advanced user looking to accelerate your prototyping, this course demonstrates how to produce intuitive, readable Python applications without the manual overhead. Join us to bridge the gap between traditional video analytics and modern GenAI development.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Understanding of Python and basic knowledge of computer vision models.

title: CSO Insights: Driving AI-Optimized Energy Efficiency and Data Center Performance
abstract: Join Chief Sustainability Officers (CSOs) to learn how AI is transforming enterprise energy and infrastructure at scale. This session will include: insights on applying AI to optimize energy efficiency, grid-integrated operations, and enhance data center performance at scale, concrete examples from leading companies—including Amazon, PepsiCo and Equinix—demonstrating measurable impact on operations and supply chains, and executive perspective on translating sustainability and energy efficiency goals into bottom-line impact—exploring how AI efficiency translates into a win-win for operational and strategic advantage.

title: CUDA Developer Best Practices
abstract:
Join this live Q&A session with some of NVIDIA’s own CUDA developers to demystify the process of building real-world CUDA applications. From utilizing CUDA accelerated libraries, profilers, and debugging tools to setting up CI pipelines, packaging, and builds—this session covers the entire development life cycle. If you’re looking for practical guidance on integrating GPU acceleration into your software, this is the perfect opportunity to get answers and advice from engineers who develop and maintain CUDA software every day.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod C (L2)
Connect With the Experts Pod C (L2)

title: CUDA on Windows and WSL
abstract:
Join us for a CWE session with the CUDA Windows and WSL engineering group to ask any questions you might have about performance, best practices, and how to leverage some of our new features and new platforms. Bring your questions/requests for customized answers on any topics: WSL containers, tuning programs for MCDM/WDDM, graphics interoperability (DirectX, Vulkan, OpenGL) and AI in games—even some of the most niche memory allocation and optimizations problems, and much more.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod B (L2)

title: CudaqToEinsum: Efficient Tensor-Network Conversion From CUDA Quantum for Large-Scale Quantum Circuit Simulation
abstract: We present CudaqToEinsum, a high-performance framework for converting quantum circuits into Tensor-network representations. Integrated tightly with CUDA Quantum, our method achieves 30–50× faster translation than the Qiskit + cuQuantum CircuitToEinsum route on 85,000-qubit BPS circuits. With native support for batched contraction, it processes over 150,000 samples simultaneously, achieving up to 200–300× speedups, paving the way for large-scale quantum simulations on HPC platforms.

title: Customer-Centric Innovation in Automotive
abstract: This session showcases how Porsche Engineering fosters innovation in the context of automotive engineering. Learn how NVIDIA technology helps push the frontiers of the automotive V-Model in terms of innovation depth and time-to-market. Learn about methods and components used throughout the vehicle development and validation process to ultimately enhance the customer experience in the car.

title: Cut Engineering Time With a Digital Twin Framework: Case Study on Vision Inspection and Depalletizing
abstract: Since industrial robots in real factories perform a variety of tasks — including welding, inspection, and pick-and-place — the recently developed digital twin framework should be applicable to all tasks for practical on-site deployment. In this study, we collaborated with Hyundai Motors to verify the scalability of the digital twin framework by applying it not only to the welding shop line, but also to vision inspection and depalletizing tasks. The motion planning results for each task were deployed onsite, and it was verified that the engineering time can be reduced by over 88% without compromising productivity. These results confirm that the proposed framework can quickly adapt to diverse robotic workflows, improving performance and scalability across real production lines.

title: cuTile Programming
abstract:
Express your algorithm; cuTile and Tile IR will figure out the threads, data movement, and Tensor cores.

In this talk, we'll present cuTile, NVIDIA's new block-based programming abstraction, and Tile IR, the compiler stack that it is built with. We'll explore examples in C++ and Python from a variety of domains, including inference of a mixture-of-experts large language model and a sparse conjugate gradient solver.

CUDA tile programming divides inputs into local arrays that are processed concurrently by groups of threads. Users write sequential array-centric code, and the framework handles parallelization, synchronization, and data movement behind the scenes. Architecture-specific details are abstracted away—you express your algorithm once in a high-level way, and the system lowers it to the right implementation for any NVIDIA GPU architecture.

title: Cutting-Edge Molecular Dynamics on the Latest Multi-Node NVLink Technology
abstract: Explore new techniques for scaling and optimizing GROMACS molecular dynamics on the latest NVIDIA Multi-node NVLink (MNNVL) systems, via the use of cuFFTMp and NVSHMEM, to minimize communication overhead and maximize performance. Together, we'll walk through practical, transferable steps you can apply to boost performance in your own HPC and AI workloads.

title: Data Analytics and Streaming Media
abstract: In this "Lightning Talks" session, industry experts will explore the evolving world of data analytics and streaming, covering AI-driven innovations in sponsorship analytics, data processing needs of streaming services, and real-time audience insights. Speakers will discuss how advanced data tools are driving end-to-end content automation and improving attribution accuracy, helping businesses optimize strategies and increase engagement. Gain actionable insights on the role of AI in refining analytics and enhancing operational efficiency in the streaming industry.

speakers.name: Bianca Pryor
speakers.jobTitle: Director of Data Science for Media & Entertainment

title: Data to Decisions: GPU-Accelerated Decision Optimization
abstract:
Learn to optimize decision optimization problems with GPU-accelerated workflows using NVIDIA cuOpt, RAPIDS, CUDA-X libraries, and Nemotron models. This hands-on lab dives into advanced LP/MIP solver integration, accelerated primal heuristics, cutting-plane methods, and deployment patterns for large-scale, real-time optimization.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Foundational knowledge of optimization: Familiarity with Linear Programming and Mixed Integer Programming concepts (constraints, relaxations, heuristics, cutting planes).

  • Basic Python proficiency: Ability to read and modify Python scripts used in optimization workflows.

  • Some experience with GPUs or CUDA-based frameworks: Prior exposure to GPU computing (e.g., RAPIDS, CUDA-X libraries) is helpful but not mandatory.

  • Comfort with OR tooling: Experience with at least the optimization solver ecosystem

title: Decoding Quantum Errors With AI
abstract: Learn the basics of quantum error correction (QEC) and why decoding is a primary bottleneck for quantum computing. Using hands-on exercises, learn about key decoder metrics like accuracy, scalability, throughput, and latency and their implications for quantum computation. Lastly, implement your own AI decoder workflow using the CUDA-Q QEC library. We assume that you're familiar with the fundamentals of quantum computing (gates, circuits, measurement, etc.), but do not necessarily have experience with QEC.

speakers.name: Monica Van-Dieren
speakers.jobTitle: Sr. Technical Marketing Engineer, Quantum and HPC

title: Deep Dive on Gigawatt AI Factories
abstract:
As at-scale AI workloads for training and inference make use of gigawatt-scale multi-GPU systems, massive data transfer, and memory access capabilities of Vera Rubin NVL72 systems in AI factories, we will show how these large systems get architected and deployed in the data center, leading to a robust reference architecture embraced globally for its speed of deployment and performance.

This session will showcase internal deployments and the tools and software stack developed and leveraged on these platforms. You'll also have the opportunity to have deep dive views on how multi-node NVLink, system interconnects, and storage, as well as the latest liquid-cooled designs, are shaping the future of AI infrastructure.

title: DeepFrame: Scalable Long-Context Video Understanding With Omnimodal Fusion
abstract: We present DeepFrame, an end-to-end framework for processing multi-hour videos using omnimodal fusion (video+audio+text) across 128k-256k tokens. Our hybrid GPU infrastructure leverages L40S/H100/B200 for data generation and training, with NVIDIA NeMo Curator for automated video annotation. The system employs Flash Attention 2 (40% VRAM reduction) and mixed precision training (2-3x speedup) on AWS ParallelCluster. We evaluate on LVBench, WorldSense, and custom NARU benchmarks for narrative intelligence. Applications include media moderation, education, security, and retail analytics. Results were announced in January 2026. This work demonstrates breakthrough capabilities in long-form video understanding using NVIDIA's hybrid GPU infrastructure for large-scale AI development.

title: Deploy and Customize Your AI Factory: Make Your AI Factory Feel Like Home!
abstract:
This hands-on lab will get you down and dirty with customizing an AI factory. Experience how typical administrator tasks are made easier with NVIDIA Mission Control, and explore the interfaces and tools that NVIDIA Mission Control provides for doing "Day 1 and beyond" work.

Prerequisite(s):

  • To be successful, participants should have an understanding of AI clusters, Linux, and containers.
  • Bring a laptop as this session is very interactive!

speakers.name: Max Steele
speakers.jobTitle: Sr. Technical Instructor in AI and Data Center

title: Deploy and Optimize LLMs and VLMs on NVIDIA Jetson Thor
abstract:
Learn to deploy, optimize, and accelerate large language models (LLMs) and vision-language models (VLMs) on NVIDIA Jetson Thor through hands-on exercises with real hardware. We'll demonstrate production-ready inference frameworks including vLLM and SGLang, apply NVIDIA ModelOpt for quantization, showcase systematic methods to achieve inference performance improvements, and explore advanced capabilities like tool calling and live VLM inference — all running on edge devices you can touch and interact with.

Prerequisite(s):

Required:

  • Basic understanding of Large Language Models and transformer architectures

  • Familiarity with Python programming and container technologies (Docker)

  • Experience with command-line interfaces and SSH for remote device access

  • Laptop with network connectivity (Ethernet preferred, WiFi acceptable)

Recommended:

  • Prior exposure to PyTorch or inference frameworks
  • Basic understanding of model quantization concepts
  • Familiarity with REST APIs and web services

title: Deploy Lambda’s Bare Metal Instances with NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72 & GB300 NVL72 (Presented by Lambda)
abstract: We explore how Lambda’s bare-metal cloud unlocks the full performance of NVIDIA rack-scale systems by removing overhead and delivering the hardware as designed. You’ll learn how direct access to GPUs, CPUs, NVIDIA NVLink, and high-bandwidth fabrics increases efficiency, reduces time-to-train, and enables scaling for frontier AI workloads.

title: Deploy One-Click Agent Blueprints With NVIDIA NeMo Agent Toolkit on On-Prem AI Infrastructure
abstract: Astra Blueprints enables one-click deployment of agentic AI workflows on on-premises NVIDIA infrastructure using the NeMo Agent Toolkit. The framework provides blueprint-based templates to standardize workflow creation and configuration while supporting customization across use cases. With continuous integration/continuous deployment (CI/CD) and GitOps automation, Astra orchestrates code, image, and deployment life cycles via the Astra Console, integrating with Vault, ArgoCD, and JFrog Artifactory. OpenTelemetry ensures unified observability with real-time metrics, traces, and logs. By abstracting infrastructure complexity and ensuring enterprise-grade scalability, privacy, and compliance, Astra Blueprints accelerates the transition from prototype to production—empowering teams to deploy and monitor multi-agent AI systems efficiently.

title: Deploy Reliable Agents at Scale With Reinforcement Learning and High-Performance Inference (Presented by CoreWeave)
abstract: Reinforcement learning (RL) is essential for developing reliable and autonomous agents that learn from experience, delivering enhanced accuracy and cost efficiency. In this deep dive session, learn how to use the technical architecture of CoreWeave’s RL infrastructure and inference solutions, highlighting how to define model weights, use Weights & Biases with serverless RL and the deployment of high performance inference stacks. We will cover use cases to show how to leverage these tools on NVIDIA GPUs to build a unified stack that scales seamlessly, optimizes performance, and accelerates your time to production.

title: Deploy State-of-the-Art Gen AI on Your RTX PRO Workstation
abstract:
This session guides you through the end-to-end deployment of generative image models on NVIDIA RTX PRO workstations by leveraging ONNX Runtime and TensorRT-RTX. We'll demonstrate how to ship a multi-stage model pipeline across a wide set of hardware without sacrificing performance. This workflow will be powered by classic graphics APIs, ONNX Runtime, and the TensorRT-RTX Execution Provider. You'll leave with the skills to convert raw models into high-performance, offline-capable applications that run seamlessly across diverse hardware architectures.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Foundational knowledge of Python and GenAI models
  • Experience programming in C++
  • General knowledge about Generative AI

title: Deploying 8B Models on Edge GPUs: 1.58-Bit Quantized LLM on CUDA Environment
abstract: Explore our edge-optimized quantization approach that enables full 8B-scale reasoning on Jetson Orin NX. By combining 1.58-bit and INT4 quantization with custom CUDA kernels and fused attention/Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP) layers, we achieve 8B inference in just 2 GB VRAM — bringing large-scale generative AI fully offline.

title: Deploying and Optimizing AI Inference at Scale
abstract:
As foundation models move toward deeper test-time computation, inference becomes the dominant scaling constraint. Latency, throughput, and cost are governed by a small set of forces: autoregressive decoding, KV-cache growth, memory bandwidth, and scheduling under contention. This workshop frames large-scale inference through these emerging laws of inference, starting from first principles and building toward real systems. Learners begin with monolithic and gateway-based vLLM deployments on Kubernetes to establish baseline behavior, then transition to NVIDIA Dynamo to operate aggregated and disaggregated inference architectures using built-in KV-aware routing and scheduling. A core emphasis is observability: attendees will deploy a full stack (Prometheus, Grafana, Loki, Tempo) to monitor metrics, capture structured logs, and perform distributed tracing. The outcome is a principled understanding of where inference time and money go - and how architectural choices bend those curves in production.


Certificate: Upon successful completion of the assessment, you’ll receive an NVIDIA certificate to recognize your subject matter competency and support your professional career growth.


Prerequisite(s):

  • Command-line proficiency.
  • Experience working with configuration files (e.g., YAML, JSON).
  • Familiarity with containers and container-based workflows (e.g., Docker) and some basic knowledge of Kubernetes.
  • Basic understanding of large language model (LLM) inference concepts.
  • Familiarity with distributed computing concepts, such as data or model parallelism.

title: Design an Enterprise-Grade Platform for Generative and Agentic AI at Scale (Presented by EXL Service)
abstract:
In this session we explore EXLerate.ai, EXL’s enterprise-grade platform for developing, orchestrating, and running Gen AI and agentic AI solutions at scale. We will illustrate how EXLerate.ai enables capabilities such as domain-aware copilots, agentic workflow orchestration, intelligent automation, and decision intelligence in enterprise environments.

While generative AI presents significant opportunities to transform how work gets done, it also introduces complex challenges related to enterprise data integration, contextual relevance, governance, security, cost efficiency, and operational reliability. EXLerate.ai addresses these challenges through a modular platform architecture, enterprise data and knowledge grounding, flexible model orchestration, and built-in guardrails designed to support responsible, scalable, and production-ready AI systems.

title: Design and Optimization of GPU-Aware MPI Allreduce Using Direct Sendrecv Communication
abstract: Explore new techniques for accelerating GPU-aware MPI Allreduce using direct send/receive communication with throttling. Our design improves scalability and overlaps computation with communication, achieving up to 7% faster nanoGPT training while maintaining strong performance across diverse workloads.

speakers.name: Dhabaleswar Panda
speakers.jobTitle: Professor and Distinguished Scholar of Computer Science

title: Designing and Operating an Energy Efficient AI Factory
abstract: As AI factories become mission‑critical infrastructure, organizations must design, build, operate, and maintain electro‑intensive environments that are intelligent and scalable. This session shows how Schneider Electric and NVIDIA, along with open standards including SysML and OpenUSD enable the full AI Factory lifecycle through a unified approach powered by accelerated computing, high‑fidelity simulation, and agentic AI.

speakers.name: Natasha Nelson
speakers.jobTitle: CTO of Services and VP of EcoStruxure Power

title: Designing Modular Infrastructure for the AI Factory Era (Presented by Intelliflex)
abstract:
AI workloads are scaling faster than traditional data center models were designed to support, particularly as power density requirements increase and expansion timelines compress.

In this session, we explore the infrastructure requirements of AI factories—including power density, modular deployment approaches, and direct liquid cooling readiness—and examine architectural strategies that enable phased scalability while avoiding the constraints of retrofitting legacy, low-density facilities for accelerated computing at scale.

title: Desk-to-Scale: Engineering Sovereign Financial Agents With Open Tools and Edge AI (Presented by HP)
abstract: Financial institutions face a dilemma: build complex AI agents using the latest innovation, but without risking sensitive data leaving their secure perimeter. We will demonstrate how to bridge this gap by combining high-performance edge AI with a frictionless developer workflow leveraging open-source tooling. We explore how teams from Red Hat and Columbia University's Secure FinAI Lab utilized the HP ZGX Nano and the ZGX Toolkit to transform a bare-metal AI station into a fully governed "AI factory" in under 30 minutes—automating the setup and supporting the rapid development of innovative solutions to real-world financial problems. See how the ZGX Toolkit enables a seamless "desk-to-scale" workflow, where an Anti-Money Laundering (AML) detective agent achieves an 80% success in identifying financial crime topologies, is built, tested, and audited locally before being deployed to the cloud.

speakers.name: Andrea Schiliro'
speakers.jobTitle: Solution Product Marketing Manager, New Business Incubation Advanced Compute and Solutions

title: Detect Windshield Damage Using Physically Realistic Synthetic Data
abstract: Explore a practical pipeline that uses Omniverse synthetic data and Cosmos-Transfer to measure windshield damage with pixel-level masks and produce clear repair versus replace calls. You gain tools to iterate faster, boost model quality without real data, and harden claims with trusted, auditable outputs.

title: Develop a Real-Time Digital Twin for Rapid Automotive Engineering Design
abstract: Learn how Rivian built a real-time digital twin for rapid engineering design through NVIDIA Omniverse, Apollo AI Physics models, and CUDA-X libraries.

speakers.name: Andy McAllister
speakers.jobTitle: Sr. Manager of Aerodynamics and Environmental Performance

title: Develop AI-Powered Operational Dashboards for Digital Twin Applications
abstract:
Create intelligent operational dashboards for digital twin applications using NVIDIA Omniverse. Build compact, real-time monitoring interfaces with React to track factory metrics, robot states, and sensor data streams. Embed custom panels into Kit applications and deploy dashboards across local and cloud environments. Learn to integrate LLM agents for natural language interaction with your digital twin data. Through hands-on exercises, learn the workflow for dashboard creation for streaming deployment, enabling intelligent monitoring and control of complex industrial operations.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Familiarity with REST APIs, data streaming, and real-time visualization concepts
  • Basic understanding of factory operations, IoT metrics, and monitoring workflows
  • Experience with Python scripting for backend integration and automation
  • Knowledge of LLM concepts and natural language processing

title: Develop Physical AI Applications and Build Data Factories With the Robotics Ecosystem (Presented by Foxconn)
abstract: In this session, we will share how Foxconn leverages its own advantages in the era of Physical AI, together with Project Genesis, a group-level centralized AI platform initiative, to cooperate with global ecological partners such as NVIDIA, BCG, ABB, Intrinsic, Siemens, etc., to build “AI Factory” and “Data Factory,” and dive into the application of various automation and robots in production lines that integrate the concept of Physical AI technology to jointly promote the transformation of Foxconn manufacturing into “AI intensive.”

title: Develop Production Agents with Eval-Driven Design
abstract:
This training lab will explore new techniques for building robust, production-ready AI agents by focusing on an evaluation-driven design workflow. You'll learn how we use human-created datasets for iterative improvement and how to apply continuous eval and iterate cycles, including prompt changes and experimentation, to significantly improve agent performance.

Prerequisite(s):

  • The prerequisites for this course are practical familiarity with AI/ML fundamentals, including Large Language Models (LLMs), and programming proficiency in Python.

  • Attendees should also have a working understanding of basic prompt engineering and experience with the overall lifecycle of building an AI application, as the lab is focused on the advanced, production-ready stage of evaluation and iterative design.

title: Develop World Foundation Models With NVIDIA Cosmos
abstract:
Learn how world foundation models generate physics-based data to advance physical AI development. Explore open tools for video processing, tokenization that accelerates training, and frameworks to customize models for autonomous vehicles, robotics, and humanoids. Get help from experts to optimize your physical AI development pipelines with NVIDIA Cosmos.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod C (L2)

title: Developing AI 2035: The Next Decade of Intelligence
abstract: As AI evolves into the engine of global innovation, its future will define how we work, create, and solve humanity’s greatest challenges. This luminary panel brings together AI leaders to chart the decade ahead—where breakthroughs in large-scale compute, foundation models, and intelligent systems converge to expand human potential. Building on a decade of NVIDIA DGX innovation, panelists will explore how next-generation AI balances power and sustainability, fosters discovery, and advances responsible progress for all.

title: Developing AI Physics Models for Spacecraft Reentry
abstract: Learn how Blue Origin has been fine-tuning and using the NVIDIA AI physics models for their hypersonic spacecraft re-entry challenges. You’ll hear about how they use simulation within their research and design processes; how they see the role of AI within their overall workflows and work done to fine-tune and optimize AI Physics models for their applications.

title: Developing Generative AI Models for Market Activity
abstract: Nasdaq's cutting-edge generative AI platform creates ultra-realistic synthetic market order books and order flow data using advanced deep learning architectures. By leveraging sequential generative models and transformer-based networks, it produces statistically accurate limit order book simulations that preserve the statistical properties and microstructure dynamics of real financial markets. The platform generates high-fidelity synthetic data indistinguishable from actual market conditions, while providing sophisticated vector embeddings for enhanced search and retrieval applications.

speakers.name: Douglas Hamilton
speakers.jobTitle: VP - Head of AI Research and Engineering

speakers.name: Michael O'Rourke
speakers.jobTitle: SVP - Head of AI & Emerging Technology

title: Digital Twins at Scale: The New Retail Growth Infrastructure
abstract: Retail brands are under pressure to produce high-quality content at unprecedented speed and scale without increasing costs, and this session demonstrates how AI-powered digital twin technology built with NVIDIA Omniverse addresses that challenge. By transforming physical products into hyper-realistic, physics-accurate virtual assets, retailers can instantly deploy them across marketing, advertising, and e-commerce. Using robotic capture systems pre-trained with NVIDIA Isaac Sim and AI lighting agents trained with NVIDIA Kaolin, products are converted into production-grade digital twins with optimized illumination and material realism. Real-world case studies illustrate how workflows built with Omniverse enable mass content creation, synthetic data generation, and real-time rendering—replacing traditional photoshoots with scalable, AI-driven pipelines that deliver measurable improvements in speed, efficiency, and performance.

title: Disaggregated Inference Experiments With NVIDIA Dynamo for Telecoms Reasoning, AI Agent Use Cases
abstract:
We explore how disaggregated inference optimizes GPU resource utilization in real-world reasoning and AI agent use cases within telco environments. We also analyze the workload characteristics of Wikipedia-based AI agents, which increase prefill inputs. To handle such large inputs, we experimented with the NVIDIA Dynamo framework on NVIDIA H100 GPUs in a disaggregated setup. Our results show an approximate 16% overhead in time-to-first-token and confirm superior stability under high loads, compared to aggregated inference.

From a telecom perspective, the disaggregated inference could represent a significant breakthrough to enable flexible, on-demand GPU allocation across multi–data center architectures and supports scalable AI agent deployment. Future work will focus on developing cost models for geo-distributed GPU resources to ensure efficient resource planning for our customers.

speakers.name: Kota Tsuyuzaki
speakers.company: NTTドコモビジネス株式会社
speakers.jobTitle: イノベーションセンター テクノロジー部門 担当課長 エンジニアリングマネージャー

speakers.name: Sotetsu Suzugamine
speakers.jobTitle: イノベーションセンター テクノロジー部門 Software Engineer

title: Discover Top In-Demand Learning Paths Across Europe, the Middle East, and Africa
abstract:
We invite you to join us as we explore NVIDIA's Deep Learning Institute Training Catalogue, uncovering practical use cases and real-world applications that extend beyond standard course descriptions.

In this session, our NVIDIA experts will guide you through some of our most popular and in-demand learning paths, demonstrating how they can deepen your AI knowledge, strengthen your skills, and support your career development.

title: Distillation Recipes and Synthetic Data Fine-Tuning for Algorithmic Trading
abstract: Explore how model distillation and compression techniques are redefining the role of large language models in quantitative finance. In this expert talk, we’ll examine strategies for reducing cost, latency, and complexity while preserving accuracy for real-time alpha generation and risk prediction. You'll gain insights into the essential techniques behind efficient model design and discover how these methods drive scalable, high-performance deployment on NVIDIA’s accelerated computing platform.

title: Distributed AI Computing From AI Factories to the Edge (Presented by Supermicro)
abstract: Enterprises are moving from AI experimentation to production, where return on investment (ROI) matters as much as performance. This session, presented by Supermicro and NVIDIA, explores how AI factories, edge AI, and on-premises deployments work together to deliver scalable, cost-effective AI solutions. We'll highlight how systems powered by GPUs such as the NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition support inference, visualization, and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) workloads across data centers and edge environments. We'll also examine how RAG pipelines benefit from tightly integrated GPUs, networking, and storage to deliver low-latency, context-aware results—helping enterprises improve data control, reduce operational costs, and maximize ROI from AI investments.

title: Distributed Inference at Scale Using Dynamo
abstract:
NVIDIA Dynamo is a distributed inference serving framework built to run LLMs efficiently, reliably, and at massive scale. It helps AI providers reduce cost-per-token, meet strict service level objectives, and scale deployments using Kubernetes across large GPU clusters. Learn how Dynamo delivers high-performance distributed inference across frameworks such as SGLang, TensorRT-LLM, and vLLM. We’ll explore key capabilities — including disaggregated serving, KV-cache-aware routing, topology-aware scheduling, and intelligent memory and cache management — that make Dynamo a powerful foundation for production workloads. Whether you’re evaluating Dynamo, experimenting it, or deepening your understanding of its capabilities and real-world impact, join us to ask questions, see how the system works under the hood, and get practical guidance from the experts who build and deploy it.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod A (L2)
Connect With the Experts Pod A (L2)

title: DiT 视频模型推理加速新方案：兼顾精度与效率的平衡之道
abstract: 我们将深入探讨三种面向 DiT 视频模型的全新解决方案，解决 training-free 加速技术中存在的精度损失问题。随着高效、高质量的视频生成在降本增效和提升用户体验方面变得愈发迫切，本次分享希望能提供一些具有参考价值的思路与经验。

speakers.name: 康 何
speakers.jobTitle: 可灵AI Infra 推理方向负责人

title: Don’t Leave Tensors on the Table: Programming and Optimizing Tensor Cores
abstract: Explore practical techniques for maximizing Tensor Core performance in your CUDA kernels. We’ll discuss CUTLASS-based coding patterns to unlock efficient pipeline utilization and streamlined data management. Together, we’ll use Nsight Compute to analyze, optimize, and fine-tune your workflows for optimal Tensor Core performance.

title: Drive Optimal Tokens per Watt on any AI Infrastructure Using Benchmarking Recipes
abstract: Join our session to learn how benchmarking recipes can evaluate performance and inform infrastructure tuning to optimize tokens/watt. Learn about the intersection of performance and total cost of ownership (TCO) through NVIDIA's performance tools, and understand the NVIDIA Exemplar Cloud initiative.

title: Driving Factory Autonomy With Digital Twins: Hyundai’s Virtual Commissioning Journey
abstract:
Hyundai Motor Group is advancing factory autonomy by leveraging NVIDIA Omniverse to build scalable, high-fidelity digital twins across its manufacturing operations.

In this session, we share Hyundai’s real-world journey of implementing Omniverse-powered factory digital twins, with a focus on virtual commissioning as a critical enabler for reducing deployment risk, accelerating system integration, and improving factory startup efficiency. Gain insights into how Hyundai virtualizes diverse manufacturing workloads—ranging from robotics and automation systems to production line logic—within a shared, collaborative digital environment. We will also highlight how cross-functional collaboration between engineering, IT, and operations teams is transformed through a unified digital twin platform, enabling faster decision-making and continuous innovation.

speakers.name: Jong-ho Shin
speakers.jobTitle: Senior Manager, Manufacturing Solution Division, E-FOREST Center

title: Driving National Growth Through Sovereign AI Investment
abstract:
Around the world, nations are accelerating their efforts to build AI capabilities that drive long‑term economic competitiveness. In this fireside chat, Marcus Wallenberg—a global business leader with deep experience in innovative models—joins NVIDIA to explore how countries can design, fund, and operate AI infrastructure as a strategic national asset.

Together, the conversation will unpack how sovereign AI platforms function as economic backbones, the policy frameworks that unlock large‑scale private capital, and the practical playbooks leaders can use to establish durable AI ecosystems. Drawing on real‑world examples across finance, industry, and national innovation systems, the session will provide actionable guidance for governments, investors, and organizations seeking to accelerate AI adoption and national productivity.

speakers.name: Marcus Wallenberg
speakers.jobTitle: Chair of the Board of Directors

title: Dual Engines of Thought: A Dual-Engine Architecture With Breadth–Depth Coordination for Open-Ended Reasoning in LLMs
abstract: Dual engines of thought (DEoT) — an adaptive reasoning framework that dynamically orchestrates breadth and depth reasoning modules to enable stable, efficient, and context-sensitive inference in large language models. DEoT employs an entropy-regulated controller to judiciously navigate the reasoning space, mitigating both informational sparsity and computational overhead. This dynamic coordination facilitates robust multi-source integration and enhances the model's capacity for deep causal inference. Empirical results on the open-ended News-to-Question benchmark reveal that DEoT significantly outperforms standard LLMs, delivering responses that are not only more analytically nuanced, but also more logically coherent. Our architecture demonstrates broad applicability across high-impact domains, including biomedicine, economics, geopolitics, industrial systems, and advanced technologies.

speakers.name: Teng-Ruei Chen
speakers.company: Taiwan Intelligence Research and Development Co., Ltd.

title: Dynamic Rate Limiting for Multi-Tenant GPU Inference in AI Factories
abstract: This poster shows how dynamic, priority‑aware rate limiting turns AI factories into efficient, shared GPU inference plants that keep critical workloads fast while boosting overall utilization.

title: Edge Computing 101: Introduction to Smart Edge and Autonomous Robots
abstract: Discover the transformative potential of NVIDIA’s edge AI platform in shaping the future of smart edge and autonomous robotics. This session will provide an overview of the platform’s capabilities across embedded, industrial, and enterprise edge environments, with a focus on how general intelligence at the edge enables machines to perceive, reason, and make real-time decisions, directly where data is generated. Learn about the best practices and software frameworks that power AI-driven robots and intelligent vision systems—from advanced multi-modal sensor processing to low-latency, on-device inference—unlocking greater autonomy without reliance on the cloud. Explore how these innovations are accelerating the rise of generative physical AI systems and reshaping industries including healthcare, retail, robotics, transportation, manufacturing, and beyond.

speakers.name: Chen Su
speakers.jobTitle: Head of Edge AI Product Marketing

title: Efficient Iterative QPE Simulations for Quantum Chemistry Using Distributed State-Vector Methods
abstract: We demonstrate Iterative quantum phase estimation (QPE) simulations for quantum chemistry up to 31 qubits on 64 GPUs using chemqulacs-gpu, our distributed simulator with NVIDIA cuQuantum backend. Through memory-efficient algorithms and optimized communication strategies, we achieve strong scalability for evaluating quantum algorithms toward fault-tolerant quantum computing.

title: Efficient On-Policy Distillation for Transferring Reasoning Capabilities ​via Parallel and Sparse Training​
abstract: We present an efficient on-policy distillation framework enhanced by the novel Zero-trick, which mitigates logit alignment gaps in sparse distillation. Our work fills the gap of open-source on-policy distillation tools and drastically reduces computational overhead compared with full-logit distillation, making advanced reasoning capabilities more accessible for practical small-scale models.

title: Efficient SubtleHD-Enhanced MR Images for Improved Dementia Classification
abstract: Our poster demonstrates new solutions for robust, accelerated dementia classification by training a 3D ResNet34 model on U.S. Food and Drug Administration-cleared SubtleHD enhanced MR images, and explores how to improve performance by accelerating the ConvNeXt enhancement pipeline using NVIDIA TensorRT on various GPUs. This work is timely because it provides a validated, high-efficiency pipeline that resolves the critical trade-off between rapid MR scanning and reliable AI-guided diagnosis in neurology.

title: Efficient Workload Management for AI Factories: What Every Admin Needs to Know
abstract:
In this hands-on lab you'll learn, as an AI factory administrator, how real workloads are run on a cluster. Intended for IT types (not data scientists!), this lab will have you configuring Run:ai, allocating and monitoring resources, and building a workload with NVIDIA NIMS to understand just what the user experience is, and what tools are at your disposal to make the AI factory workloads actually work.

Prerequisite(s):

  • To be successful, participants should have an understanding of AI clusters, Linux, and containers.
  • Bring a laptop as this session is very interactive!

speakers.name: Max Steele
speakers.jobTitle: Sr. Technical Instructor in AI and Data Center

title: Elevating Business Jet Customer Experience With an Enhanced Rendering Workflow
abstract: We'll walk through how we built our graphics pipeline in Omniverse, an upgrade that slashed rendering times by nearly a factor of 100 and cut our production cycles in half. This improvement now lets us deliver photorealistic videos alongside traditional renderings, significantly enhancing what we can offer our customers. We’ll delve into our customer workflow and the custom tooling we’ve developed around Omniverse to maximize efficiency.

title: Eliminate the Data Bottleneck: Accelerate Enterprise AI at Scale (Presented by Elastic)
abstract: Successful enterprise AI deployments require context from your petabytes of unstructured, proprietary company data. Yet indexing and vectorizing this data at enterprise speed and scale remains one of the biggest bottlenecks to implementing AI in production, resulting in stalled deployments and high overhead costs. Elastic’s GPU-accelerated search with NVIDIA cuVS now delivers 12x faster indexing speeds and enables enterprise-scale AI deployments that accelerate your path from proof of concept to production.

title: Emotion-Aware Voice Agent
abstract: Our goal is to develop emotion-aware voice agents capable of understanding and responding with appropriate emotion. Traditional agents, relying solely on text-based sentiment analysis, miss critical emotional cues conveyed through speech. Our research integrates multi-modal sentiment analysis, processing both the semantic content (text) and the prosodic features (speech) to create a holistic emotional understanding. This allows for more natural, empathetic, and engaging human-computer interactions.

title: Empower US Manufacturing Through Wistron's Smart Manufacturing (Presented by Wistron Corp.)
abstract: Join Wistron technology leaders to explore how the company’s digital twin platform, built on NVIDIA Omniverse libraries and OpenUSD, is accelerating factory deployment, from Hsinchu to Dallas. Discover how these factory-scale digital twins are enabling many use cases; including streamlined factory design, layout validation, and manufacturing process simulation in the early planning. Get a deep dive into how these digital twins are enabling virtual commissioning and helping identify integration issues among robots, conveyors, and automation systems before physical installation. Learn about the real business outcomes being generated across Wistron’s operations, from faster iteration cycles to fewer deployment risks and eliminated rework during ramp-up.

title: Enable Enterprise AI Factories With Secure, High-Performance Networking
abstract: AI continues to reshape enterprise applications, boosting productivity, streamlining operations, and delivering better outcomes. To realize these gains, enterprises need infrastructure that can keep pace with modern AI. Traditional infrastructure has hit its limits, diverting compute and power from AI workloads and creating performance bottlenecks and security gaps at AI scale. Learn how Red Hat OpenShift, NVIDIA BlueField DPUs, and high-performance Ethernet fabrics provide optimal infrastructure for enterprise AI. DPU-based isolation separates infrastructure services from user workloads, reducing the attack surface and enforcing policy boundaries. We'll also cover how OpenShift automates provisioning, updates, monitoring, and troubleshooting for BlueField-enabled nodes to simplify adoption at enterprise scale.

title: Enable Seamless Human-Humanoid Collaboration in a Shared Operational Environment (Presented by EY)
abstract: Learn how Fincantieri, one of the world’s largest shipbuilders, designed and implemented humanoid robots engineered to perform complex tasks in dangerous shipyard environments characterized by high variability and stringent safety requirements. Leveraging NVIDIA’s simulation and AI orchestration solutions, EY helped Fincantieri accelerate workflow automation and reduce manual interventions. Dive into Fincantieri’s strategic vision and understand the challenges for integrating humanoids into an industrial environment.

title: End-to-End FP8 Training for Large Language Models: Higher Accuracy at Lower Cost
abstract:
Explore a plug-and-play FP8 training recipe that lets you train reasoning LLMs faster and with less memory—while matching BF16 accuracy—so you can scale context, batches, and experiments on the same budget. See how you can adopt it across pre-training, fine-tuning, and reinforcement learning (RL) to unlock more results per GPU hour. 

Our end-to-end FP8 pipeline improves speed and memory use without changing your model or schedule. Learn how to apply it today to do more with fixed compute budgets and deliver stronger downstream results.
