We'll also share an FP8-first workflow that maintains accuracy while cutting compute and memory overhead. You get practical steps to drop it into your pre-training and post-training stacks.

Dive deep into an inclusive, end-to-end FP8 approach that turns training efficiency into real task gains.

title: Engineering Medical-Grade Platforms for Real-Time AI
abstract: Join us to learn how to turn NVIDIA IGX and Holoscan into a reusable, medical-grade platform for real-time AI. We'll walk through the complete stack, from system architecture and safety concepts to Holoscan graph design and containerized SaMD deployment, with a focus on the decisions, trade-offs, and patterns you can apply to your own devices. The session emphasizes how to meet strict latency and reliability constraints in a pass-through video pipeline, how to design a common regulated platform that can host multiple AI applications, and how to enable external teams to build and ship models on top of that platform.

title: Engineering Real-World LLM Inference: Bridging Open-Source and Production Systems
abstract: In production, LLM inference performance is shaped by model architecture, hardware, workload patterns, and service-level agreement constraints. This session presents a practical workflow for profiling inference stacks, identifying real bottlenecks, and evaluating optimizations across kernels, scheduling, and system design, showing how to leverage fast-moving open-source innovations while applying our own enhancements to achieve production-grade performance and stability, and how to feed lessons from production back into upstream development.

title: Enhance Data Center Efficiency and Throughput by Co-Scheduling on NVIDIA CPU+GPU Systems
abstract: Coherent NVIDIA CPU-GPU systems provide a versatile platform to support a wide spectrum of workloads. However not all jobs require all capabilities at scale. Co-scheduling CPU-only, partially accelerated, and fully GPU-accelerated jobs on NVIDIA CPU-GPU nodes can boost throughput and energy efficiency, removing the need for multiple dedicated clusters. We discuss isolation mechanisms including CPU affinity via cgroups, cache and memory bandwidth partitioning through ARM’s MPAM extensions, and InfiniBand QoS for network traffic separation. Learn to identify co-schedule candidates and assess scheduling policy design options and incentives for user adoption. We'll present workload studies aligned with NERSC’s upcoming Doudna system, demonstrating projected improvements in utilization and efficiency while highlighting open challenges in enabling fine-grained resource sharing.

title: Enhance Production of Synthetic Radar Images From Geostationary Satellite Observations With Generative Diffusion Models
abstract: Explore how generative diffusion models can transform geostationary satellite and lightning observations into realistic, high-resolution radar reflectivity imagery. This work introduces a probabilistic approach that improves key metrics and reproduces fine-scale storm structures, helping extend radar-like coverage to regions without operational radar systems.

title: Enterprise AI at Speed: Simplifying AI Factory Deployments at Scale
abstract: Learn how NVIDIA Enterprise AI Factory powers a versatile, full-stack ecosystem, where specialist platforms provide the backbone for running inference and agents in production. We’ll share patterns for deploying AI at scale—when to build vs. buy, how to work with software partners, and how to keep efforts tied to real ROI instead of science projects.

title: European Robotics Startup Showcase
abstract: Learn how European startups are leveraging NVIDIA platforms to advance robotics and automation across real-world industries. From teaching robotic hands dexterity with scalable world models and automating smart agriculture using edge AI to reinventing hospitality operations through simulation-based design and applying adaptive robotics to healthcare and rehabilitation—discover how these innovators integrate foundation models, teleoperation, and digital twins to unlock new possibilities for autonomous systems in manufacturing, agriculture, services, and beyond.

title: Europe’s AI Launchpad: Unlock Startup Growth Through Sovereign AI Infrastructure
abstract: This session will explore Europe’s dynamic AI compute landscape and guide founders through leveraging both publicly funded AI factories—designed for open research—and private NCP/AI factories focused on commercial activity, using the concept of a unified digital continuum for strategic growth. Discover actionable pathways to access sovereign AI infrastructure, including a comprehensive overview of services from major players and how startups can engage with high-speed, secure NVIDIA-powered systems through tailored programs and partnerships developed for European innovators. We'll also highlight how new regional AI growth zones and an expanding network of AI factories offer localized access to advanced technology, talent, and venture funding—enabling startups to build competitive advantages rooted in European digital sovereignty and regional collaboration.

speakers.name: Pierre-Antoine Beaudoin
speakers.jobTitle: Regional Lead, Startup Ecosystem, Southern Europe

title: Evaluation-Driven Development: Best Practices for Building Reliable Agents
abstract:
Building agents is easy; reliably improving them is hard. As workflows shift to complex long-horizon tasks, traditional testing fails. How do you debug and improve non-deterministic behavior?

This session presents a framework for evaluation-driven development. We move beyond “vibe eval” to disciplined engineering covering ablations, unit tests, trajectory evaluation, and LLM-as-judge patterns. Using a case study of achieving No. 1 on a Deep Research leaderboard, we demonstrate how to build pipelines that catch hallucinations and regressions while preventing overfitting. You'll leave with a workflow for implementing evaluation methods that take agents from prototype to scalable deployment using a suite of tooling enabled by NVIDIA NeMo and open NVIDIA Nemotron models for advanced reasoning.

title: Everything You Want to Ask About NVIDIA Enterprise Reference Architectures
abstract: Engage directly with NVIDIA specialists to discuss the NVIDIA Enterprise Reference Architecture program in this informal "Connect With Experts" session. This is a unique opportunity to ask questions, share insights, and gain a deeper understanding of how these reference architectures and accompanying deployment guides for Networking, Infrastructure Software, Observability, and Sizing help our global systems partners to streamline enterprise AI deployments. This session is ideal for IT professionals, AI system architects, and anyone interested in leveraging NVIDIA's cutting-edge technologies to deploy AI in the enterprise. Don’t miss this chance to connect with experts and deepen your understanding of NVIDIA's innovative enterprise AI solutions!

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod A (L2)

title: Everything You Wanted to Know About AI Factories!
abstract:
Learn what goes into an AI Factory - Hardware, software, networking, storage ... nothing is off limits. Want to understand how NVIDIA RTX PRO servers can be used with NVIDIA Spectrum-X networking and Run:ai? Have questions about which AI factory is right for your use cases?  Want to know how to stand a AI factory quickly and make sure it is scalable into the future?  This is the place to be.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Scott Ellis
speakers.jobTitle: Sr. Director, Solutions Architecture and Engineering

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod D (L2)
Connect With the Experts Pod D (L2)

title: Extend Robot Perception With Industrial Safety Agents
abstract:
Autonomous forklifts and mobile robots are transforming loading docks and warehouse aisles, but relying only on onboard sensing leaves them vulnerable to blind spots, occlusions, and conservative safety behaviors that increase cost. KION will show how they use NVIDIA Halos for outside-in safety on NVIDIA IGX, combining infrastructure cameras, low-latency detections, and safety logic to supervise multiple robots with virtual fences, tripwires, and dynamic zones. 

Learn how outside-in safety architecture boosts throughput, reduces false stops, and enables closer human–robot collaboration for use cases such as autonomous forklift trailer loading and autonomous mobile robot (AMR) zoning with Metropolis VSS and Cosmos Reason.

title: Fast and Accurate Virtual Drug Screening Powered by GPU-Annealer and CUDA-Q
abstract: This work presents a fast and accurate GPU-accelerated virtual drug screening pipeline. In the first stage, a GPU-annealer was built to simulate a quantum annealer and used for molecular docking, achieving faster performance with competitive AUC and EF1. In the second stage, CUDA-Q VQE refined binding energies for reliable ranking and better EF1. During the entire pipeline, GPU delivers up to four orders of magnitude speedup over CPU execution, accelerating early drug discovery.

title: Fast High-Fidelity Urban Airflow Emulator With Explainable Optimized Sensor Placement
abstract: Explore how we enable real-time, near-DNS-quality reconstruction of urban airflow using only sparse, strategically optimized sensors, along with interpretable sensor layouts that cities can actually deploy. Our approach offers new tools for understanding how wind moves pollutants and heat through dense urban spaces, helping communities make faster and better-informed decisions. This work is especially timely as the World Health Organization reports that air pollution contributes to 7 million premature deaths every year, underscoring the urgent need for accurate, scalable monitoring solutions that support healthier and more resilient cities.

title: Faster Together: Train and Deploy a Speculative Decoding Model for Low-Latency LLM Inference
abstract:
Dive deep into theory and practice of low-latency inference by deploying NVIDIA TensorRT-LLM with advanced speculative decoding techniques. You'll train an Eagle-3 draft head to propose candidate tokens efficiently, serve it, and benchmark it using AIPerf to quantify how these strategies minimize latency.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Familiarity with bash and Python.
  • Familiarity with the concept of token.

title: Find the Bottleneck: Optimize AI Pipelines With Nsight Systems
abstract:
Learn about using NVIDIA Nsight Systems for bottleneck detection in AI pipelines and explore new features to optimize and scale the execution of your applications. This lab will guide you through the performance analysis process of GPU-accelerated AI applications that run across multiple, possibly containerized, compute instances. In addition to profiling of CPU, GPU, network and I/O activity, we'll show how NVTX and plugin mechanisms enable the collection of custom data to support an even more comprehensive and tailored analysis on the target platform. We'll also show how the recipe system can be used to identify bottlenecks in applications running across multiple nodes. This lab is for both beginners and experienced developers who want to learn about the latest features, tips, and tricks for performance analysis to get the most out of their hardware.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Familiarity with GPUs and CUDA
  • Up to date Chrome or IE browser

title: Fine-Tune a Telco Reasoning Model: A Guide to Synthetic Data, Tool Calling, and Evaluation
abstract:
Network operation centers (NoC) are the central nervous system of telecommunications, but they are often overwhelmed by "alarm storms." In a traditional setup, engineers manually validate alarms, swivel between multiple dashboards, check topologies, and perform root-cause analysis. This manual process is time-consuming and prone to fatigue. To solve this, we are moving toward zero-touch, self-healing networks. In this tutorial, we'll walk through creating a fine-tuning playbook that integrates synthetic data generation, training, and evaluation pipelines. We'll demonstrate how to build an AI-driven reasoning model capable of autonomously performing NoC engineer workflows — detecting issues, calling tools, and remediating incidents without human intervention.

Prerequisite(s):

  •  Familiarity with Python, LLM fine-tuning concepts, and basic containerization.

title: Five Bottlenecks, Five Fixes: How We Avoid Leaving Training Performance on the Table
abstract: GPU clusters are fast, until they're not. We'll walk through five performance pitfalls from real researcher code, the fixes that made them disappear, and how to use technologies like CUDA streams, CUDA graphs, and custom kernels along the way.

title: Foundation Models Revolutionize Molecular Dynamics Simulations for Chemistry and Materials Science
abstract: Molecular dynamics simulation, using the explicit equations of quantum mechanics (e.g., density functional theory) is the workhorse of computational materials science and chemistry. Until recently, more than a third of academic supercomputer time was spent on this task. We can now speed up this task by a factor of a million or more using machine learning, without substantial loss of accuracy. This amount of speedup leads to a qualitative change in what is possible to simulate, and therefore to understand, in a wide range of application fields, all the way from catalysis and battery materials to glasses and alloys, and even drug discovery. While some, by now classic, rules of thumb continue to deliver (e.g., more data yield better accuracy), the problem may be special in some ways. We find that encoding the known laws of physics, such as symmetry and locality, help generalization.

title: Fraud Prevention in the Age of Agentic Commerce
abstract: Join a Stripe engineering lead for an inside look at how Stripe’s AI‑powered fraud prevention solution, Radar, is built with NVIDIA hardware and software to detect and block fraudulent transactions at massive scale, including emerging risks and new fraud vectors from agentic commerce to free trial abuse prevention. Trained on patterns derived from more than $1.4 trillion in annual payment volume, Radar uses advanced ML techniques to surface high‑confidence signals that reduced fraud rates for its users by 17% year-over-year.

title: From AI Factories to the Edge: Architecting Distributed Inference to Unlock Enterprise ROI (Presented by Akamai)
abstract: AI inference and agents are forcing infrastructure to evolve. As interaction shifts from human to machine-centric, unlocking latency, cost, and performance advantages will be critical. AI Factories excel at foundational model training and large-scale concurrent GPU workloads. However, production inference—especially multi-modal workloads and agentic workflows requiring low-latency responses, multi-step reasoning, and real-time tool calls—demands a different approach. This session demonstrates how distributed inference architectures optimize GPU utilization for responsiveness to maximize ROI by keeping training centralized while deploying fine-tuning, post-training, and production inference on high-performance global networks.

title: From Challenge to Competitive Advantage: How VC Portfolio Companies Leverage NVIDIA to Win Markets
abstract: AI venture funding reached $100.8 billion in 2024—37% of all venture capital (VC) investment. Yet, most promising AI startups don’t fail due to weak markets or teams, but from challenges bridging research and production: scaling infrastructure, solving domain-specific problems, and building defensible technical moats. The focus is now on “doing AI right” for real impact. Top VCs now treat NVIDIA partnership as essential portfolio infrastructure—not just for compute, but as a strategic accelerator that helps founders solve hard problems, lead technically, and speed up R&D. This session shares how startups used NVIDIA’s ecosystem to achieve breakthrough results.

title: From Cluster to Factory: Scale AI Infrastructure Operations With Software-Defined Intelligence
abstract: Be among the first to hear how software-defined intelligence is aiding enterprises to scale AI operations. Hear directly from an AI innovator as they reveal key learnings from deploying NVIDIA DGX, sharing firsthand insights into architecting and deploying high-performance AI infrastructure and orchestrating complex inference and training workflows. In this session, we’ll also discuss the critical role of software for NVIDIA Rubin and how to simplify complex liquid-cooled deployments at scale, boost site reliability engineering (SRE) productivity, and enable enhanced AI-powered anomaly detection and remediation across the entire AI factory.

speakers.name: Jon Klinginsmith
speakers.jobTitle: Executive Director of Cloud Engineering and High Performance Computing

speakers.name: Bijal Shah
speakers.jobTitle: Director of Product Management, Data Center Enterprise Products

title: From Code to Capital: Empowering Women Tech Founders
abstract: This session explores proven strategies, real-world challenges, and ecosystem shifts shaping the journey from building breakthrough technologies to securing critical funding for women tech founders. Despite receiving only a fraction of global venture capital, women-led startups are achieving remarkable growth and impact—particularly in deep tech and AI sectors. Join a dynamic conversation with investors, entrepreneurs, and advocates to uncover pathways for women in tech to access capital, expand networks, and drive innovation, ensuring talent and ambition translate into tangible business success.

speakers.name: Jessica Driscoll
speakers.jobTitle: Regional Lead, Startup Ecosystem, UK and Ireland

title: From Complexity to Confidence: Core-to-Edge Secure AI Factories with Consistent Operations (Presented by Cisco Systems)
abstract: While AI practitioners push rapid innovation, IT teams struggle with fragmented guidance and operational overhead when deploying secure, high-performance AI infrastructure across data center and edge to meet training and inference needs. Discover how prescriptive, validated reference designs such as Cisco Secure AI Factory with NVIDIA simplifies deployment and operations of trusted AI infrastructure with the same capabilities and operating model across data center and edge. Get actionable patterns to accelerate deployment and focus on innovation.

speakers.name: Kevin Wollenweber
speakers.jobTitle: SVP and GM of Data Center and Internet Infrastructure

title: From Concept to Capability: Scaling AI Surrogates Across JLR
abstract: As Jaguar Land Rover (JLR) continues its digital transformation journey, AI surrogates are unlocking new frontiers in computer-aided engineering (CAE). This session explores how the Aerodynamics team pioneered AI as an essential engineering “pilot,” proving its value in one of the most complex and high-impact CAE domains. Through robust data workflows and scalable deployment strategies, this approach has enabled faster, cleaner, and more reliable delivery of AI-driven insights. Discover how this foundation is now being expanded across broader CAE disciplines, transforming modeling efficiency, data integrity, and decision-making throughout the organization.

title: From Data to Decisions: Accelerate Supply Chain Planning With Agentic AI (Talk to Your Supply Chain)
abstract: This poster presents an agentic AI system that enables supply chain planners to ask complex operational questions in natural language and receive accurate, validated answers sourced directly from enterprise datasets. By combining entity normalization, Text2SQL generation, and a reflection-based self-correction loop, the system eliminates manual data stitching across SAP/HANA/Databricks tables and significantly reduces time-to-insight. Deployed across NVIDIA’s Operations Data Platform, the solution has already delivered measurable value with over 3,800 invocations and 1,900-plus planner-hours saved in just three months, demonstrating strong business adoption and scalability. The architecture is designed for extensibility, enabling future multi-retriever reasoning across bill of materials (BOM), logistics, supplier risk, and material readiness workflows.

title: From Data to Decisions: Enabling AI Agents With Business Knowledge
abstract: The next wave of business innovation is powered by AI developer platforms that grant agents deep access to your enterprise knowledge. In this session, discover practical strategies for leveraging these platforms to connect information from across business systems, automate workflows, and support smarter decision-making. Live demonstrations will highlight how you can use AI-powered tools to streamline processes, enhance compliance, and guide innovation in your own organization. You’ll leave with frameworks and real-world examples to help scale knowledge, efficiency, and impact—no matter your role or industry.

title: From Data to Deployment: Engineering Sovereign Language Models for a Changing World
abstract:
Get a glimpse into the day-to-day life of a sovereign LLM model maker. Learn what drives them—specifically, the motivations behind creating sovereign models and the roles these models play in today’s AI ecosystem, particularly in light of emerging sovereignty requirements across Europe and around the world.

We'll also explore what it takes to build state-of-the-art models—from data preparation and quality assurance to large-scale distributed training, compression optimization, and deployment (using tools such as NeMo, Model Optimizer, Dynamo, TRT-LLM)—as well as how to develop a successful developer ecosystem around your models and products to ensure long-term sustainability.

title: From Data to Meaning: Vision-Language Models Shaping the Cities of Tomorrow
abstract:
In this session, we’ll explore how K2K is applying data-compliant Vision-Language Models (VLMs) integrated with NVIDIA Cosmos Reason and Metropolis Blueprint for Video Search and Summarization (VSS) to turn passive footage into proactive decision-making.
We’ll walk through how VLM-driven understanding enables cities to automate operational processes, anticipate incidents, and optimize resource allocation through granular semantic insight rather than traditional detection alone. We’ll also share how generated data, synthetic data and simulation pipelines are being used to scale robust urban AI without exposing personal identity data.
The result: AI agents that don’t just “see,” but interpret, reason, and support real-time city operations in a trustworthy way.

title: From Foundation Models to SLMs: Powering Vertical AI Factories on NVIDIA (Presented by Tech Mahindra, Ltd.)
abstract:
Enterprises and governments are rethinking how they deploy large foundation models alongside smaller, domain-specific language models to achieve accuracy, security, and cost efficiency at scale. While foundation models provide broad intelligence, domain-tuned small language models (SLMs) are increasingly critical for embedding AI into vertical, regulated, and low-latency operational workflows. 

In this session, we explore how Tech Mahindra is leveraging NVIDIA-accelerated platforms to support the evolution from foundation models to production-grade AI factories—systems that continuously train, fine-tune, deploy, and operate both LLMs and SLMs. Drawing on real-world experience, including telecom-specific language models delivering measurable operational outcomes—such as AI-led handling of over 50% of network operations tickets across autonomous and human-approved workflows—and India’s Indus AI mission focused on education at national scale, we show how sovereign AI, private AI, and edge AI can be integrated to deliver secure, scalable, and vertical-ready AI solutions, leveraging NVIDIA technologies such as NVIDIA AI Enterprise, RAPIDS, and NeMo-based data tooling.

speakers.name: Nikhil Malhotra
speakers.jobTitle: CIO and Global Head of AI and Emerging Tech Business

title: From Foundation to Full-Throttle: Expert Advice on AI Model Customizations
abstract:
Specialized models are becoming necessary to win in the enterprise. Come speak to our field team about your challenges with deploying specialized models. They can share real stories from their experience with customization strategies, their successes and their failures.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod C (L2)

title: From Grace to Vera: NVIDIA’s next data center CPU
abstract:
In the past two years, the NVIDIA Grace CPU has reshaped the foundation of modern data centers. Its successor, the NVIDIA Vera CPU, will become available soon. Vera marks an exciting evolution of CPU design. Based on a custom Arm CPU core, this new CPU provides unprecedented performance and efficiency. But what does this mean for system integrators and developers? In this talk, we will discuss how Vera improves upon Grace. We'll analyze the new features of the upcoming Vera CPU, and how you can use them to unleash Vera’s full potential. We will also revisit typical applications and usage scenarios, and detail how Vera’s architecture is particularly geared toward speeding up such workloads.

After the session, join us at our follow on "Connect with the Experts Session" to ask further questions with our team of experts - How to Run and Optimize Your Workloads on the NVIDIA Grace and Vera CPUs [CWES81771].

speakers.name: Matthias Langer
speakers.jobTitle: Sr. AI and Developer Technology Engineer

title: From Idle to Optimized: Managing GPUs, Kubernetes, and Automated Compute at Scale (Presented by HPE)
abstract:
As AI workloads grow in size and complexity, infrastructure teams are under pressure to deliver high GPU utilization, predictable performance, and strong operational controls across a mix of Kubernetes and bare-metal environments. Limited visibility into GPU behavior can quickly lead to inefficiency, cost overruns, and governance gaps.

This session examines practical approaches to observing, provisioning, and optimizing GPU-accelerated infrastructure for AI workloads. We’ll discuss strategies for end-to-end GPU monitoring, automation patterns for Kubernetes and bare-metal environments, and techniques for aligning performance optimization with policy and governance requirements. The focus is on repeatable, scalable practices that infrastructure and platform teams can apply in real-world AI environments.

title: From Imagination to Experimentation to Full-Scale Adoption: Ensure a Successful Generative AI Project (Presented by SHI)
abstract: Join us as we explore SHI's approach to supporting customers' AI journies and adoption of generative AI within their existing enterprise environments. We’ll share best practices and challenges encountered in identifying, prototyping, building, and scaling AI applications.

title: From Ingestion to Inference: Mastering the High-Performance GPU Data Science Pipeline
abstract:
Get a comprehensive guide to constructing a complete, high-performance data science pipeline that leverages GPU acceleration throughout its life cycle. We'll sequentially cover the pipeline's core components: data ingestion, exploration, processing, and model training. For the initial stages of data ingestion and exploration, we'll demonstrate the use of GPU-accelerated frameworks, specifically Polars and/or cuDF pandas, to achieve significant speedups in data manipulation and analysis. Then we'll showcase the power of the cuML library (part of the RAPIDS ecosystem) for training and testing various machine learning models entirely on the GPU.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Attendees need basic Python skills and introductory data science knowledge, but no prior GPU experience is required.

title: From Isolation to Integration: Evolving Confidential Computing for a Scalable, Secure Future
abstract:
Generative AI is rapidly becoming the defining workload of modern computing, but securing these powerful systems without compromising performance is still a challenge for most enterprises. This session dives into how you can lock down your most valuable AI assets—models, data, and prompts—while continuing to push the limits on speed, scale and insights.

Google and NVIDIA will share how confidential computing on NVIDIA GPUs can protect your data and model using accelerated computing, so your information stays shielded even while in use.

speakers.name: Nelly Porter
speakers.jobTitle: Director of Product Management, Trusted Cloud

title: From Kernels to Clusters: How PyTorch Powers High-Performance AI (Presented by Meta)
abstract: Discover how PyTorch is redefining high-performance AI from the ground up. This session explores PyTorch’s latest advances—from custom kernel authoring and the Helion compiler stack to Monarch’s next-generation distributed runtime for reinforcement learning to Torchcomms’ vendor-neutral, scalable communication layer. We’ll highlight how PyTorch’s robust fault tolerance and elasticity enable reliable training at massive scale, and how these innovations together create a unified, production-ready stack for both research and deployment. Learn why PyTorch is the open foundation for AI innovation across clusters, clouds, and devices.

title: From Physical Vision Systems to OpenUSD: Building Digital Twins for Virtual Commissioning
abstract: As conventional development of machine vision solutions is time-consuming and costly, and AI-driven automation accelerates, the ability to virtually commission vision systems before physical deployment is a game-changer. Basler has developed a comprehensive workflow for virtual commissioning by creating digital twins of machine vision systems built on NVIDIA Omniverse and OpenUSD. This approach helps companies configure, simulate, and optimize vision solutions in a virtual environment—cutting prototyping costs, reducing deployment risks, and speeding up AI-driven automation. This talk will explore how Basler transformed traditional camera hardware into interoperable, scalable virtual models that enable sensor realistic simulation of vision systems and synthetic data generation for AI training.

title: From Replacement to Enhancement: A Human-Centered Approach to Multi-Modal AI Design
abstract: As the landscape of artificial intelligence evolves, the need for AI systems that embrace the full range of human experience has never been more important. Drawing on research from the Stanford Institute for Human-Centered AI (HAI), we'll explore strategies for embedding human-centered design principles into AI technologies. By emphasizing the power of interdisciplinary collaboration, we'll illustrate how AI can enhance human capability and shift the narrative around AI from one of replacement to one of augmentation. See how multi-modal user interfaces can create richer, more holistic interactions with AI, and how academia and industry can co-create AI solutions that serve as partners in enhancing human agency while fostering positive social impact within diverse communities.

title: From Research to Production: How Alpamayo Accelerates Autonomous Vehicle Development
abstract: Discover the latest advancements in end-to-end autonomous driving as the industry accelerates toward scalable, Level 4 AI-driven systems. This session explores how NVIDIA’s Alpamayo—particularly reasoning vision-language-action models (VLA)—enable vehicles to interpret, reason about, and navigate complex real-world scenarios with greater humanlike understanding. We’ll highlight how this approach is strengthened by a continuous, self-improving loop powered by NVIDIA Cosmos and agentic AI, and safeguarded by NVIDIA Halos, NVIDIA’s comprehensive full-stack safety system for physical AI. Finally, we’ll discuss how NVIDIA is building an open ecosystem to empower partners in developing next-generation, robotaxi-ready autonomous vehicles.

title: From Sandbox to Scale: Build Production-Ready AI Agents for Your Business
abstract:
Join our experts for a candid, face-to-face discussion on the critical steps needed to take your AI agents from proof-of-concept to production. We'll go beyond the basics, diving into real-world challenges like building for scale, ensuring robust security, and maintaining performance in enterprise environments. This is your chance to get practical advice, ask your toughest questions, and learn what it takes to build agents that truly deliver value for the business.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod A (L2)

title: From Signal to Strategy: Unlock Alpha With AI-Powered Research and Trading
abstract:
In today's real-time markets, every millisecond and every signal matters. Financial firms are overwhelmed by the scale and variety of data, from ultra-low-latency market feeds to an explosion of unstructured and alternative content such as news, filings, research, and transcripts. Traditional research and trading stacks struggle to turn these data into production-grade strategies fast enough and with the accuracy needed to generate sustainable alpha.

By fusing precise quantitative time-series data with unstructured intelligence and running it on NVIDIA GPUs, the bank can build domain-accurate models, reduce hallucinations through time-aware retrieval and vector search, and safely deploy agentic AI that supports quants, researchers, and traders in real time; shortening the path from research to live deployment, and improving productivity through research copilots and execution agents.

title: From Silicon to Solution: Building a World-Class Inference Engine for the AI Builder (Presented by GMI Cloud)
abstract:
As NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs power production inference stacks originally built for previous generations, developers may observe unexpected bottlenecks. Higher peak compute and faster interconnects do not automatically translate to lower latency or better cost efficiency at scale.

This session examines how inference system behavior changes when transitioning from NVIDIA Hopper to NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs. We’ll focus on concrete architectural shifts and show how the architecture improves. Rather than benchmarking peak performance, we’ll analyze observable patterns that emerge under real inference traffic and discuss how to redesign scheduling, batching, and monitoring strategies to fully benefit from NVIDIA Blackwell architecture.

title: From Smart Factory to Lights-Out Factory: Operationalizing Industrial AI With LTTS and NVIDIA (Presented by LTTS)
abstract:
Manufacturing is advancing automation, yet factories still depend on human input for planning, quality, and operations. The next step is autonomous, self-optimizing factories powered by industrial AI.

L&T Technology Services (LTTS) introduces its Lights-Out Factory (LoF) solution, built on NVIDIA’s Industrial AI platform, Omniverse, Isaac, and CUDA-accelerated AI. LoF integrates digital twins, robotics, and AI-driven decision-making into a closed-loop system, enabling factories to move from assisted automation to autonomy.

This session shows how LTTS combines manufacturing expertise with NVIDIA’s AI to create learning factories. Discover how virtual twins simulate workflows, AI adapts to real-time conditions, and robotics improve yield, safety, and throughput. Real-world results include reduced cycle times, improved yields, and faster ramp-ups, with a roadmap for scaling LoF deployments

speakers.name: Umashankar KA
speakers.jobTitle: Senior Architect, AI Digital Twin Solutions

title: From Vision to Reality: Building AI Factories
abstract: Discover how to turn the vision of AI into a tangible reality within the industry. This session explores the organization, design, and deployment of “AI factories,” environments that integrate data, algorithms, and processes to accelerate innovation. We will share our experience creating real use cases on the NVIDIA platform, collaborating with strategic partners, and driving operational efficiency—a complete journey through digital transformation that turns AI into a true value engine for the organization.

title: Fundamentals of GPU-Accelerated Workflows with CUDA Python
abstract:
This course delivers a hands-on introduction to GPU-accelerated computing in Python, empowering developers to build fast, scalable applications using NVIDIA’s CUDA ecosystem. Through guided notebooks, participants master CuPy for array acceleration, cuDF for GPU DataFrames, and the cuda-python API to write custom kernels—all without leaving Python. Real-world exercises in data science, machine learning, and scientific computing emphasize performance, interoperability, and end-to-end efficiency.

Learners progress from drop-in speedups to fully integrated GPU pipelines, mastering data movement, asynchronous execution, and profiling with Nsight tools. By the end, attendees can transform CPU-bound Python code into production-grade GPU solutions, bridge prototyping to deployment, and apply best practices for performance and reproducibility in modern accelerated computing.


Certificate: Upon successful completion of the assessment, you’ll receive an NVIDIA certificate to recognize your subject matter competency and support your professional career growth.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Basic Python competency including familiarity with variable types, loops, conditional statements, functions, and array manipulations.
  • NumPy competency including the use of ndarrays and ufuncs.
  • No previous knowledge of CUDA programming is assumed.

title: Future of Enterprise AI: Infrastructure to Intelligence (Presented by Tata Consultancy Services)
abstract:
The future of enterprise AI is shaped by how intelligence is designed, deployed, and scaled across the enterprise. Next-gen computing platforms enable advanced models, including generative and agentic AI, to operate reliably in complex environments, allowing organizations to move beyond isolated automation toward AI-driven systems that reason, learn, and act in real time. 

This session examines how enterprises are designing AI-native architectures that combine high-performance compute, modern data platforms, and advanced models to support production-grade AI at scale. It explores how governance, trust, and resilience are embedded into AI systems from Day 1. With examples from industries such as banking, financial services, telecommunications, and media, the discussion highlights how accelerated AI is transforming enterprises from software-driven execution to intelligence-driven outcomes.

speakers.name: Sujatha Gopal
speakers.jobTitle: CTO of Communications, Media & Information Services

speakers.name: Amit Kapur
speakers.jobTitle: Chief AI and Services Transformation Officer

title: Galaxea Open-World Dataset and G0 Dual-System VLA Model
abstract: We present the Galaxea Open-World Dataset, a large and diverse collection of robotic behaviors captured in real human environments. Demonstrations are collected with a consistent robotic embodiment and annotated with precise subtask-level language descriptions for both training and evaluation. Building on this dataset, we introduce G0, a dual-system framework combining a vision-language model (VLM) for multi-modal planning and a vision-language-action (VLA) model for fine-grained execution. G0 is trained through a three-stage curriculum: cross-embodiment pre-training, single-embodiment pre-training, and task-specific post-training. Extensive benchmarks on tabletop manipulation, few-shot learning, and long-horizon mobile manipulation validate the approach, showing that single-embodiment pre-training, together with the Galaxea Dataset, is crucial for strong overall performance.

title: Game Agent Models - Picking & perfecting the right AI models for your gaming applications
abstract: As developer approaches to in-game agentic AI become more advanced, standard off-the-shelf models often fall short. Game developers frequently seek expertise in developing and customizing the most suitable models for their specific applications, as well as optimizing these models for peak performance. In this session, our experts will demonstrate how we develop and evaluate language models with Agentic AI capabilities for gaming platforms. They will guide you on utilizing NVIDIA tools to assess, train, and fine-tune AI models specifically designed for your gaming applications.

title: Generate Rare Driving Scenarios Using World Foundational Models Plus Omniverse
abstract: Design a world‑model‑driven framework that automatically generates diverse, realistic, and targeted rare driving scenarios, and quantify its impact on downstream perception and planning performance.

title: Generate Synthetic Data for Physical AI with NVIDIA Cosmos World Foundation Models
abstract:
This hands-on lab introduces roboticists, AI developers, and pipeline engineers to NVIDIA Cosmos world foundation models (WFMs) for synthetic data generation in robot learning pipelines. Participants will learn how to leverage simulation scenes from Isaac Sim and real-life images and videos to provide initial datasets and augment them with Cosmos Transfer, then evaluate data quality with Cosmos Reason—all within a production-ready robotics pipeline. Through this session, you'll experience an end-to-end data pipeline workflow, leveraging Cosmos WFMs to generate synthetic data from real-world and physically-accurate simulated data, and using Cosmos Reason as an intelligent critic to validate data quality. By completing this lab, you'll unlock the ability to generate high-quality training datasets, accelerating your action model development.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Familiarity with robotics simulation concepts (what is synthetic data, why it matters)
  • Basic Python scripting experience (reading/writing scripts, understanding function calls)
  • Understanding of machine learning fundamentals (training, fine-tuning, evaluation)
  • Familiarity with Cosmos (recommended course: An Introduction to NVIDIA Cosmos for Physical AI)

title: Generative AI in Media & Entertainment
abstract: This fast-paced lightning session features leading voices in generative AI and autonomous agents, exploring breakthroughs in creative generation, tool-using systems, and practical, real-world deployments. Each speaker will offer a distinct perspective on how these technologies are redefining creativity, productivity, and the future of intelligent software. Expect rapid insights, vivid demonstrations, and forward-looking ideas that reveal where generative and agentic AI are heading—and what they make possible today.

speakers.name: Rick Champagne
speakers.jobTitle: Director of Global Media & Entertainment Marketing Strategy

title: Generative Antibody Factory: GPU-Accelerated Diffusion Model and Protein Language Model for Nanobody Discovery
abstract: We present a GPU-accelerated generative AI pipeline that designs and evaluates nanobody candidates in days instead of months — combining diffusion-based structure generation with protein language models to improve developability and experimental success.

title: Gesture-Based Control of Heavy Equipment Using Simulation-First Robotics Workflows (Presented by Marvik)
abstract: Marvik presents a real-world use case demonstrating how computer vision, simulation, and NVIDIA Isaac Sim can be used to design more intuitive and safer human–machine interfaces for heavy equipment. The project explores gesture-based control of an excavator, using a standard camera and real-time pose estimation to translate operator movements into machine commands inside a high-fidelity simulated environment. By validating control logic, latency, and usability in simulation before touching physical hardware, this approach reduces development risk, shortens iteration cycles, and lowers costs. The session highlights how simulation-first workflows enable scalable foundations for future applications such as remote operation, semi-autonomous machinery, and intelligent operator assistance, while improving safety and operational efficiency in industrial settings.

title: Get NVIDIA-Certified at GTC for Free: Associate Certification
abstract:
Register separately to book an exam slot and take your chosen exam onsite.

title: GLARE: GPU-Accelerated 3D CT Vision-Language Reasoning via Efficient Anatomical Boundary Guidance
abstract: Interpreting volumetric CT scans using vision-language models requires significant GPU resources and robust spatial focus. GLARE introduces slice-wise visual-instruction prompting, overlaying explicit blue contours generated by NVIDIA VISTA-3D on every axial CT slice to direct model attention to clinically relevant regions. Combined with a compact Qwen-2.5 decoder, this design enables high-performance medical reasoning with reduced VRAM footprint and inference time, delivering state-of-the-art VQA accuracy on medical benchmarks.

title: Glasses-Free 3D Smart Educational Training and Decision Support Platform for Radiation Oncology Treatment
abstract: We present a light field-based 3D planning platform that enhances visualization, precision, and workflow efficiency in radiation therapy planning. The system integrates advanced eye-tracking, light-field rendering, and GPU-accelerated computation to provide real-time, glasses-free 3D visualization of anatomical structures and radiation dose distributions. By performing automated image segmentation and continuous ray-tracing calculations, the platform enables clinicians to instantly identify underdosed or overdosed regions and make rapid adjustments. Clinical trials demonstrate significant improvements in both accuracy and efficiency, reducing the experience-based planning gap from 67 minutes to 16 minutes. Integrating powerful GPU computing not only ensures real-time performance, but also establishes a new standard for intelligent, interactive, and precise radiation therapy planning.

title: Glider: A Unified Platform Architecture for Resource Placement and Coordinated Scheduling of Dynamic AI Workloads
abstract: Dive deep into new solutions for the complex challenge of scheduling dynamic AI workloads. We present Glider, a unified platform that improves resource utilization by using workload-centric orchestration, fragmentation-aware placement, and native gang scheduling to efficiently coordinate parallel jobs.

title: Global Governance and the Future of AI Safety Regulation
abstract: The pace of AI regulations is increasing, and the complexity of complying with different laws is challenging for global companies. For example, EU member countries are passing "companion" regulations for the Artificial Intelligence Act. Meanwhile, the U.S. federal government hasn't adopted any AI regs, but many states have, and the current administration's focus differs from that of the previous administration. At the same time, Congress is focused primarily on child safety. How can companies comply with laws in such a fragmented environment?

title: GPU Access to Unbounded Dataset Sizes Breaks Open Document Ingestion and Search
abstract: Vector databases with 1 trillion vectors cannot fit in memory, and VecDB ingestion and search requires the massive concurrency, memory bandwidth, and latency tolerance of GPUs. Come see new programming models, new storage server reference designs, new SSD offerings, and new customer-oriented scaled results.

title: GPU Efficiency Optimization for Multi-Trillion Video Recommendation Models
abstract: Large-scale recommendation systems, critical for Facebook Video, face significant computational and memory challenges due to inherent scale, imbalanced sparse embeddings, and increased dense computation. We've advanced multifaceted optimizations for GPU resources. Specifically, we implement 60-plus custom forward and backward kernels with 2-100X speedup, including rowwise FP8 for GEMM, custom Flash Attention masks, feature encoder operators, and Tensor memory/compute operation on jagged Tensors. We also explore various techniques to eliminate stream sync points and CPU blocking operations and demonstrate the effectiveness of overlapping communication with compute, distributed inference/caching, and AOTI lowering. These combined optimizations result in >4X QPS gain for both training and inference, significantly lowering down infra cost and enabling model innovations.

title: GPU ♥ LPU: Everything You Wanted to Know
abstract: With language processing units (LPUs) and GPUs in the same data center, developers can optimize for both ultra-low latency and high throughput. LPUs excel at token-by-token and interactive workloads, while GPUs excel at massive parallelism and larger batches. This fireside chat helps developers understand the complementary strengths of LPUs and GPUs.

title: GPU-Accelerated Chinese Brain-to-Text Interface Powered by NVIDIA H100 80GB
abstract: This poster presents the first GPU-accelerated EEG-to-Chinese text generation framework, enabling brain-to-text communication for Chinese speakers with speech disorders. By leveraging the NVIDIA H100’s Transformer Engine and mixed-precision training, the system achieves threefold faster training and 60 TFLOPS performance, making real-time decoding feasible. The model decodes 128-channel EEG signals into open-vocabulary Chinese text, addressing a long-standing gap in multilingual brain-computer interface (BCI) research. This work empowers inclusive assistive communication and sets a new foundation for large-scale, clinically deployable Chinese brain-computer interfaces.

speakers.name: Chisheng Chen
speakers.company: Harvard Medical School/Beth Israel Deaconess Medical Center
speakers.jobTitle: AI Research Assistant, Harvard Medical School, and AI Trainer, OpenAI

title: GPU-Accelerated Image Processing and Data Loading With NVIDIA Image Processing Libraries
abstract:
Explore how to build high-performance, GPU-accelerated image pre- and post-processing pipelines using nvImageCodec, NPP, DALI, and CV-CUDA across domains such as semiconductors, medical imaging, geospatial, and more. See how nvImageCodec (with nvTIFF, nvJPEG, nvJPEG2000, HTJ2K) accelerates decode/encode, how NPP provides low-level image primitives, and how CV-CUDA and DALI enable scalable, model-ready pipelines. We’ll share recent optimizations and roadmap highlights, and use this session to gather your feedback on requirements across these libraries.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Mahesh Khadatare
speakers.jobTitle: Sr. CUDA Math Library Engineer and Team Lead

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod A (L2)

title: GPU-Accelerated Machine Learning Interatomic Potentials With cuEquivariance and MILAP
abstract: Explore new techniques to extend the spatial and temporal limits of machine learning interatomic potentials (MLIAP). Leverage MLIAP for distributed inference in molecular dynamics and cuEquivariance for accelerated performance, achieving 26× faster training and 12× faster inference.

title: GPU-Accelerated Multi-Agent Large Language Models  for Enhanced Recovery After Surgery (ERAS) Protocols
abstract:
ERAS protocols need real-time, personalized support; CPU rule engines have high latency and low throughput. We quantify performance and clinical utility of GPU-accelerated LLM inference for ERAS decision support by building a multi-agent LLM system on a single RTX 5090 with hybrid RAG, benchmarked against a CPU rules baseline across three ERAS use cases: PONV prevention, POD checklists, and chest-tube removal. Evaluations included microbenchmarks, concurrency stress tests, and task-level accuracy.

Results: Mean latency fell 2.9× (1900→650 ms); P95 fell 2.4× (2050→850 ms); max throughput rose 7× (0.5→3.5 QPS). Under 16-patient concurrency, P95 remained ~2.1 s with GPU utilization 60–80% and VRAM 16–18 GB. Task accuracy reached 95%.

A single RTX 5090 enables bedside, sub-second responses and supports 8–16 concurrent patients, offering a practical solution with a favorable cost profile.

speakers.name: Hsing-hsien Wu
speakers.company: Division of Thoracic Surgery, Tainan Municipal Hospital, Taiwan

title: GPU-Accelerated Scalable Domain-Specific Data Curation Using VLM
abstract: We will explain how to implement data curation specialized for a specific domain (e.g., autonomous driving) by linking NVIDIA NeMo Curator and vision language models and utilizing image caption information. Solving the duplicate data problem, which is a bottleneck in AI development, and creating highly accurate datasets that are impossible to do manually are essential to improving model performance. This poster provides cutting-edge, practical insights that will dramatically improve AI learning efficiency and quality.

speakers.name: 山田 直
speakers.jobTitle: プラットフォーム開発本部 開発部 MLエンジニアリンググループ Physical AI 技術開発リーダー

title: GPU-Accelerated Simulations of Flow-Structure Interaction at Extreme Scales
abstract: Numerical simulation of the Navier-Stokes equation is among the most complex problems in computational sciences. The complexity increases tremendously when incorporating moving boundaries and modeling interactions with multiple physics, such as structural mechanics, acoustics, and more. We present a sharp-interface immersed boundary method and accelerate it using GPUs to simulate some of the most complex problems currently beyond the capabilities of many industrial solvers.

title: GPU-Accelerated SIP/RTP Real-Time Voice Customer Service System
abstract: We present a GPU-accelerated real-time voice interaction system built on NVIDIA NeMo, TensorRT-LLM, and Riva, capable of running directly over standard SIP/RTP telephony. Using only one RTX 5070 Ti or RTX 5090 GPU, the system achieves sub-600 ms end-to-end conversational latency, performing automatic speech recognition (ASR), LLM inference, and text-to-speech (TTS) fully on-device. This work demonstrates how small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) can deploy on-premises AI voice agents using consumer-grade GPUs, reducing cloud costs and ensuring secure, low-latency communication.

title: GPU‑Accelerated Performance Gains With AI-RAN powered by Nokia and NVIDIA (Presented by Nokia)
abstract: Get a look at how Nokia’s co‑innovation with NVIDIA marks a deep integration of GPUs in the radio access network (RAN), bringing high‑performance computing to the edge and accelerating the path to 6G leadership. This session will discuss the latest performance benchmarks and new use cases for AI‑RAN.

title: Graduate Fellowship Fast Forward Talks
abstract: Join a special presentation from our 2025-26 Graduate Fellowship recipients to learn "what's next" from the world of research and academia. Sponsored projects involve a variety of technical challenges, including machine learning, humanoid robotics, computer vision, speech and audio, reliability, drug discovery, agentic AI and trustworthy AI. These minds lead the future in our industry, and we're proud to support the 2025-26 NVIDIA Graduate Fellows. We'll also announce the 2026-27 Graduate Fellows at this session. For more information on the NVIDIA Graduate Fellowship program, visit www.nvidia.com/en-us/research/graduate-fellowships

speakers.name: Bill Dally
speakers.jobTitle: Chief Scientist and SVP of Research

title: GreenMind: A LLM for Complex Reasoning — Chain-of-Thought Data and Self-Correction Cycles on NVIDIA NEMO
abstract: We introduce GreenMind, a large language model designed for complex reasoning through chain-of-thought data and self-correction cycles. By leveraging the GRPO strategy from NVIDIA NEMO for fine-tuning, we guide the model to generate logically coherent responses, deliver informative answers, and offer intuitive explanations. These features provide value not only to end users, but also to ongoing research aimed at improving data quality and sampling techniques.

title: Groove Intelligence: Reviving Vinyl Through Artificial Intelligence
abstract: Explore how AI and computer vision can read sound visually. This project presents a digital turntable that plays vinyl records using only a 4K camera and deep learning to reconstruct audio from microscopic groove images in real time. Using NVIDIA GPU acceleration, we merge art and technology to preserve fragile analog media and reveal new possibilities for sound restoration, cultural heritage, and multi-modal AI — where image and sound become one expressive language.

title: GTC Live 2026 Keynote Pregame
abstract: Catch the excitement of the keynote pregame. Be there for the live conversations between the hosts and industry leader panelists as they examine accelerated computing beyond AI, NVIDIA’s role as a full-stack infrastructure company, and the five-layer foundation behind one of the largest infrastructures build-outs in history — and the ecosystem required to deliver it worldwide. See the full program here.

title: Hands-On NVIDIA DGX Spark (Sponsored by GCL)
abstract: NVIDIA is teaming up with GCL for a hands-on lab on NVIDIA DGX Spark. In this lab, you will build an AI agent using the latest open-source NVIDIA and community model, and explore enhancing and extending the agent abilities.

title: Hands-On With Earth-2: Building Local and National Weather Resilience With AI
abstract:
We explore the life cycle of AI-based high-resolution regional weather forecasting applications, which are used by businesses and governments to achieve independent local weather and climate prediction capabilities at high accuracy and unprecedented speed. We'll start by creating a custom high-resolution model, guiding you through preparing a training dataset, training a downscaling diffusion model with NVIDIA PhysicsNeMo, and packaging it for real-time use. We then use NVIDIA Earth2Studio to integrate our custom model with a global AI-based weather forecasting model, such as FourCastNet 3, to build a full regional forecasting workflow, enabling sharper and more actionable local forecasts. We'll then discuss leveraging custom weather observations to improve the accuracy of the predictions, and deploying the workflow in operations so it can be productized as an inference service.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Previous DLI: https://learn.nvidia.com/courses/course-detail?course_id=course-v1:DLI+S-FX-31+V1
  • Basic Python and Deep learning experience.

title: Hard-Won Lessons From Production Inference at Scale (Presented by Together AI)
abstract: Inference is where AI systems face real-world constraints: latency users can feel, costs that scale with every request, and reliability requirements that leave no room for failure. In this session, hear lessons in delivering and continuously optimizing inference in production on Together AI, the AI-native cloud—powering Cursor’s Composer 2.0 background coding magenta at massive scale. Drawing on Together’s research-driven inference platform and Cursor’s experience operating AI products under real developer traffic, the discussion explores how foundational systems work translates into real-world improvements in speed, cost, and reliability—serving trillions of tokens per day across multi-data center deployments and running production inference on NVIDIA HGX B200 and NVIDIA GB200 NVL72, even under unpredictable demand.

title: Hardware-Efficient and Lossless Video Generation at Scale: Native Sparse Attention and Mixed-Low-Bit Quantization
abstract: We present a unified, hardware-aware framework for scaling large video generation models—such as Diffusion Transformers (DiT)—without compromising visual fidelity. Learn how production-grade model compression techniques jointly eliminate the quadratic cost bottlenecks that limit training for long-context AIGC models. The session covers domain-specialized sparse attention for video sequences, robust low-precision training pipelines especially about low-bit attention mechanism. With industrial-scale results demonstrating 2x end-to-end acceleration from sparse attention and 60–70% savings via low-bit computation—all with near-zero accuracy loss—this talk provides a practical blueprint for building efficient, lossless, and deployable video generative models at scale.

title: Harness Agentic AI and Reasoning Models to Accelerate Scientific Discovery
abstract: Learn how an AI co-scientist that combines powerful reasoning models with science simulations can expand the aperture for human scientists to achieve breakthroughs in domains such as fusion energy and targeted alpha therapy for cancer. This session delves into strategies for overcoming the challenge of data scarcity for training, for employing reinforcement learning leveraging physics simulations, AI surrogates, emulators, and building AI agents that can reiterate autonomously.

title: Harness AI to Accelerate Innovation in Healthcare Robotics
abstract: The next era in surgery will be defined by the convergence of advanced technology and human-centered care. In this session, Neda Cvijetic, global head of robotics and digital R&D at Johnson & Johnson MedTech, will highlight the role of AI, simulation, and accelerated computing in Johnson & Johnson’s mission to unlock the full potential of medical innovation for those who dedicate their lives to care.

speakers.name: Neda Cvijetic
speakers.jobTitle: Global Head, Robotics and Digital R&D

title: Harness NVIDIA AI Advanced Tools for Generative AI in Digital Health
abstract:
Explore the NVIDIA Data Flywheel, which uses agent deployment artifacts from production applications to increase the overall accuracy and reduce the latency/cost of agentic AI systems. We'll explore how to utilize the NeMo Microservices Platform for programmatic control of datasets, fine-tuning, evaluation, and inference.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Familiarity with Python, basic understanding of how agentic systems work, hands-on knowledge is encouraged but not required.

title: Harness the Potential and Navigate the Challenges of Agentic AI Across Colleges and Universities Worldwide
abstract:
Discover how to design, deploy, and scale agentic AI systems that transform research acceleration, student support, and campus operations while navigating governance, safety, and infrastructure challenges unique to higher education. Join NVIDIA's Higher Education and Research team to explore proven architectures, deployment patterns, and integration strategies that help you move from experimentation to production-ready AI agents across your institution.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod A (L2)

title: Healthcare Reimagined: Bridging Digital Intelligence and Physical Autonomy
abstract: Robotics and AI are redefining healthcare, accelerating diagnostics, surgery, patient care, and operational efficiency. We'll highlight how NVIDIA technologies drive this transformation, from physical AI systems that perform precision tasks and address workforce shortages to digital AI agents that interpret multimodal data, predict patient needs, and guide decision-making. We'll dive into the latest NVIDIA technology—spanning cutting-edge hardware, AI factories, and next-gen simulation—enabling both physical and digital robots. You’ll see how these technologies unify sensing, perception, planning, control, and generative reasoning into deployable devices. You'll also learn how medical technology developers are building on NVIDIA’s ecosystem of tools, reference workflows, and partner solutions to accelerate R&D, improve system intelligence, and bring autonomous capabilities to life.

speakers.name: Prerna Dogra
speakers.jobTitle: Director Product Management & Developer Ecosystem - MedTech

title: High Performance AI-RAN Made Simple; Python to CUDA Signal Processing at the Speed of Light
abstract:
The Aerial Framework has been designed from the ground up to meet the needs of 3GPP radio access networks (RANs)—signal processing workloads with microsecond latency requirements. It is a platform that unites research, test beds, and production deployments to solve development challenges for real-time applications.  

The session will give a hands-on tutorial to design algorithms in JAX/Torch and instantly deploy to real-time GPU pipelines with inline networking.  

GitHub: https://github.com/NVIDIA/aerial-framework

title: High-Performance Classical Control for Scaling Quantum Computers
abstract: Large-scale, useful quantum computers will require the full machinery of fault tolerance, bringing a formidable challenge for classical control electronics. In this talk, we'll illustrate the importance of hybrid quantum-classical systems by outlining PsiQuantum's single-photon architecture for quantum computing, how it implements quantum error correction, and initial work with NVIDIA to accelerate this with GPU supercomputing.

title: High-Performance Inference for Frontier AI Models (Presented by Baseten)
abstract: How do you go from a state-of-the-art foundation model to a globally available usage-based API? This session provides an overview of the roadmap and architecture from model performance optimization to multi-region GPU-based infrastructure setup to the precise mechanics of request prioritization, token accounting, and rate limiting. Building on an inference stack that includes technologies like NVIDIA Dynamo, TensorRT-LLM, and CuTe, we'll trace the path from model weights to a robust production service.

title: High-Performance LLM Serving and Training with SGLang
abstract:
This training lab shows you how to optimize and scale LLM workflows with SGLang. We walk through practical performance tuning using the SGL-Cookbook, dive into profiling and bottleneck analysis for developers, and demonstrate its deep integration into reinforcement learning (RL) training frameworks by showing how to use SGLang in a real RL run with the Miles RL framework.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Comfortable with Python and command-line workflows
  • Curious about how LLM systems are optimized and scaled in practice
  • No prior experience with SGLang, performance tuning, or RL training is required

title: Holistic Model Co-Design: Close the Loop With Software and Hardware for Inference Efficiency
abstract:
The economics of inference are changing faster than ever, with the cost of serving frontier models growing at an unprecedented rate. To continue advancing intelligence per unit cost, we must look beyond isolated optimizations and adopt a holistic, cross-layer design philosophy spanning silicon, software, and model architecture.
 
This session will explore the opportunities of hardware/software/algorithm co-design, highlighting how aligning model innovations with NVIDIA hardware features can unlock orders-of-magnitude efficiency gains. By educating the community on these co-design principles, we can accelerate the development of the next generation of frontier models and deepen our strategic advantage through tighter integration between hardware and intelligence.

title: How AI Coding Augments IT Teams Without Replacing Them (Presented by CDW)
abstract:
This session shows how AI coding can be used today by IT teams with the tools and systems they already have. You will see how common IT work such as maintaining legacy code and scripts, reducing technical debt, and creating tests and documentation can be done faster with AI, without changing platforms or processes.

Through real-world examples and a short demo, we will show how AI coding fits into existing IT workflows instead of requiring new tools or large integrations. The focus is on practical and low-risk use cases that deliver quick value, reduce ongoing maintenance work, and help teams spend more time on higher-impact efforts like modernization and optimization.

title: How AI Factories Can Shift-left to Accelerate Infrastructure and Ecosystems Readiness
abstract: As AI infrastructure scales, the traditional “build-then-test” model has become a critical bottleneck. Join NVIDIA and CoreWeave to explore how a shift-left methodology, powered by digital twins, is transforming the AI factory lifecycle. CoreWeave will share how NVIDIA Air decouples software automation from hardware lead times, enabling validation of complex systems well before physical infrastructure arrives. In parallel, NVIDIA uses these digital twin environments to empower a global ecosystem of software partners to develop and optimize next-generation platforms without requiring access to physical hardware. This session demonstrates how a simulation-first approach ensures the entire stack, from cloud automation to ISV solutions, is production-ready on day one, dramatically reducing time-to-market and capital risk.

title: How AI Is Changing Everyday Work, Insights from NVentures Portfolio Companies
abstract: This NVentures-hosted panel explores how AI is redefining the way we work across industries, from healthcare to manufacturing. Hear from innovative portfolio leaders who are applying AI to streamline operations, enhance decision-making, and unlock new levels of human productivity.

title: How AI is Changing the Scientific Method in Biology and Beyond
abstract: Understand where AI fits into the scientific method: from hypothesis generation to experiment design, in-silico experimentation, and  programmable labs to data interpretation.

speakers.name: Rory Kelleher
speakers.jobTitle: Sr. Director, Global Business Development, Life Sciences

title: How AI Systems Evolve Into Long-Horizon Agents
abstract: Learn how an AI system can evolve from simple search into long-horizon agents that run for hours or days to complete complex, real-world tasks. This session walks through four stages of that evolution—AI search, asynchronous workflows, agents, and long-horizon agents—and examines the architectural patterns and technical trade-offs required at each step. We will cover practical approaches we learned building Genspark, including model routing, tool execution frameworks, validation of agent trajectories, and reliability strategies needed to operate agent systems at scale.

title: How AI-Infused Platforms Redefine Modernization, Delivery, and Value (Presented by Wipro)
abstract: In the AI era, competitiveness will be determined not by how fast enterprises modernize once, but by how effectively they institutionalize change itself. AI platforms illustrate this shift by productizing modernization intelligence, embedding AI across the software life cycle, and enabling enterprises to reduce the cost of change while accelerating AI readiness.

speakers.name: Shivakumar Krishnan
speakers.jobTitle: GM, Global Practice Head of Application Engineering – Industry Cloud & Digital

title: How HBM4 Unlocks Efficient LLM Serving at Scale (Presented by SK hynix)
abstract: Explore how next-generation high-bandwidth memory (HBM4) improves large language model serving by addressing two critical inference constraints: memory bandwidth and capacity. Through detailed profiling and controlled scaling studies across modern LLMs and reasoning- and agent-based workloads, we show how HBM4’s substantially higher bandwidth and increased capacity translate into lower latency and higher throughput in LLM serving systems, and we project the performance gains production AI systems can expect from GPU platforms equipped with HBM4.

title: How NVIDIA Can Help You, as a Researcher, Accelerate Scientific Discovery
abstract:
Join this engaging session led by NVIDIA experts who collaborate closely with the global academic research community, supporting leading researchers in leveraging NVIDIA software technologies to drive scientific advancements across diverse fields. We'll provide insights into how various NVIDIA teams, representing different functional areas, can assist in accelerating your scientific discoveries at multiple levels. The team will outline their approach to working with individual researchers and groups, share success stories, and introduce a range of developer support programs, including the Academic Grant Program, DLI courses and teaching kits, etc. We'll also focus on aligning discussions with specific research domains such as robotics, biology, Gen AI, data processing, and climate science—while showcasing tools and resources available to help researchers enhance research outcomes.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Judit Szulagyi
speakers.jobTitle: Director of Higher Education and Research (EMEA)

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod B (L2)

title: How NVIDIA Tapped Its Unstructured Data for AI Insights
abstract: Enterprises sit on a treasure trove of knowledge, the biproduct of years of digital operations, locked away in silos. In this session, NVIDIA IT, along with ecosystem partners, will walk through the architectural patterns, governance models, and “vendor lab” environment NVIDIA created to unlock the value of this data for AI developers and agents.

title: How Perplexity AI Scales Real-Time Search With Breakthrough Mixture-of-Experts Performance
abstract: Scaling mixture-of-experts (MoE) models in production while delivering high performance is challenging. This session explores how the NVIDIA Blackwell NVL72 makes MoE scaling practical and efficient with full stack innovation across compute, networking, and software. Hear from Perplexity AI on why they have adopted MoE models, detailing how they leverage Blackwell NVL72 to deliver real-time search with breakthrough performance.

title: How Snap Saves Millions with Accelerated Apache Spark
abstract: We'll detail our journey, achievements, and insights gained from implementing the Apache Spark RAPIDS accelerator across Snap's most substantial data processing pipelines. A significant cost saving of 76% to 90% has been realized in comparison to utilizing CPU-only jobs. We are currently collaborating with Google and NVIDIA to further deploy the RAPIDS accelerator to a broader range of our production workloads.

title: How Sony PlayStation Built a Multi-Tenant GPU Cluster to Accelerate R&D
abstract: This talk outlines how we design and operate a global GPU research cluster that supports AI and visual computing work for our console platform and game development studios. We use NVIDIA GPUs to train advanced models that run on PlayStation hardware to improve rendering, gameplay systems, and overall player experience. Running shared GPU infrastructure at scale brings challenges: securing data center space, long procurement cycles, multi-tenant Kubernetes management, and noisy-neighbor pressures as research needs shift. We will share lessons in cluster design, planning, security, and operations that help studios innovate and push the console experience forward.

title: How to Accelerate AI Workflows With NVIDIA Run:ai
abstract:
Unlock faster, smarter AI development in this hands-on introduction to the NVIDIA Run:ai platform. In this lab, you’ll explore how Run:ai streamlines machine learning operations, removes infrastructure bottlenecks, and simplifies workload management to accelerate outcomes. Learn how dynamic resource allocation transforms efficiency across enterprise teams, ensuring workloads run faster, smoother, and at scale. Then apply what you learn directly in your lab environment as instructors guide you through real-world examples of accelerated ML operations. You’ll leave with practical skills and a strong foundation for optimizing performance with Run:ai.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Basic understanding of machine learning workflows and terminology.
  • General familiarity with containerized environments (Docker or Kubernetes preferred).
  • No prior Run:ai experience required.

title: How to Build End‑to‑End Physical AI Systems for Humanoid Robots
abstract:
Physical AI brings intelligence to machines, industrial arms, autonomous mobile robots (AMRs), and humanoids, enabling them to perceive, reason, and act in the real world in real time. These robots go beyond scripted behavior to make intelligent decisions on the fly: moving materials, assembling parts, and collaborating safely with people in dynamic environments.

In this session, experts will walk through practical, end-to-end workflows for physical AI development using NVIDIA Isaac and Omniverse technologies. You’ll learn workflows spanning data collection and curation, digital twin creation, model training and scaling, high-fidelity simulation, and deployment to real hardware. We’ll highlight proven toolchains and real-world examples that developers can apply to accelerate the path to smarter, more adaptable robots.

title: How to Build Your Custom AI Advantage (Presented by Mistral AI)
abstract: Enterprises need AI that builds on their unique expertise and strengthens their competitive edge. In this session, Tim Lacroix (co-founder and CTO, Mistral AI), Kari Briski (vice president of Gen AI, NVIDIA), and Carlos Pereira (chief architect, Cisco Customer Experience) reveal how Mistral AI’s open-source leadership—paired with a full-stack AI approach—delivers unmatched customization, efficiency, and control. We’ll reveal new models, products and platforms from Mistral AI that help to unlock the ROI of AI in the enterprise.

speakers.name: Carlos Pereira
speakers.jobTitle: Fellow and Chief Architect, Customer Experience (CX)

title: How to Design and Build Gigawatt Scale AI Factories
abstract:
Designing gigawatt-scale AI factories requires a shift from bespoke construction to standardized, modular architecture. This session introduces DSX, NVIDIA’s modular reference design for large-scale AI infrastructure. We move beyond traditional data center design to a purpose-built framework optimized for the latest liquid-cooled compute.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod B (L2)
Connect With the Experts Pod F (L2)

title: How to Provision and Install NVIDIA Run:ai
abstract:
Get grounded in the practical fundamentals of deploying NVIDIA Run:ai from the ground up.
