This lab walks you through every stage of installation—from environment preparation to final validation—so you can confidently stand-up Run:ai within your own organization. Follow a guided, instructor-led deployment in your provided lab environment, learn how to verify installations, and practice diagnosing and resolving common setup issues. Whether you’re preparing for full production rollout or building a proof of concept, this session delivers the skills to deploy successfully and maintain stability.

Prerequisite(s):

  • General experience with Kubernetes cluster administration.
  • Basic understanding of containerized workloads and GPU infrastructure.

title: How to Run AI-Powered Computer-Aided Engineering Simulations
abstract:
In this lab, we explore how to build AI surrogate models for crash simulations, one of the most computationally intensive tasks in computer aided-engineering (CAE). Learn how modern AI architectures can reproduce high-fidelity physics simulations at a fraction of the cost, enabling faster design exploration and decision-making. We'll walk through the full workflow for developing an AI surrogate for CAE applications, including data preparation and analysis, training and optimization, loading and running inference with pre-trained models, and evaluating model accuracy and uncertainty. We'll also discuss how AI-accelerated workflows are transforming CAE across various industries and areas of research, and how these tools can integrate seamlessly into existing engineering pipelines.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Basic understanding of engineering or scientific modeling workflows and the associated technical terminology
  • Basic programming knowledge in Python (data structures, functions, basic libraries)
  • Understanding of deep learning concepts (neural networks, training, inference)
  • Experience with PyTorch or similar ML frameworks
  • Experience with Linux command line operations
  • Basic understanding of data preprocessing techniques
  • Basic knowledge of parallel computing concepts
  • Understanding of model validation and verification methods

title: How to Run and Optimize Your Workloads on the NVIDIA Grace and Vera CPUs
abstract:
Join us to explore how NVIDIA's latest generation of ARM-based CPUs can boost data center performance and energy efficiency. We'll help you optimize your applications for the current NVIDIA Grace architecture while providing insights into NVIDIA Vera CPU, the next-generation processor based on a custom NVIDIA core — available in the second half 2026. Learn practical strategies to enable your code to run efficiently on these CPUs and understand how to take advantage of the next architectural improvements. We welcome participants interested in evaluating and deploying Grace today, as well as those considering adopting Vera in the future. You should have a foundational understanding of CPU programming. Bring your application-specific and general questions — we're here to help you prepare for both current and future generations of NVIDIA's data center processors.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod C (L2)

title: How to Simulate, Train, Validate, and Deploy an End-to-End Robotics Workflow with NVIDIA Isaac
abstract:
In this full-day workshop, we'll guide you through an end-to-end robotic workflow—from simulation-based training to real-world robot deployment. You'll simulate the robot in NVIDIA Isaac Sim™, train the policies in Isaac Lab, validate the trained skills through software-in-the-loop testing, and deploy them to physical robots. Through practical exercises, you'll gain end-to-end experience in the techniques that bridge the sim-to-real gap: synthetic data generation, policy training and refinement, hardware testing on edge devices, and real-robot deployment. This workshop demonstrates how simulation accelerates robot learning and reduces the time and cost of bringing AI-powered automation to production environments.


Certificate: Upon successful completion of the assessment, you’ll receive an NVIDIA certificate to recognize your subject matter competency and support your professional career growth.


Prerequisite(s):

  • Intermediate experience with Python, including working with packages, virtual environments, and basic scripting.
  • Familiarity with 3D content concepts such as meshes, materials, transforms, and scene hierarchies from tools like Blender, Maya, or similar DCC applications.
  • Understand command-line usage for running scripts and managing project files. Understand basics of world foundation models.
  • Have familiarity with robotics or simulation workflows (for example, using simulation tools or working with physics-enabled assets).

title: How to use NVIDIA Warp to Build GPU-Accelerated Computational Physics Simulations
abstract: Discover how NVIDIA Warp enables the next generation of GPU-accelerated physics, geometry processing, and differentiable programming. This training lab introduces Warp’s core capabilities and modules, then moves into a hands-on notebook where participants build a high-performance physics solver entirely in Python. This is demonstrated through a practical computational fluid dynamics example based on a 2-D Navier–Stokes formulation. You'll leave with an understanding of what Warp is designed for, how its core building blocks work, and how it can be used alongside deep learning frameworks like PyTorch and JAX in modern computational engineering workflows.

title: How Virtual Twins are Shaping the Next Industrial Revolution
abstract:
AI-powered virtual twins, science-validated industry world models, and virtual companions are shaping the next frontier for industries.

Learn how Dassault Systèmes’ industry world models, agentic 3DEXPERIENCE platform, and virtual twin technologies—combined with NVIDIA AI infrastructure, open models, and libraries—are establishing new ways of working and transforming  biology, materials science, engineering,  and manufacturing.

title: How We Scaled RL for Agentic Coding: Lessons from Training SWE-1.6
abstract: Sharing lessons from the training of SWE-1.6.  Preview which beat top open-source models on SWE-Bench Pro. We will cover RL environments for agentic coding, a stable algorithm for async RL, how we made our training 6x faster with NVFP4 & more, and how we approach GPU allocation for async RL.

title: Hybrid Inference With SLMs on Devices and NVIDIA NIM Microservices in the Cloud
abstract: We explore how small language models (SLMs) running on small devices integrate with NVIDIA NIM microservices running in the cloud or local GPU environments to enable real-time, hybrid AI inference. Our hybrid local–cloud inference approach combines on-device processing for instant interaction with GPU-accelerated reasoning in the cloud. This solution achieves latency under 200 ms and is expected to significantly reduce bandwidth usage — potentially by up to 10× — while scaling across industries from consumer electronics to healthcare and retail. Built with NVIDIA technologies including NeMo, TensorRT, and Triton, we demonstrate how hybrid inference brings AI closer to the user, powering natural, multi-modal experiences directly on devices.

title: HyperSack: Resource-Aware Distributed Hyperparameter Optimization for Lightweight Vision and Language Models on GPUs
abstract: Hyperparameter optimization (HPO) can unlock the full potential of deep learning (DL) models, but it's one of the most compute-intensive tasks in DL. Existing HPO parallelization strategies underutilize powerful GPUs, especially for training lightweight deep neural networks. Resource-sharing mechanisms such as multi-process service can improve GPU utilization, but naive adaptations for HPO workloads lead to poor performance. We present HyperSack: a distributed HPO framework for dynamic and resource-aware scheduling on GPU-based HPC systems. HyperSack cuts the execution time of HPO workloads by orchestrating efficient placement of DL training jobs. Our tests on vision and language models HPO workloads show up to 2.8x performance improvement in execution time on AI00s, 4.0x on HI00s, and 3.9x on a combination of AI00s and HI00s using HyperSack over standard HPO parallelization methods.

speakers.name: Dhabaleswar Panda
speakers.jobTitle: Professor and Distinguished Scholar of Computer Science

title: IGX and Jetson: Autonomous Robots and Physical AI Applications
abstract:
NVIDIA IGX and Jetson are the world's leading computing platform for AI at the edge. It offers high-performance compute and advanced NVIDIA AI software stack, as well as an extensive ecosystem to help you create and take products to market. Join with NVIDIA engineers and experts to ask your questions and join discussion on topics ranging from hardware design to system software, from the latest AI models to application frameworks like Metropolis, Isaac, and Holoscan.

Please Note: Martin Doe, Dipen Patel, Anubhav Rai, Kaustubh Purandare, Ilies Chergui, Shashank Maheshwari, Aayush Pathak, Kiril Stoynov, Alexander Yu, Michael Silverman, Kenneth MacLean, Thejas Shivashankar, and Neel Patel, will be joining us for the Tuesday 3PM session only. Phil Lawrence, Amulya Yarlagadda, Mahdi Azizian, Suhas Hariharapura Sheshadri, David Barkin, Bernie Yip, Chris Bareng, Dac Tran, Gian Luca Storti, and Chitoku Yato will be joining us for the Thursday 2PM session only. We encourage you to attend the corresponding session if you'd like to hear from a particular expert directly.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Suhas Hariharapura Sheshadri
speakers.jobTitle: Director of Product Management - Robotics & Edge AI

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod E (L2)
Connect With the Experts Pod A (L2)

title: Immersive Robotics Teleop 101: Standardize Large-Scale Data Collection and Processing Pipeline
abstract: Learn to build scalable data pipelines for training robot policies using Teleop Core, an open-source framework that unifies simulation and real-world teleoperation. We will demonstrate how to standardize integrations with human interface devices such as PICO headsets and controller to accelerate the creation of very large robotics datasets.

title: Improving AIDC Operational Efficiency with AI Hardware Diagnostic Innovations (Presented by Aivres)
abstract: This session will dive into the current difficulties in addressing common data center conundrums, with a specific focus on the intricate world of AI hardware interaction and failure analysis.  With a comprehensive overview of existing strategies for effective troubleshooting and a detailed case study of real-world data center diagnostic scenarios, we will analyze the limitations of current methodologies. Furthermore, this talk will explore forward-looking perspectives beyond present-day solutions and outline the desired features and functionalities that next-gen AI hardware could incorporate to transform and simplify the diagnostic landscape. Gain actionable insights into current best practices and contribute to the advancement of shaping the future design of resilient data center hardware.

title: In-Storage ETL: Fast In-Place Dataset Transformation for Machine Learning
abstract:
Most extract, transform, load (ETL) workflows pull data out of object storage into client machines or servers for preprocessing, augmentation, or custom transformations. This lacks scalability, and unnecessary data movement often degrades performance significantly. Unlike other ETL tools, in-storage ETL performs transformations on the same machines where your data lives, minimizing redundant transfers by exploiting data locality.

We introduce an in-storage ETL system that runs I/O-intensive transformations close to the data. It supports inline transformations for real-time training, and offline batch processing where results are stored in a destination bucket. We’ll explore its architecture and benefits, highlighting linear scalability and high performance. Learn how to accelerate ETL pipelines, streamline workflows, and scale seamlessly from a real-world example of data preprocessing.

title: Industrial intelligence at gigawatt scale (Presented by Schneider Electric)
abstract: The convergence of AI and advanced simulation technologies is transforming how industries design, build, and operate both products and facilities. This session explores how NVIDIA Omniverse libraries and OpenUSD-based digital twins are enabling Schneider Electric to drive a new era of AI-driven simulations and smart buildings across all sectors—from manufacturing and energy to data centers and AI factories. Learn how these technologies create interoperable, scalable, and virtual environments that accelerate innovation, optimize workflows, and unlock unprecedented collaboration. Discover how AI factories and intelligent infrastructure can leverage real-time simulation to reduce costs, improve efficiency, and drive continuous improvement. Join us to understand why open standards and immersive simulation ecosystems are the foundation for the future of AI.

title: Industrial Takeoff: Reinventing Operations with Physical AI at Scale (Presented by Accenture)
abstract:
Industrial operations are entering a decisive new phase. As manufacturers face rising pressures on resilience, sustainability, productivity, and geopolitical exposure, emerging technologies—Physical AI, Robotics, and next generation digital twins—are converging to redefine how factories and supply chains operate.

Discover how Accenture and Airbus are reinventing operations—from foundational technologies to transformative business applications. With NVIDIA Omniverse and Isaac Sim, Accenture can enable high-fidelity simulation, synthetic data generation, and scalable AI training across interoperable industrial digital twin environments. With this foundation for Physical AI, the industry is ready for takeoff.

title: Innovating Liquid Cooling Solutions for New-Generation AI Data Centers (Presented by Aivres)
abstract: With drastically increasing power and thermal density in new-generation AI chips and servers, AI data centers face enormous challenges in effectively and efficiently cooling their AI computing infrastructure at all levels—node, rack, pod, and data center. In this session, we will share Aivres energy-efficient liquid-cooled AI server products and solutions based on NVIDIA GPUs that fully meet the needs and technical challenges of new-generation AI data centers. Building on the primary liquid-cooling loop design, we extend the discussion to the secondary cooling loops, coolant distribution units, power supply, and liquid-cooled server rack designs. Finally, we will showcase our reference solutions and deployments for new-generation liquid-cooling AI data centers.

title: Inside NVIDIA DGX AI Factory: Accelerating Networking for AI Across Cloud, Core, and Edge
abstract: AI infrastructure must deliver predictable, high‑throughput pipelines for large‑scale training and inference while enforcing strict security, data sovereignty, and multi‑tenant isolation across cloud and on‑premises environments. These demands can overwhelm traditional host‑based networking and security, reducing utilization and fragmenting policies. This session will bring together NVIDIA technologists building NVIDIA’s own DGX Cloud platform powered by NVIDIA Networking and show how they are extending the learnings to sovereign DGX AI factory solutions. Understand design patterns such as zero‑trust for hardened multi‑tenant clusters, accelerated Kubernetes networking, intelligent data platforms, and security services that enable consistent, high‑performance AI factories everywhere.

title: Inside Pixar’s RenderMan: XPU Technology and Storytelling on Toy Story 5 (Presented by Dell)
abstract: As one of the world’s most influential animation studios, Pixar has long pushed the boundaries of storytelling through technology. RenderMan has powered Pixar’s films since before Toy Story, evolving with artists, stories and hardware. That evolution continues with Toy Story 5 and the rollout of RenderMan XPU, Pixar’s hybrid CPU+GPU designed for modern production workflows. This session explores how XPU transforms look development, lighting, and final-frame rendering for artists—treating rendering not just as compute, but as an active part of storytelling through faster iteration, visual exploration, and immediate feedback. The talk also highlights Pixar’s Machine Learning Denoiser—used on every Pixar film since Toy Story 4—as a predictable, reliable artist-facing ML tool that accelerates rendering without sacrificing creative control. The session includes live demos of RenderMan XPU running on Dell Precision workstations with NVIDIA GPUs, showing how local GPU power supports performance and creative flow. Attendees will also see the Toy Story 5 trailer rendered entirely with XPU. And don’t miss the end‑of‑session giveaway—one attendee will walk away with a Dell Precision mobile workstation. Must be in the room to win.

speakers.name: Logan Lawler
speakers.jobTitle: Dell Pro Max AI Solutions Leader

title: Inside Resilient AI Factories: How Predictive Analytics and IST are Driving Next-Level Data Center Reliability
abstract: AI factories are transforming AI workloads, but maintaining uptime in these GPU-powered environments is a critical challenge—where even a single fault can disrupt weeks of progress and cost millions. Learn how NVIDIA’s advanced fault detection, predictive analytics, and innovative In-System Test (IST) facilitate rapid diagnostics, enabling organizations to quickly identify and resolve issues. By minimizing downtime and preventing costly interruptions, these technologies drive a significant reduction in total cost of ownership and deliver improved return on investment. This session will highlight how automated recovery and hierarchical diagnostics empower organizations to deploy and scale AI infrastructure with confidence and efficiency.

title: Insights From NVIDIA Research
abstract: The talk will give some highlights from NVIDIA Research for the past year. Detailed topics will be disclosed closer to the event.

speakers.name: Bill Dally
speakers.jobTitle: Chief Scientist and SVP of Research

title: Inspecting Multimodal Data with Nemotron and Gemma 3
abstract: This workshop introduces a multi-agent approach to solving complex, visual-heavy enterprise tasks. You will learn to use Google Gemma 3 as an agentic orchestrator to plan tasks, call tools, and leverage Nemotron for high-fidelity visual reasoning.

title: Inter-GPU Communication Techniques and Libraries for HPC and AI
abstract:
Discuss with experts everything related to inter-GPU communication through NVLink, Infiniband, or other networks. We'll cover all communication libraries: MPI, NCCL, NIXL, NVSHMEM and UCX. This is the perfect place to discuss performance benefits of CUDA stream/graph aware communication, GPU/Kernel initiated communication using device APIs, GPU Direct, NVLink, Infiniband, and SHARP to accelerate your deep learning training workload or your HPC application.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod F (L2)
Connect With the Experts Pod B (L2)

title: Interactive 2D–3D Visualization Interface for Digital Pathology
abstract: Explore an interactive GPU-accelerated interface that links 2D level-by-level images with 3D component renderings, making it easier for researchers and clinicians to navigate complex 3D spatial structures in dense tissue datasets, such as digital pathology.

title: Interactive Digital Twin for Fusion With Integrated Sensors and Agentic AI
abstract: The session will describe the development of the digital twin for the DIII-D Experimental Fusion Reactor. The talk will describe the development of the AI models and live sensor data integrated with the CAD data to develop an interactive digital twin.

title: Interactive Digital Twin of the Left Atrium Accelerated on NVIDIA GPUs
abstract: We created an interactive digital twin of the heart's left atrium that allows researchers to simulate and study atrial arrhythmias, and practice treating them through virtual ablation. This gives physicians the ability to test ablation techniques without performing these invasive procedures on live patients.

title: Introduction to Neural Rendering
abstract: Learn how neural rendering is transforming real-time graphics and simulation by embedding machine learning for massive performance gains and fidelity. This session introduces the core computational approaches and shows how the differentiable GPU programming layer essential to this work critically expands the high-performance compute ecosystem.

title: Keeping Pace with F1: How AI Technology Drives Performance at Speed (Presented by Lenovo)
abstract: Few environments have greater demand for rapid, relevant, and reliable operations than Formula One. In this session, you’ll get privileged visibility into how AI-ready hardware is powering high-performance workloads across the uniquely distributed, time-critical operations of Formula One racing. The session will also cover best practices for optimizing inferencing across enterprise environments, based on Lenovo AI inferencing infrastructure, workstations, and services and real-world AI use case deployments with NVIDIA.

title: LAMMPS at Mixed Precision: Speeding Up Material Science Simulations
abstract: By implementing reduced precision, we improved end-to-end simulation throughput by factors ranging from 1.8x to ~12x across a variety of interatomic potentials in the LAMMPS molecular dynamics code, as measured on an RTX Pro 6000 Blackwell Server Edition GPU.

title: Large-Scale JAX Training With Pipeline Parallelism
abstract: We'll give an overview of the first practical system for enabling pipeline parallelism (PP) for JAX on NVIDIA GPUs. JaxPP accepts simple annotations on the training step functions, and then will introduce Multiple Programs operating on Multiple Data (MPMD). This system is a compiler that works on top of JAX and its XLA compiler. We will give an introduction to pipeline parallelism and how it works on training. We'll also describe our experience of using JaxPP effectively on Cohere's training models.

title: Latency Optimization in BugNeMo
abstract: High query latency hindered NVIDIA’s BugNeMo Text2SQL co-pilot operating on millions of records. We identified and resolved three key bottlenecks—table hint injection, inefficient LIKE searches, and missing indexes—through AST-based query rewriting, full-text indexing, and targeted index creation. These optimizations reduced median latency from 26.9 to 7.8 seconds, vastly improving responsiveness and scalability of the Gen AI-powered platform.

title: Learn how New AI Coding Agents and Tools Unlock GPU Performance for Everyone
abstract: Whether you're hand-tuning CUDA kernels for the latest GPUs or vibe-coding your way to the next great AI app, intelligent developer tools are here to help. This session will put you on the fast track to bug-free GPU-accelerated code by introducing the latest developer tool technologies from NVIDIA. We'll start with a brief overview of the free tools available to all developers, and then dive deep on the latest features that provide even more insights into application behavior and performance. These include Python, Tile, and AI framework profiling enhancements, new debugging and cloud-integrated workflows, and customized AI copilots to help along the way. No matter what level you're focused on in the software stack, you'll leave this session with new ideas and next steps to unlock the full potential of the latest GPU platforms.

title: Learn Skill Abstraction From Action-Free Videos
abstract: Learning from videos is a path toward generalist robots by leveraging diverse visual data beyond traditional datasets. Videos often contain recurring skills (e.g., grasping, lifting) across tasks and environments. While skill-based methods can learn reusable behaviors, they usually require clean, action-labeled data. Conversely, existing video-learning methods often train monolithic models or focus on single-step dynamics, hindering skill composition and long-horizon planning. We propose Skill Abstraction from Optical Flow (SOF), which learns skills directly from action-free videos. SOF uses optical flow as a surrogate for action and adapts skill learning to operate on flow-based representations. It plans in skill space and translates these plans into actions. SOF improves multitask and long-horizon performance, enabling skill acquisition and composition directly from raw visual data.

title: LeHome: A Simulation Environment for Deformable Object Manipulation in Household Scenarios
abstract:
Our goal is to deliver a visually realistic and physically authentic simulation that enables robots to handle daily tasks in ordinary homes (especially deformable objects), and promote large-scale home adoption of robots by bridging the sim2real gap. Challenges include interacting with diverse, non-standard objects and adapting to dynamic environments; expensive and labor-intensive household data collection; the complexity of modeling diverse deformable objects; and the high cost, large size, and expertise of industrial robots.

We contribute diverse high-fidelity assets across four room types via NVIDIA Newton; accurate, fine-grained, and causally interactions with objects through Isaacsim Features; support for multiple robotic embodiments, especially low-cost robots; and efficient and extensive teleoperation methods for data collection with Leader & Follower, Xbox, etc.

title: LESS: Large Language Model Enhanced Semi-Supervised Learning for Speech Foundational Models Using In-the-Wild Data​
abstract: Although state-of-the-art speech foundation models (SFM) can produce high-quality text pseudo-labels, applying semi-supervised learning (SSL) for in-the-wild, real-world data remains challenging due to its richer and more complex acoustics. We therefore introduce LESS (Large Language Model Enhanced Semi-supervised Learning), a versatile framework that uses LLMs to correct pseudo-labels generated on in-the-wild data. In LESS, pseudo-labeled text from automatic speech recognition (ASR) or automatic speech translation (AST) of the unsupervised data is refined by an LLM, and further improved by a data filtering strategy. Across Mandarin ASR and Spanish-to-English AST evaluations, LESS delivers consistent gains, with an absolute Word Error Rate reduction of 3.8% on WenetSpeech, and BLEU score increase of 0.8 and 0.7, achieving 34.0 on Callhome and 64.7 on Fisher test sets, respectively.

title: Leverage Acceleration for AI and HPC in Algorithmic Trading
abstract:
Join this interactive session with NVIDIA experts to discover how the convergence of artificial intelligence and high performance computing (HPC) is reshaping algorithmic trading. As trading strategies grow in complexity, the need for high-throughput processing and advanced modeling capabilities becomes critical. Speak with our experts about the latest methods to leverage acceleration for data-intensive tasks, from training sophisticated AI models to running massive-scale simulations.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod D (L2)

title: Leverage AI and Accelerated Computing for Digital Biology: Parabricks, BioNeMo and Clara Open Models
abstract:
Join NVIDIA experts to explore the field of digital biology. Discuss the latest advancements in accelerated computing and foundation models for drug discovery and genomics — from bioinformatics tooling to protein structure prediction, molecular dynamics to molecular generation, and much more. Learn how NVIDIA BioNeMo, Parabricks, and RAPIDS-singlecell, together with NVIDIA NIM, empower developers, researchers, and enterprises to quickly create generative AI solutions across chemistry, biology, and genomics. Engage directly with our product managers, developer relations, and solution architects to address your challenges and answer your questions.

Please Note: Kushal Shah will be joining us for the Tuesday 11AM session only. We encourage you to attend this session if you'd like to hear from him directly.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod B (L2)
Connect With the Experts Pod B (L2)

title: Leverage DL to Enable Blood-Based Multi-Cancer Detection and Empower Health Systems to Reach More Patients
abstract: Multi-modal liquid biopsy tests with paired clinical data have the potential to unlock deep learning (DL) models to improve population-level multi-cancer detection to reach the 120 million people in the United States alone who are eligible for cancer screening. Such tests produce data for millions of molecules, such as cell-free DNA (and billions of bases) per patient, before incorporating EMR, imaging, and other data types. Learn more about the unique challenges and opportunities that Freenome and others are collaborating on to validate, launch, and improve ML/DL models for multi-cancer, and ultimately multi-disease, detection.

title: Leverage Physical AI to Simulate and Orchestrate Robotic Fleets for Retail Fulfillment Centers
abstract: Dive into the technology behind the new era of physical AI that's redefining Europe's supply chain and facility automation. Otto Group presents the “robotic coordination layer,” which connects robot fleets in real warehouses to a digital twin, enabling the deployment of the latest technology for intelligent automation.  Discover how Otto Groups, NVIDIA, and Reply collaborated to not only launch the latest AI and robotics systems in the field of logistics across Europe, but also create a scalable blueprint for the future of logistics.

title: Leverage the Power of Thor in Industrial Applications (Presented by Advantech)
abstract: As physical AI becomes more prevalent in industrial applications, performance, longevity, and reliability are critical. Enter NVIDIA Jetson Thor solutions from Advantech. In this session, Advantech will provide an overview of both NVIDIA IGX and Jetson Thor, discuss use cases in a variety of industries, and showcase how NVIDIA Thor platform and Advantech together solve some of the biggest challenges facing you today.

speakers.name: Jason Waldman
speakers.jobTitle: Associate Director of Embedded Business Development

title: LFQUIAD: Lookup-Free Quantized Autoencoder for Few-Shot Unsupervised Industrial Anomaly Detection
abstract: Explore how we generate realistic synthetic anomalies using diffusion-based inpainting to train a quantized autoencoder without relying on large codebooks. Learn how our LFQUIAD framework improves anomaly detection and segmentation with minimal real data, offering a scalable and plug-and-play solution for industrial and medical inspection.

title: Life Sciences: Scale Multi‑Agent for Accelerated Clinical Operation Systems With NVIDIA and AWS (Presented by Cognizant)
abstract: Discover how life sciences leaders are transforming enterprise‑scale clinical operations through multi‑agent AI systems built on NVIDIA’s accelerated computing and AWS’ cloud‑native intelligence. This session brings AbbVie and Clario to the stage to share how organizations can strategically modernize workflows, unify data and automation platforms, and unlock new operating leverage—positioning their enterprises for faster innovation, responsible AI adoption, and measurable business impact.

title: LightRV: An End-to-End Lightweight Training Solution for Open-Source Reasoning Vision-Language Models
abstract: We introduce a fully open-source, end-to-end training solution for reasoning-capable vision-language models, enabling efficient, low-cost construction of such VLMs without building from scratch. Through collaborative explorations with Zeekr, the pipeline has demonstrated promising applicability to cockpit entertainment scenarios.

title: Liquid Cooling Readiness for Vera-Rubin Reference Architectures (Presented by nVent)
abstract: Vera-Rubin deployments extend the performance envelope of direct-to-chip liquid cooling while remaining compatible with the same core liquid-cooling architecture used for Grace-Blackwell systems. This session defines what it means to be “liquid-cooling ready” for NVIDIA SuperPOD reference architectures by examining coolant distribution unit (CDU) and technology cooling system (TCS) network design across Grace-Blackwell and Vera-Rubin platforms. A properly designed TCS manifold with common CDU and rack-drop interfaces enables scalability through CDU count and rack-drop configuration, allowing systems to scale from GB200 to VR300 without changes to the underlying TCS architecture.

title: Liquid Cooling: How to Achieve 100% Heat Capture on 500kW+ Racks (Presented by CoolIT Systems)
abstract: As rack power densities surge beyond 100 kilowatts (kW), achieving 100% heat capture becomes increasingly essential for thermal management and operational efficiency. Current coldplate designs focus on high-thermal design power components such as CPUs and GPUs, but future architectures demand complete heat removal across all components. Even at 90% heat capture, a 500kW rack leaves 50kW for air cooling, which is a stretch for even the best air-cooled implementations. Enabling 100% heat capture will be critical for supporting next-generation AI infrastructure. Join us to learn how integrated cooling loops and advanced coldplate technologies enable complete heat removal across the entire server.

title: Liquid-Cooled AI for Next-Generation Platforms (Presented by Supermicro)
abstract: As AI factories scale to support larger models and higher cluster densities, liquid cooling has become essential. This session, presented by Supermicro and NVIDIA, explores how next-generation AI factories are built using liquid-cooled NVIDIA HGX systems and NVL72 rack-scale platforms, enabling dense, high-performance GPU clusters for large-scale AI training. Gain insight into Supermicro's deployment of these platforms as validated, rack-scale solutions, rather than standalone servers. We'll cover key design and deployment considerations, including direct-to-chip cooling and rack-level liquid integration, and show how liquid-cooled total solutions accelerate time-to-online while improving performance and energy efficiency for modern AI factories.

title: Liquid-Cooled AI Server Platforms for Next-Generation Performance (Presented by ASRock Rack, Inc.)
abstract: Explore ASRock Rack AI server solutions from edge to cloud and discover how ASRock Rack adopts liquid-cooling approaches in next-generation AI platforms to unlock higher performance and scalability. Learn how these liquid-cooled server designs are validated in ASRock Rack’s Liquid Cooling Lab to ensure reliability, efficiency, and readiness for modern AI workloads.

title: Live Agent Gauntlet
abstract:
Put your AI agent to the test in a competitive, benchmark-driven challenge. In this esports-style hackathon, teams are given the challenge in advance and have dedicated time to build and optimize their agents before the event.

On the day of the competition, teams will load their agents live and evaluate performance head-to-head on standardized benchmarks. Results will be tracked in real time on a public leaderboard, showcasing the strongest approaches and optimizations.

title: LLM 如何赋能量化投资
abstract:
LLM 作为 AI 技术的核心分支，正在重塑量化投资范式。LLM 具有超乎寻常的语义理解与推理能力，可以作为强大的工具模型嵌入量化投资流程。
从 LLM 的输出随机性出发，我们可以依赖 LLM 进行因子挖掘，并基于 NVIDIA NIM 和 TensorRT-LLM 加速挖掘；
从 LLM 的语义理解能力出发，我们基于 GPU 本地部署LLM解读海量文本、提取情感信息，构建或优化量化选股策略；
从 LLM 的多模态思维链出发，我们可利用 LLM 对K线图像进行交互式技术分析。LLM 正在引发一场量化投资技术更迭的革命......

speakers.name: 康 何
speakers.jobTitle: 策略首席分析师，金融工程首席分析师

title: LLM-Generated CUDA Kernels: Are We There Yet?
abstract: Dive deep into why current large language models struggle to generate performant CUDA kernels for accelerated systems, despite being capable of functional code. We'll explore the latest advancements in benchmarking, agentic frameworks, and industry efforts that are building the necessary infrastructure to close this performance gap and enable you to write "speed of light" AI-coded CUDA.

speakers.name: Mark Gabel
speakers.jobTitle: Sr. Manager, AI Quality for Development Tools

title: LoBE-GS: Load-Balanced and Efficient 3D Gaussian Splatting for Large-Scale Scene Reconstruction
abstract: Explore new techniques for load-balanced 3D Gaussian splatting that make large-scale scene reconstruction faster and more accessible. We introduce LoBE-GS, a framework that balances computation across partitions to achieve up to 2x faster training while maintaining quality—enabling real-time visualization and interactive exploration through Omniverse streaming.

title: Low-Cost Motion Learning Pipeline for Industrial Humanoids on NVIDIA Jetson Platforms
abstract: In real factories, safety regulations, security restrictions, and a lack of skilled operators make it difficult to collect and deploy motion data at scale. To address these challenges, we developed an intuitive teleoperation pipeline using a single consumer-grade head-mounted display (HMD), removing the need for external trackers or multi-camera systems. The system integrates Jetson-based inference with an RTOS-driven dual-network control architecture—EtherCAT for ALICE-M1 and RS-485 for ALICE4—enabling deterministic real-time operation. A GR00T-Mimic-inspired data augmentation method further diversifies robot trajectories without additional data collection, enhancing robustness across manipulation tasks. Experiments in industrial scenarios show stable autonomous execution and confirm the feasibility of deploying Jetson-based humanoid systems for real-world industrial applications.

title: Magic Attention: A Composable Framework for Exploring Warp-Specialized Attention on Blackwell
abstract: Optimizing attention on NVIDIA Blackwell demands more than a monolithic kernel to handle diverse models, training, inference, and RL workloads. We introduce Magic Attention, a high-performance, composable framework built on CUTLASS/CuTe that navigates the vast scheduling design space of the FlashAttention algorithm on the NVIDIA Blackwell architecture. We discuss four different forward attention kernel schedules, leveraging two Tensor Memory (TMEM) partitionings for deep pipeline overlap, and convolution-inspired runtime tensor packing. We will also discuss the use of softmax emulation and correction skipping.

title: Make AI Factories Production-Ready: Inference, Networking, and DPUs (Presented by F5, Inc.)
abstract:
As AI factories move from experimentation to production, teams quickly learn that GPU performance alone does not determine inference capacity, latency, or user experience. Inference workloads produce long-lived, bursty, stateful traffic that strains traditional networking and security architectures, reducing token throughput, increasing time-to-first-token, and introducing instability at scale.

This session explores how AI factories built on NVIDIA accelerated computing benefit from BlueField DPU-accelerated networking and cloud-native traffic management. We examine the unique demands of AI inference, how NVIDIA DPUs offload and isolate critical data paths, and how inference-aware traffic control improves performance, reduces latency, and enables consistent multi-tenant inference services in production environments.

title: Make Federated Learning Robust When Devices Join and Leave
abstract: This work introduces the first open-source framework for benchmarking federated learning (FL) under dynamic client participation, demonstrating that this practical scenario causes significant performance degradation. To address this challenge, we propose knowledge-pool federated learning (KPFL), a generic plugin that uses a shared knowledge pool and generative knowledge distillation to improve model robustness and prevent knowledge loss.

title: Making AI Agents Explainable for Manufacturing at Scale With Dataiku and NVIDIA (Presented by Dataiku)
abstract: As manufacturers accelerate toward autonomous AI agents to reduce costly unplanned downtime, trust, transparency, and safety have become the critical blockers to adoption in high-stakes environments. This session shows how Dataiku and NVIDIA, together with the Puffin Inspector framework from Dataiku’s Open Source Lab, make AI-driven scheduling explainable, governed, and ready for real-world industrial orchestration today.

title: MANGO : Thai Multi-Modal Adaptive Neural Generative Orchestrator
abstract: We explore new techniques for fine-tuning open-source multi-modal models like Qwen, LLaMA, and Gemma to understand Thai language and culture. By combining curated Thai data from the internet, university courses, and volunteer contributions, we aim to improve model performance and enable AI applications for education, travel, call-center services, and research

title: Manufacturing AI Agent and Explainable AI for Human-in-the-Loop Knowledge Transfer
abstract: Explore a Multi-Tool ReAct AI Agent that integrates XGBoost, GPU-accelerated SHAP, and LLM reasoning on NVIDIA NIM to preserve and scale tacit manufacturing expertise. We demonstrate how this agentic AI framework improves optimization, interpretability, and operational decision-making, helping manufacturers accelerate digital transformation through explainable, data-driven intelligence.

title: Manufacturing Intelligence: Agentic AI to Unlock the Next Level of Performance
abstract:
Hear about an intelligent, self-learning, and adaptive system where manufacturing expertise is accelerated, orchestrated, and augmented in real time through a unified, virtual platform (digital twin). The platform is accessible to operators, engineers, maintenance teams, and managers via natural interfaces and smart tools.
All knowledge, experience, and best practices related to production—world-class manufacturing—are transformed into structured digital models, continuously enriched by data and made available through AI agents acting as “digital mentors.”

speakers.name: Paolo Avagliano
speakers.jobTitle: Managing Director - Manufacturing and Operations Lead

title: Massive GPU Acceleration for Scalable Quantum Compilation and Graph Analytics
abstract: Subgraph isomorphism is a major bottleneck in graph analytics, scientific computing, and quantum-compilation pipelines, yet most solutions rely on backtracking that cannot exploit modern GPUs. Motivated by real quantum-compilation challenges, we present a GPU-native formulation that replaces serial search with parallel relational tabular operations built from reusable “motifs,” enabling efficient joins and filters using NVIDIA RAPIDS. Across diverse benchmarks, we achieve up to 600x speedups on a single NVIDIA H200 GPU. By combining Q-CTRL’s quantum performance management infrastructure software and NVIDIA’s GPU platforms, we demonstrate a data-centric method that simplifies high-performance graph analysis, accelerates large-scale applications, and opens a path toward emerging hybrid HPC–quantum architectures and future deployment on HPC–quantum interconnect systems like NVQLink.

title: Master AI Infrastructure At Scale: A DevOps Playbook
abstract:
Your AI factory is ready for action—now it’s time to put it to work.

In this session, two NVIDIA senior solutions architects will walk you through the essential steps for deploying and managing your AI infrastructure. Aiming at teams beginning their AI factory journey, we'll focus on real-world deployment practices and insights drawn from leading customer environments. Together, we’ll discuss how to streamline cluster onboarding, configuration, and monitoring, laying the foundation for efficient and resilient operations from Day One.

title: Master Immersive Real-Time Visual Process Understanding and Guidance
abstract:
Extended reality (XR) applications powered by agentic AI will revolutionize how frontline workers do their jobs. Join NVIDIA experts for a deep-dive discussion on how to design and train agentic AI systems for enterprise use cases. In this session, we'll share strategies and best practices for: leveraging the Nemo Agent Toolkit to build powerful, scalable, and optimized agents for XR use cases, optimizing agentic AI to accurately interpret and guide complex workflows, and deploying AI to the edge using NVIDIA Cosmos for low-latency feedback.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod D (L2)

title: MATATA: Weakly Supervised End-to-End Mathematical Tool-Augmented Reasoning for Tabular Applications
abstract: We introduce MATATA, a new way to train agentic small language models to reason through complex business documents with tables and numbers — without expensive human annotation or large models. Discover how our weakly supervised, end-to-end approach improves accuracy and computational efficiency for document understanding and reasoning tasks.

title: Maximize GPU Resources in Your Virtualized Environment: Best Practices for Sizing and Deployment
abstract:
Join this interactive session for direct insights and recommendations from NVIDIA specialists on fully utilizing your virtualized GPUs to deploy graphic-intensive apps, digital twin simulations, AI development, and more. Engage with experts who've navigated virtualization complexities, offering practical advice and proven methodologies. Bring your questions and leave with actionable strategies to enhance your virtualized data center operations.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod B (L2)

title: Maximize LLM Throughput and Improve GPU Efficiency
abstract: This session focuses on practical and system-level techniques to increase large language model throughput while reducing GPU cost and waste. It covers both training and inference optimization, with an emphasis on real-world bottlenecks such as memory bandwidth, kernel inefficiencies, batching limits, and underutilized GPUs.

title: Maximize Memory Bandwidth on Modern GPUs
abstract:
Struggling to fully utilize GPU memory bandwidth on modern architectures? In this interactive session, NVIDIA engineers will work with you in real time to diagnose and optimize memory-bound kernels. Bring your own code, performance traces, or ideas and get hands-on guidance on loop unrolling, vectorization, prefetching, and asynchronous Tensor Memory Accelerator (TMA) pipelines. Learn to recognize common pitfalls, explore TMA patterns, and apply producer–consumer pipelines to maximize throughput. Walk away with practical, actionable solutions tailored to your workloads.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod F (L2)

title: Maximize Memory Bandwidth on Modern GPUs: Practical Techniques, Patterns, and Worked Examples
abstract: Modern GPU generations (since Volta) have increased memory bandwidth faster than streaming multiprocessor (SM) count, which makes the classic “add more threads” recipe insufficient for many memory-bound kernels. This tutorial gives hands-on, implementation-focused guidance to saturate memory bandwidth on contemporary NVIDIA GPUs using loop unrolling, vectorization, asynchronous pipelining (Tensor memory accelerator, or TMA), cache prefetching, and other techniques. Through step-by-step worked examples (e.g., stencils, SpMV), we show common pitfalls (peeling, misalignment, tiny kernels) and practical fixes, plus variants of TMA usage and a producer-consumer TMA pattern you can reuse.

title: Maximize Tokens-per-Watt With AI Factory Digital Twins, From Chip to Grid (Presented by Siemens)
abstract:
AI factories are becoming mission‑critical, gigawatt‑scale infrastructure that must be engineered as a single, connected system. This session presents a “chip‑to‑grid” workflow focused on two key performance indicators: time‑to‑token and watts‑per‑token. 

It begins at the silicon layer, where AI‑native, GPU‑accelerated electronic design automation that utilizes Fuse and NVIDIA Nemotron accelerates design cycles. The workflow then extends to the factory, where Siemens Digital Twin Composer with NVIDIA Omniverse enables decision‑grade digital twins across power, cooling, and layout before construction. The session will conclude with an operational reference architecture that improves efficiency, resilience, and uptime. Join us to explore how digital twins redefine AI factory design, construction, and operations—creating a new level of operational excellence.

title: Measuring Brand, Fame, and Virality Using Spatio-Temporal Causal Graphs
abstract:
We introduce a GPU-accelerated system for decomposing engagement into Brand (persistent equity), Fame (spatial reach), and Virality (temporal dynamics). Standard analysis cannot separate these effects because they are mathematically entangled: observing any two determines the third. We address this by using spatio-temporal causal graphs that explicitly encode influence structure, with CUDA kernels for CSR propagation and distributed Shapley computation across GPU clusters.
Built on an Online Evolving Spike Neural Network distributed pipeline, the system uses variance-optimal stratified sampling for coalition evaluation and processes 100,000+ marketing time series at daily resolution via custom CUDA kernels and C++ simulation bridges. The framework enables teams to identify whether observed variation is driven by content quality, audience segmentation, or timing effects—separating forces that traditional attribution methods conflate. This turns engagement decomposition into actionable budget allocation grounded in large-scale causal inference.

title: Medical Speech and Transcription Frontiers: Transforming European Healthcare Delivery
abstract: This panel explores how AI technologies designed and developed in Europe are revolutionizing the healthcare sector, particularly in the realms of speech recognition and transcription. Panelists will discuss the challenges and opportunities of building robust, privacy-focused, and multilingual AI tools for clinical and patient-facing applications across the continent. Gain insights into cutting-edge advancements, collaboration models, and ethical considerations shaping the future of AI-driven speech solutions in European healthcare.

title: MedTech: Physical AI for Imaging, Intervention, and Robotics
abstract:
Physical AI is redefining how next-generation medical devices perceive, decide, and act in the real world. From advanced imaging systems and autonomous interventional workflows to intelligent surgical robots, developers now require a unified stack that bridges data, simulation, and real-time runtime and control. NVIDIA’s platforms — such as Holoscan, Isaac for Healthcare, Omniverse, Cosmos and Metropolis — deliver an end-to-end foundation for building and scaling physical AI: model training with domain-specific foundation models, high-fidelity digital twins for procedure-aware simulation, and deterministic low-latency deployment at the edge. Join NVIDIA experts to learn how to leverage our full-stack technologies to accelerate your development — from imaging and interventional systems to next-generation robotics — and bring safer, smarter MedTech to market faster.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod A (L2)

title: Megatron Core: Customer Stories on the Open-Source Training Framework for Ultra Speed at Scale
abstract: Join this session as Megatron core customers Reflection, Periodic Labs, XHS Rednote, and ByteDance describe their engineering use cases and best practices for pre-training their workloads. You will also hear about their contributions into this OSS project, features they leveraged for NVIDIA's latest hardware systems, and tuning for best performance at scale.

speakers.name: Santosh Bhavani
speakers.jobTitle: Sr. Software Product Manager, Deep Learning

title: MindVLA-o1：开启全能范式——下一代统一视觉-语言-动作自动驾驶大模型探索
abstract: 随着⾃动驾驶进⼊端到端基础模型时代，系统挑战体现为对推理效率、⻓时稳定性与复杂场景泛化能⼒的整体建模与权衡。本⽂介绍理想汽⻋下⼀代⾃动驾驶基础模型 MindVLA-o1：基于统⼀ VisionLanguage-Action 的 Omni 架构，在单⼀ Transformer 中联合建模感知、推理与控制。模型通过离散Action Tokens 与 MoE 实现⾼效、稳定的精准控制；采⽤快慢双系统推理，在实时性与复杂场景推理间⾃适应切换；并结合基于模型的强化学习闭环与软硬件协同设计，实现可量产、拟⼈化的端到端⾃动驾驶。

speakers.name: Ling Cai
speakers.jobTitle: 理想汽车基座模型负责人

title: Minimize Sim-to-Real Gap: Leveraging a Digital Twin Framework for New Vehicle Manufacturing Line With 28 Robots
abstract: When deploying robot motions generated with a digital twin, a large sim-to-real gap can lead to significant onsite engineering time. In this study, we collaborated with Hyundai Motors to assess the onsite deployment feasibility of the digital twin framework by applying it to a welding shop line for new vehicle manufacturing. By minimizing sim-to-real gap, the collision-free motion trajectories for each robot were deployed onsite within 18 minutes of engineering time per robot. These results highlight that the proposed framework is applicable even to large, line-scale factory environments, and our calibration method is effective for rapid onsite deployments of trajectories generated by the digital twin framework.

title: MLOps 201: Master Operational Excellence With Agentic AI
abstract:
The next leap in enterprise AI is industrializing intelligence, where automation and human input combine to improve agility, decision quality, and process efficiency. This session shows how NVIDIA applies lean manufacturing principles and agentic AI to its own AI operations through three core MLOps platforms: the Data Factory for synthetic data generation and labeling, the Eval Factory for model benchmarking, and the NIM Factory for inference optimization. 

From these real-world examples, we distill a practical playbook for representing any process in AI-native terms, identifying high-impact automation opportunities, embedding continuous improvement, and building a culture of operational excellence. The result is an AI-first organization with a repeatable model for AI-enhanced operations, one that extends beyond MLOps to transform the entire enterprise and its business model.

speakers.name: Nik Spirin
speakers.jobTitle: Director, Generative AI and LLMOps Platform

title: MLOps 202: From Models to Production AI Systems at Scale
abstract: Modern production AI is more than a foundation model. The model monolith is evolving into the data center-scale distributed system made of modular microservices. This talk shows how NVIDIA brings the full stack together by applying extreme software and hardware co-design to maximize performance across infrastructure, orchestration, and application layers. We cover an AI project journey from zero to production. Starting with a simple cloud API, we progressively add complexity while introducing key NVIDIA technologies, including NVIDIA AI Enterprise, NIM, NeMo, Run.ai, Dynamo, and GPU infrastructure, to explain how each of them addresses real engineering and operational challenges. Get a clear guide for turning AI prototypes and isolated experiments into enterprise-grade systems, and understand the decisions, trade-offs, and architectural patterns behind modern production AI.

speakers.name: Nik Spirin
speakers.jobTitle: Director, Generative AI and LLMOps Platform

title: MLOps Best Practices: Build an AI Agent
abstract:
Learn practical ways to implement MLOps first principles as you create autonomous AI agents using LangGraph and NVIDIA NIM by building an agentic system that demonstrates tool integration, multi-step reasoning, and adaptive planning. Master the foundational agent architecture that MLOps engineers use to automate tedious workflows like incident investigation, experiment documentation, and model health monitoring — then customize it for your own use cases.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Basic programming knowledge

title: Model Builders at the Frontier of EMEA’s Open and Sovereign AI Movement
abstract: Europe’s drive toward sovereign AI is redefining the way open, transparent, and multilingual large models are built at scale. This panel explores the technical foundations that drive this effort. Speakers will discuss best practices in data quality, domain adaptation, and culturally aligned evaluation for low-resource languages, showing how sovereign and open models can meet European requirements while delivering state-of-the-art performance. The discussion will also highlight how NVIDIA Nemotron enables European, Middle Eastern, and African (EMEA) developers to build and fine-tune competitive large models, accelerating innovation while reducing dependence on proprietary platforms. In addition, the panel will touch on how European developers and companies are preparing these models for real-world inference and deployment across diverse applications.

title: Multi-Modal AI Biomarker for Early Cancer Cachexia Detection Integrates Clinical, Radiologic, and Laboratory Indicators
abstract: Cancer cachexia is a multifactorial metabolic syndrome marked by substantial weight loss, skeletal muscle wasting, and systemic inflammation contributing to poor quality of life and increased mortality. No definitive biomarker exists and commonly used serum indicators, skeletal muscle metrics, and anthropometric measures overlap with other conditions, limiting diagnostic specificity. Existing composite indices (Cancer Cachexia Index and Cachexia Score) integrate multiple symptomatic factors but lack standardized thresholds and are rarely scalable for clinical use. We present a multi-modal AI biomarker for early cancer cachexia detection, integrating diverse mechanistic signals from routinely available patient data, including clinical data, inflammatory and nutritional lab markers, skeletal muscle metrics (CT scans), and cachexia symptoms extracted from clinical notes using LLMs.

speakers.name: Matthew Schabath
speakers.company: H. Lee Moffitt Cancer Center and Research Institute

title: Multi-Modal RAG: Build Agentic AI Systems With Video, Audio, and Text
abstract:
Enterprise developers are moving beyond text-based retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) to harness multi-modal data—video, audio, and text. This Connect with the Experts session explores best practices for designing and optimizing multi-modal agentic RAG systems. Learn to combine speech recognition, visual understanding, and LLMs to enable context-rich, dynamic enterprise AI applications. The discussion covers real-world implementation including vector indexing of multi-modal embeddings, latency optimization for large-scale deployments, and fine-tuning for domain-specific retrieval. Gain insights into integrating NVIDIA Nemotron models and cuVS accelerated vector search to create unified RAG pipelines that reason across diverse data types. Engage with NVIDIA experts and fellow developers to exchange design patterns and best practices for powering next-generation enterprise intelligence.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. title: Multi-Vector Variational Generative Framework for Personalized Cognitive Modeling and Training
abstract: We introduce an AI-driven framework that uses NVIDIA MONAI and GPU acceleration to quantitatively model brain aging and cognitive trajectories from MRI, addressing the limitations of subjective cognitive assessments. This timely research enables earlier risk prediction, personalized cognitive training, and future development of avatar-based interfaces powered by NVIDIA NeMo technologies.

speakers.name: Yi Tien Lee
speakers.company: Taipei Medical University Research Center for Neuroscience, TMU Ph.D. Program in Medical Neuroscience
speakers.jobTitle: Associate Research Fellow and Assistant Professor

speakers.name: Yung-Chieh Chen
speakers.jobTitle: Attending Neuroradiologist and Assistant Professor of Radiology

title: Multilingual Benchmarking and Evaluation of Safety Guardrails in Low-Resource Languages ​
abstract: Safety guardrails remain biased toward high-resource languages, leaving low-resource communities vulnerable to harmful model behavior, cultural misalignment, and legal inconsistency. We introduce UbuntuGuard, a new multilingual benchmark for safety evaluation across 11 African languages. We generate contextualized, policy-grounded dialogues using NVIDIA Nemo-Curator, with policies authored via GPT-5. Policies and dialogues are translated into languages such as Yoruba, Swahili, and others to create a diverse Multilingual Policy-Dialogue dataset. We evaluate leading guardian models, including NVIDIA Nemoguard-8B, uncovering significant multilingual safety gaps, especially under cross-lingual settings. Results show alignment between dialogue and policy language is critical, highlighting the need for adaptive, culturally grounded guardrails that operate reliably across linguistic boundaries.

title: NanoViT: Basis-Aligned Hybrid Neural Network for Jetson Devices
abstract: Computer vision tasks often demand intensive computation, even on GPU-equipped devices. To address this challenge, we present NanoViT, a hybrid architecture that integrates convolutional layers with transformers, achieving inference speeds comparable to conventional convolutional neural networks (CNNs) on NVIDIA GPUs. Unlike prior work that relies heavily on empirical experimentation, NanoViT is grounded in new theoretical insights into the role and behavior of convolution in visual classification.

speakers.name: Lucas Mohimont
speakers.company: Laboratoire d'Informatique en Calcul Intensif et Image pour la Simulation / LRC DIGIT URCA-CEA

speakers.name: Luiz Angelo Steffenel
speakers.company: Laboratoire d'Informatique en Calcul Intensif et Image pour la Simulation  /  LRC DIGIT URCA-CEA

title: Natural Language to 3D Geometric CAD: A Multi-Agent LLM Framework for Design Synthesis Using Hierarchical Decomposition
abstract: We present a multi-agent LLM framework that automatically converts natural language descriptions into parametric 3D CAD models through hierarchical decomposition. Our system uses specialized AI agents for different stages of the process, including reasoning, planning, compiling, and self-repair. The system achieves better-than 90% success rates in generating parametric CAD models with preserved dependencies, enabling the generation of editable designs. Our approach empowers domain experts to express requirements in natural language and automatically generate 3D models, accelerating the design process and democratizing CAD access.

title: Nemotron Safety Guard Multi-Modal
abstract:
As more and more contemporary LLMs exhibit multi-modal capabilities, comprehensive safety evaluation becomes critical for reputational, regulatory, and ethical compliance. Rules and regulations such as EU AI Act have stiff penalties for violations.

Existing vision language model (VLM) safety benchmarks typically cover limited safety categories such as violence, sexual content, and hate speech, and rely on third-party judge models like GPT-4 that may not align with NVIDIA safety standards. Consequently, there is a compelling need for a dedicated safety guard model and an accompanying benchmark that closely adheres to NVIDIA's internal safety policy.

title: Nemotron Unpacked: Build, Fine-Tune, and Deploy NVIDIA's Open Models
abstract: Hear from NVIDIA’s VP of Applied Deep Learning Research the story of NVIDIA Nemotron—an open-source ecosystem for the full AI life cycle. NVIDIA Nemotron is a collection of multi-modal and reasoning models, datasets, and training techniques designed to leverage NVIDIA’s experience in accelerated computing for each step of your AI journey.

title: NetMind: Telecom Agentic AI Assistant Toward Level-4 Autonomous Networks​
abstract: NetMind platform accelerates the journey to Level-4 autonomous networks, solving multiple problems related to network information retrieval, fault management, self-optimization, and energy efficiency. By integrating LLM, multi-agent, and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) with real-time telemetry, it delivers autonomous intelligence with proven results: 95% faster fault resolution, 30% peak-load reduction, and 15% energy savings. This highly scalable solution is ready for deployment across 10 international markets.

title: Network Automation Lessons Learned From Launching 20+ Multi-Tenant Clouds and AI Factories (Presented by Netris)
abstract: Both enterprises and NVIDIA Cloud Partners (NCPs) must establish consistent tenant isolation across NVIDIA Spectrum-X Ethernet, NVIDIA Quantum InfiniBand, NVLink Multi-Node, DPU, virtual, and edge networks. This requires hard isolation across bare metal, virtual machines (VMs), and container workloads on all involved fabrics to support data sovereignty and compliance requirements.

title: Neural Physics for AI-Powered Differentiable PDE-Solvers and Digital Twins
abstract: AI has revolutionized surrogate models in the field of computational physics. However, in this session we take a look at how AI tools can benefit physics-based modeling. We will introduce the neural physics approach, which writes multi-grid solvers as convolutional neural networks whose weights are set from numerical theory, rather than by training. We give demonstrations that indicate the breadth of application possible from complex environmental problems to industrial problems; demonstrate the scalability of the approach; describe the potential for optimization tasks; and discuss future possibilities of combining physics-based models (untrained neural networks from neural physics) with surrogate models (trained neural networks).

title: Neural-Based High-Resolution Weather Localization in Thailand: Downscaling and Forecasting
abstract: Explore new AI techniques for high-resolution local weather simulation in Thailand. Global weather models like GFS provide broad forecasts, but often miss local extremes. Regional models such as WRF-ROMS capture more detail, but require hours of computation, limiting use to one or two runs per day. Accelerated by NVIDIA technology, we present two AI-powered solutions for fast, high-fidelity, and energy-efficient local weather prediction: (1) Weather Downscaling, which refines GFS outputs to 3×3 km resolution, and (2) AI Weather Forecasting, which generates high-resolution 24-hour, 3×3 km forecasts in seconds on a single A100 GPU.

title: Next-Gen Data Systems: GPU Acceleration for SQL and Vector Databases
abstract:
AI applications increasingly rely on fast, scalable access to both structured data and high-dimensional vector embeddings. Traditional CPU-based systems struggle to meet these demands at scale. We'll explore how to accelerate SQL databases and vector search engines with GPUs, enabling rapid index builds, low-latency queries, and seamless integration of analytical and AI workloads. Learn how GPU-accelerated data systems unlock new performance levels for modern AI pipelines.

title: Next-Gen Digital Health Agents for Language, Speech, and Analytics
abstract:
The future of digital health is agentic. Join NVIDIA experts to discuss how AI agents are transforming the ecosystem by bridging the gap between perception and reasoning. Discuss the implementation of advanced pipelines that utilize conversational AI and speech for patient interaction, computer vision for diagnostics, and deep analytics for decision support. Whether you are a researcher or an enterprise developer, engage with our team to understand how NVIDIA NIM, blueprints, and SDKs can help you deploy robust, multi-modal agents that drive real-world clinical, research, revenue cycle, and administrative outcomes.

speakers.jobTitle: Senior Deep Learning Engineer, Speech AI

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod B (L2)

title: Next-Gen Hadoop Storage: BlueField-3 and CSAL for Performance and Resilience
abstract:
CSAL is an open-source user-mode FTL, cache, and I/O trace component accelerating big data and AI storage within SPDK (upstreamed), designed by Solidigm and commercially released on CSP Cloud. 

Traditional Hadoop storage systems face bottlenecks in high-concurrency, throughput, and low-latency demands for large-scale analytics, AI training, and real-time processing due to share-everything design. BlueField-3 + CSAL optimizes data paths for breakthrough throughput, offloads storage tasks to BlueField-3 (reduces CPU overhead by 70-80%), and redesigns HDFS with computation-storage separation for superior resource efficiency.

Triple replication, erasure coding, and RAID deliver multi-layered redundancy, ensuring high reliability, data integrity, and enterprise security across failure scenarios. Supports large-scale analytics, AI model training/inference, real-time streaming, etc.

title: Next-Generation Discovery: Agentic AI for Science, AI-Driven Simulation, and GPU-Accelerated Chemistry
abstract:
Accelerate your scientific discovery pipeline by connecting directly with experts spanning autonomous AI agents for science, AI for molecular and materials simulation, and high-performance traditional atomistic simulation. You will explore how to leverage the latest GPU-optimized tools — including NVIDIA ALCHEMI Toolkit, cuEquivariance, VASP, and GROMACS — to maximize accuracy and computational throughput for your specific research challenges.

title: Next-Generation Distributed Storage Architecture: Low-Latency and Network Optimization for AI Workloads
abstract: Dive deep into our next-generation distributed storage architecture, optimized for KVCache offloading and prefill-decode disaggregation. Explore key techniques for achieving ultra-low latency and high throughput, addressing critical I/O bottlenecks and North-South network efficiency in GPU-centric AI inference clusters.

title: Next-Generation Intelligent Surgical Robots
abstract: Explore how NVIDIA technologies are transforming the design and scalability of intelligent surgical robots, enabling software-defined, AI-powered systems that improve outcomes across the healthcare ecosystem. Learn how NVIDIA Holoscan and agentic frameworks are shaping the future of smart operating rooms and hospitals.

speakers.name: Mahdi Azizian
speakers.jobTitle: Sr. Director, Holoscan Platform and Medical Technologies

title: Next-Generation Storage Architecture With Compute-Storage Separation Based on DPUs: Redefining Data Center Storage
abstract: This poster demonstrates how to address the high coupling and performance bottlenecks inherent in traditional compute-storage systems by implementing a compute-storage separation architecture based on data processing units (DPU). It showcases practical applications delivering efficient solutions across cloud storage CSAL, XiRaid software-defined RAID, virtualized environments, and various mainstream distributed storage systems.

speakers.name: Liang Guo
speakers.company: China Academy of Information and Communications Technology Cloud Computing and Big Data Research Institute
speakers.jobTitle: Chair of the New Technologies and Testing Working Group; Chief Engineer

title: No One Will Use Your AI Agents if They Don’t Feel Human (Presented by ElevenLabs)
abstract: AI agents are getting smarter—but many still fail to earn real usage because they don’t feel human in live interaction. In this session, we’ll explore why agents need a conversational interface, what it takes to handle real-time interactions at scale, and how to build brand trust and repeat usage.

title: NVIDIA 101: From Chips to Software—How NVIDIA's AI Platform Enables the AI Revolution
abstract: Attend this introductory session for an overview of how NVIDIA’s AI platform sparked the agentic and physical AI revolution. We'll showcase real-world examples of how NVIDIA has disrupted some of the world’s major industries. An interactive Connect With the Experts session will follow the talk, where you can engage with NVIDIA’s hardware firsthand and ask any follow-up questions. Check out our Connect with the Experts session here.

title: NVIDIA 101: From Chips to Software—How NVIDIA's AI Platform Enables the AI Revolution
abstract:
This session is a continuation of the "NVIDIA 101: From Chips to Software — How NVIDIA's AI Platform Enables the AI Revolution" session led by Rambo Jacoby. In this session, you'll get the opportunity to touch and feel NVIDIA's hardware and ask any questions you may have about NVIDIA's platform, and to ask follow up questions from Rambo's earlier session, directly from Rambo himself.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod A (L2)

title: NVIDIA Certification Overview: What You Need to Know
abstract:
Considering an NVIDIA certification? Join this exclusive overview session to learn how NVIDIA’s certification program can help you validate your skills and advance your career in AI and accelerated computing.


This session provides an overview of all NVIDIA certification exams:

Associate Exams:

  • Generative AI - LLMs
  • Generative AI - Multimodal
  • AI Infrastructure and Operations
  • Accelerated Data Science (NEW)


Professional Exams: 

  • Agentic AI
  • Accelerated Data Science
  • OpenUSD Development
  • Generative AI and LLMs
  • AI Infrastructure
  • AI Operations
  • AI Networking

    • Once you’re ready, reserve your seat to take the exam onsite at GTC.

title: NVIDIA Cosmosを活用した自動運転向けデータセット基盤の実装事例
abstract:
本講演にご参加いただくことで、最先端の自動運転AI開発に必須となる、NVIDIA Cosmosを活用した高性能データセット基盤の具体的な構築ノウハウと実装事例を習得できます。 今日の自動運転AIの性能向上のボトルネックとなっている、大規模データの効率的な管理手法や、AIのロバスト性を左右する収集困難なエッジケースデータの生成・補完技術を深く理解し、皆様のプロジェクトにおける開発コストの削減とAIの実用性の向上に直結する知見を得ることが重要です。 具体的には、Cosmosの機能とAutolabeling基盤を連携させることで、実世界での収集が難しいロングテールデータを効率的に補完しつつ、ラベル付きのエッジケースデータが同時に得られる手法を解説します。
1. Cosmos Reason：高精度なデータ検索とフィルタリング
2. Cosmos Transfer：多様な仮想シーンの生成
3. Cosmos Predict：未知のシナリオの補完と拡張
これらの技術を組み合わせることで、より現実的で高性能な自動運転AIを実現するためのデータセット基盤構築の実践的な知識を提供します。

speakers.name: 梅田 弾
speakers.company: 株式会社ティアフォー

title: NVIDIA GPU 的 LLM 异步强化学习训练
abstract: 本次分享将介绍如何基于 NVIDIA Megatron-Core Trainer 与 vLLM/SGLang Rollouter 搭建一个高性能、可扩展的强化学习训练框架。该框架采用流式、全异步的架构设计，实现了样本生成与训练过程的完全解耦，大幅减轻了 rollout 阶段长尾样本带来的资源闲置问题，充分发挥 NVIDIA GPU 的并行算力。在此体系下，我们在 128 张 NVIDIA 加速卡上完成了 Qwen7B/Qwen-30B-A3B 等模型的强化学习实验，在效果持平的前提下，取得了 1.7 倍 ~ 2.7 倍的训练效率提升。

title: NVIDIA Inception Presents: Startup Pitches
abstract: Join us for an engaging hour of startup pitches showcasing groundbreaking solutions across diverse fields, including dev tools, agentic AI, and more. These dynamic presentations are part of NVIDIA Inception, a free program designed to help startups evolve faster through cutting-edge technology, opportunities to connect with venture capitalists, and access to the latest technical resources from NVIDIA.

title: NVIDIA Inception Presents: Startup Pitches
abstract: Join us for an engaging hour of startup pitches showcasing groundbreaking solutions across diverse fields, including media & entertainment, climate, energy, retail, and more. These dynamic presentations are part of NVIDIA Inception, a free program designed to help startups evolve faster through cutting-edge technology, opportunities to connect with venture capitalists, and access to the latest technical resources from NVIDIA.

title: NVIDIA Inception Presents: Startup Pitches
abstract: Join us for an engaging hour of startup pitches showcasing groundbreaking solutions across diverse fields, including AI for science, healthcare, transportaion, and more. These dynamic presentations are part of NVIDIA Inception, a free program designed to help startups evolve faster through cutting-edge technology, opportunities to connect with venture capitalists, and access to the latest technical resources from NVIDIA.

title: NVIDIA Inception Presents: Startup Pitches
abstract: Join us for an engaging hour of startup pitches showcasing groundbreaking solutions across diverse fields, including physical AI, robotics, manufacturing, and more. These dynamic presentations are part of NVIDIA Inception, a free program designed to help startups evolve faster through cutting-edge technology, opportunities to connect with venture capitalists, and access to the latest technical resources from NVIDIA.

title: NVIDIA NeMo Guardrails と Amazon Bedrock による AI ガードレール構築と検知性能評価
abstract: 本講演では、NVIDIA NeMo Guardrails と Amazon Bedrock を用いて生成 AI アプリケーションにガードレールを実装し、有害コンテンツに対する検知性能をどのように評価・改善していくかを、実例とともに紹介します。言語間（英語・日本語）の違いや、検知率と誤検知率のバランスといった複数の観点から検知性能を比較し、安全性と利便性のバランスを意識した評価プロセスの具体例を共有します。

speakers.name: 松井 凌
speakers.company: 株式会社　村田製作所
speakers.jobTitle: データ戦略推進部 データサイエンス2課 スペシャリスト

title: NVIDIA Vera: Reimagining CPUs for Agents, Reasoning, and What’s Next
abstract:
As AI workflows expand across training, inference, analytics, and reasoning-driven applications, CPUs play a growing role in AI factory performance—from orchestration and application execution to compute- and memory-intensive processing.

This session introduces NVIDIA Vera, a next-generation CPU architecture built for these demands. We'll cover the workload trends shaping CPU design and key Vera technologies, including the custom Olympus core, high-bandwidth LPDDR5X memory subsystem, and NVIDIA Scalable Coherency Fabric. Learn how Vera's architecture is designed to maximize CPU performance and efficiency across the AI factory.

speakers.name: Ian Finder
