speakers.jobTitle: Head of Product, Data Center CPUs

title: NVIDIA サービスを活用し、LLM の精度向上からフィジカル AI への応用について
abstract:
生成系 AI およびロボティクス領域では、モデル性能の向上と同時に、学習データの品質・安全性・効率性がこれまで以上に重要な競争軸となっています。
本セッションでは、NVIDIA NeMo Curator のフィルター機能を用いた高品質データの選別による学習効率化、Nemotron-Personas-Japan を活用した安全性を重視した合成データの作成、さらに NVIDIA NeMo Curator による動画データの効率的な分割・抽出を紹介します。
また、NVIDIA Cosmos を用いたロボット領域でのデータ生成を通じて、シミュレーションデータを活用した実践的な学習手法を紹介します。
LLM、VLM、Physical AI を紹介し、モデルの精度向上における、効率的な実務的アプローチを共有します。

title: NVIDIA 加速辅助驾驶 2.0 时代 AI 定义汽车的大规模应用
abstract: 本次演讲将介绍 NVIDIA 的辅助驾驶核心模块，从云端训练到车端推理的全栈解决方案，包括 NVIDIA Cosmos Reason（视频理解和 VLA 骨干网络）、Cosmos World Model（全局场景仿真）、 NuRec 神经重建和闭环仿真、ACCV-Lab 辅助驾驶训练工具包以及面向 NVIDIA DRIVE Thor 的 VLA 模型部署及展望

speakers.name: 浩楠 邵
speakers.jobTitle: 中国汽车高级解决方案架构师

title: NVIDIA NeMo を活用した半導体領域知識を組み込む小規模 LLM のファインチューニング
abstract: 本セッションでは、半導体製造装置の立ち上げ・保守作業などの支援を目的としたLLMのチューニング方法をご紹介します。NVIDIAが公開しているオープンソースの合成ペルソナデータセットであるNemotron-Personas-USA, Nemotron-Personas-Japanと合成データ生成ツールであるNVIDIA NeMo Data Designerを活用した独自データセットの構築から、NVIDIA NeMoを用いたモデルチューニングまでのプロセスを紹介し、製造業におけるAI活用の可能性を探求します。

speakers.name: Kenya Nishiguchi
speakers.company: 東京エレクトロン株式会社
speakers.jobTitle: データサイエンティスト

title: NVIDIAソフトウェアとの統合により進化する富士通の自律型AIプラットフォーム
abstract:
本講演では、富士通の自律型AIプラットフォーム「Kozuchi AI プラットフォーム」に対し、NVIDIAの多様なソフトウェア（NVIDIA NeMo、NVIDIA NIM、NVIDIA Blueprints、NVIDIA Metropolis など）をどのようにマッピング・統合することで、プラットフォーム全体の機能性や拡張性、そして適用可能なユースケースのカバー範囲がどのように進化しているのかを、全体アーキテクチャと具体的な取り組みを交えて解説します。AIプラットフォームが単体技術の集合から、学習・推論・AIエージェント構築・運用までを一体で支える基盤へと進化する中、ソフトウェアスタック全体を俯瞰した設計思想と統合アプローチを理解することは、AIを実業務で活用する上で極めて重要だからです。

Kozuchi AI プラットフォームでは、富士通独自のTakaneやマルチAIエージェント技術、セキュリティ機能を中核に据えつつ、NVIDIA NeMo によるモデル学習・カスタマイズ、NIM による推論およびデプロイ環境の標準化、Blueprint を活用したエージェント構築と最適化を組み合わせることで、信頼性と運用性を兼ね備えた自律型AI基盤の実現を目指しています。この構成は性能向上にとどまらず、AIの再利用性、スケーラビリティ、ガバナンスといった実装・運用上の課題に対応するためにも不可欠です。

特に本講演では、NVIDIA AI Blueprint for video search and summarization（VSS）の取り組みに焦点を当て、映像データを起点とした自律型AIエージェントの実装と進化を紹介します。製造現場や物流、公共空間などで生成される膨大な映像データを、VSS blueprint と Metropolis を連携させて検索・要約・理解し、業務知識やルールと結び付けて判断や提案へとつなげるアプローチは、現場AIの実用化を大きく前進させるためです。

speakers.name: Kohta Nakashima
speakers.jobTitle: 富士通研究所 コンピューティング研究所 所長 博士（工学）

title: Online Reasoning Video Segmentation with LLMs and Just-in-Time Digital Twins
abstract: Reasoning segmentation (RS) identifies and segments objects based on implicit text queries. RS is thus a catalyst for embodied AI agents, enabling them to interpret high-level commands without requiring step-by-step guidance. However, current RS approaches rely heavily on the visual perception capabilities of multi-modal LLMs, leading to several major limitations. We therefore propose an agent framework that disentangles perception and reasoning for online video RS without LLM fine-tuning. Our innovation is the introduction of a just-in-time digital twin concept, where — given an implicit query — a LLM plans the construction of a low-level scene representation from high-level video using specialist vision models. We call this approach to creating a digital twin as "just-in-time'' because the LLM planner anticipates the need for specific information and only requests this limited subset.

title: Opentrons: Close the Gap Between Intent and Execution in the Lab
abstract: AI is rapidly reshaping laboratory automation, moving beyond static scripting toward systems that can reason, perceive, and act in the physical world. In this session, James Atwood (Opentrons) explores what’s next for AI-enabled labs as large language models, computer vision, and robotics converge. He'll examine how LLMs are evolving from tools that translate scientific intent into executable workflows into components of adaptive systems that plan experiments, interact with robotic platforms, and respond to changing requirements. He'll also highlight the growing role of vision-based and multi-modal models for verification, real-time monitoring, and in-run adjustment. Looking ahead, James introduces emerging vision-language-action approaches that unify planning, perception, and execution, connecting digital intelligence with physical lab automation.

title: OpenUSD Asset Integration and Data Review With Clash Detection
abstract:
Integrate clash detection into your OpenUSD-based digital twin workflows using NVIDIA Omniverse libraries. In this lab, we’ll ensure ingested assets are run through conflict analysis using the Clash Detection SDK. You’ll create and execute collision detection queries, inspect and visualize results in an Omniverse viewport, and implement robust overlap detection in complex 3D scenes. We will learn the fundamentals of simulation for digital twins, engineering validation, and automated design verification workflows.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Familiarity with CAD workflows, 3D modeling concepts
  • Basic understanding of simulation concepts and collision detection principles
  • Experience navigating 3D viewports and interactive visualization environments
  • Intermediate scripting experience with Python

title: OpenUSD Crash Course: Build 3D Data Pipelines for Physical AI
abstract:
Discover how OpenUSD principles and Python scripting can build scalable 3D data pipelines for manufacturing, robotics, and physical AI applications. Throughout this full-day workshop, you'll learn composition arc techniques, asset hierarchy design, and performance optimization strategies essential for production pipelines. Develop practical skills in automating 3D asset workflows, managing complex scene data, and preparing simulation environments for physical AI training. This workshop directly prepares you for the NVIDIA-Certified Professional: OpenUSD Development exam.

Certificate: Upon successful completion of the assessment, you’ll receive an NVIDIA certificate to recognize your subject matter competency and support your professional career growth.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Intermediate Python experience, including packages, virtual environments, and scripting fundamentals.
  • Familiarity with 3D modeling concepts: meshes, materials, transforms, scene hierarchies, and asset organization.
  • Understanding of data pipeline concepts and file-based workflows.
  • Basic knowledge of composition and layering concepts in content creation tools.

title: Operationalize Agentic AI at Hyperscale: Microsoft Foundry in Azure AI Factories (Presented by Microsoft)
abstract:
As enterprises move from AI experimentation to always‑on, agentic systems, the challenge shifts from accessing models to operating intelligent agents reliably at scale. In this session, Microsoft will demonstrate what goes into developing purpose-built AI factories using NVIDIA technology to run production‑grade agentic AI workloads.

We begin with the design principles behind Microsoft’s AI factory approach—how NVIDIA GPU architectures are translated into deployable, repeatable platforms within Azure, for long‑running, multi‑step agent workflows across thousands of GPUs, and heterogeneous networks. See how agent behavior at scale reshapes system design, where performance, reliability, and operations must work together to keep agents responsive and continuously available.
 
Then we'll dive deeper into Microsoft Foundry as the control plane for agentic AI. Foundry enables teams to discover, deploy, and operate frontier and open models, including NVIDIA Nemotron reasoning models and NIM microservices, and turn them into AI agents. By unifying model catalogs, agent services, governance, and infrastructure, Microsoft Foundry and NVIDIA help enterprises move from single‑prompt interactions to coordinated, multi‑agent systems in production.
 
We conclude with an inside look at how Microsoft operates its global NVIDIA fleet through Foundry to deliver predictable performance, security, and cost efficiency, transforming AI factories into production platforms that power enterprise‑grade agentic AI at scale.

speakers.name: Anand Raman
speakers.jobTitle: General Manager for Cloud and AI Platforms

title: Operationalizing AI at Scale: NVIDIA’s End‑to‑End Journey to an Enterprise AI Factory
abstract: Learn how NVIDIA’s IT organization engineered an on‑premises AI factory to deliver agentic AI with enterprise‑grade security, reliability, and governance. We’ll walk through the end-to-end technical stack behind NVIDIA’s internal AI platform, from our on-prem enterprise AI factory infrastructure to containerized agentic applications. This includes scalable inference, secure enterprise data access, agent development and evaluation, standardized agent interaction, memory, runtime execution, and data flywheels for continuous improvement. You’ll see how NVIDIA has deployed AI agents for the chip design, IT, supply chain, and developer and employee productivity domains.

title: Optimize Biologics Design Workflow With Auto-Tuned Models and Multi-Agent Systems
abstract:
Applying AI to antibody therapeutics often presents significant engineering challenges—particularly in effectively linking structure prediction, molecular generation, and property optimization into a cohesive pipeline. Learn how breakthrough AI frameworks can be leveraged to architect a zero-code antibody design and optimization workflow. We'll demonstrate how multi-agent capabilities enable intelligent interpretation of complex R&D requirements—from de novo generation to multi-objective optimization—without the need for manual coding.

We'll also share key learnings and identified bottlenecks while addressing the industry-wide “ADA prediction gap” through the construction of a high-fidelity immunogenicity simulation framework that models human immune responses, including MHC binding, APC presentation, and T-cell activation.

title: Optimize Deep Learning Kernels on NVIDIA GPUs With FlashInfer and cuDNN
abstract:
Modern AI workloads live or die by the performance of a handful of critical GPU kernels. In this Connect With the Experts session, there are no slides—just open, in-depth discussions with NVIDIA engineers about FlashInfer, cuDNN, and the optimized kernels that power today’s models. Bring your hardest performance questions, unusual model shapes, and kernel-level challenges to talk through how choices around attention, Tensor operations, memory access patterns, fusion, and precision affect behavior on current GPU architectures. The conversation will center on your real-world scenarios: what limits you’re hitting, what you’ve already tried, and which directions are most promising to explore next. You’ll walk away with concrete ideas and heuristics you can apply later with your own tools and workflows, plus pointers to resources for going deeper.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod C (L2)

title: Optimize KV Caches for LLM Inference: Dynamo KVBM, FlexKV,  LMCache
abstract: This tutorial provides a practitioner-led deep dive into why KV-cache design is a first-order performance lever for production LLM inference, directly impacting throughput, tail latency, and GPU utilization. Drawing on real production experience, the session walks through KV-cache design principles and implementation approaches used in FlexKV, LMCache, and Dynamo KVBM. The discussion highlights practical lessons from deploying KV caching with modern attention patterns such as MQA, GQA, DS3.2, and speculative decoding. You'll leave with actionable recipes, code patterns, and profiling techniques to integrate KV caching into existing inference engines and scale beyond a single node.

title: Optimize Mixture-Of-Experts for Low-Latency Inference Decode on Blackwell
abstract: This ninja-level tutorial will guide you through the techniques to build a low-latency inference kernel for Blackwell using CUDA and PTX. You will learn about techniques like programmatic dependent launch, prologue minimization, lightweight CGA parallelism, and operand swapping in the Blackwell Tensor Cores.

title: Optimize NVFP4 Training and Inference at Scale Without Losing Quality
abstract:
NVFP4 and MXFP8 are the new numerical formats in Blackwell to significantly speed up LLM training and inference versus previous formats based on FP8, BF16, or FP16. In this session, we’ll discuss how to achieve this speedup without compromising the stability of the training and model accuracy. We’ll bring experts with deep knowledge on both the hardware and the software side, so that you learn how to enable NVFP4 and MXFP8 on your GPU to reduce memory footprint and accelerate training and inference.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod C (L2)

title: Optimize Performance of Vision AI Models on the Edge
abstract: As vision AI continues to expand into real-world applications—from multi-modal systems like vision language models (VLMs) to depth-aware robotics using stereo vision—optimizing model performance on edge devices has become mission-critical. This talk dives into practical strategies for deploying high-performance vision models on constrained hardware. We'll explore techniques for customizing large-scale VLMs while maintaining multi-modal alignment, and optimizing depth estimation models for real-time stereo processing with limited compute and power budgets. You'll leave with a clear understanding of how to balance accuracy, latency, and resource usage when bringing advanced vision capabilities to the edge.

title: Optimize PyTorch Models for High-Performance Inference with Nsight Deep Learning Designer
abstract:
Learn how to use a graphical user interface-based integrated development environment (IDE) purpose-built for deep neural network developers to manage the end-to-end process of going from PyTorch to a deployment-ready model that yields the best inference performance. Learn how to use a GPU-based performance profiler to guide sound decision-making for model optimizations.

Prerequisite(s):

  • The core concepts of PyTorch and Deep Learning are needed to get through this course.

title: Optimizing $1 Trillion in Payments: From Contextual Bandits to Foundational Models
abstract: Making split-second decisions that balance fraud, cost, and conversion is central to global payments. This session explores how machine learning techniques—such as reinforcement learning, contextual bandits, and transformers—can be applied to optimize decision-making at scale. Gain insights into deploying low-latency models in production environments, as well as research approaches for moving from manual feature engineering to self-supervised representation learning.

title: Orchestrate Next-Generation AI Workloads With Open-Source Slurm
abstract: Scaling next-generation discovery requires more than increased computational power; it also depends on flexible, reliable workload orchestration at large scale. This session examines how Slurm can be used to manage traditional HPC jobs, Kubernetes-based AI workloads with complex topology requirements, and converged environments in a single ecosystem. We will outline the current architecture, recent enhancements, and ongoing community-driven work that target higher efficiency, portability, and interoperability for supercomputing workloads over the coming decade.

speakers.name: Tim Wickberg
speakers.jobTitle: Director, System Software, Slurm and Slinky

speakers.name: Danny Auble
speakers.jobTitle: Head Director for Slurm and Slinky

title: Own Your AI: Beat Frontier Models With Your Own Data (Presented by Fireworks AI)
abstract: The best AI companies aren't renting intelligence from closed model APIs—they're building their own. In this session, Roberto from Fireworks AI busts three common myths about fine-tuning open models: that closed models are always better, that fine-tuning is hard, and that you need massive labeled datasets. Drawing on real production examples from real-world examples from clients, he'll show how supervised fine-tuning, direct preference optimization, and reinforcement fine-tuning turn your production data into a compounding moat—and why the best model for your product is the one you own. Powered by NVIDIA hardware.

title: PAI 集成 NVIDIA Physical AI：驱动数据合成、模型训练测评一体化 PAI Integrates NVIDIA Physical AI: Power E2E SDG, Model Training & Evaluation
abstract: 自 2025 年，人工智能平台 PAI 融合 NVIDIA Physical AI 领域的多项技术，服务中国及全球的具身智能、自动驾驶及 AGI 大模型团队。此分享将详细介绍 PAI 的 AI 云服务能力和 NVIDIA Physical AI 如何紧密协同，依托 PAI 的高性能计算资源、分布式计算服务以及开箱即用的开发平台，解决数据合成、模型训练、模型测评的实际问题。 同时还将隆重推出 2026 年 PAI & NVIDIA Physical AI 带来的最新升级能力。Since 2025, PAI (Platform for AI) has integrated advanced NVIDIA Physical AI technologies to empower Embodied AI, Autonomous Driving, and AGI teams worldwide. This session provides a technical deep-dive into the seamless synergy between PAI’s cloud-native AI infrastructure and NVIDIA Physical AI, strategically leveraging PAI’s advantages in high-performance computing resource, distributed computing service, and easy-to-use development platform to resolve critical bottlenecks in Synthetic Data Generation (SDG), model training, and performance benchmarking. Furthermore, we will unveil the 2026 roadmap and the latest full-stack feature upgrades.

speakers.name: Boyuan Huang
speakers.jobTitle: Product Director of Big Data and AI Platform

title: Parallelization of Softened Gauss on GPU
abstract: We implemented a novel CUDA algorithm for simulating the motion of stars around supermassive black holes in galactic centers, or the motion of planetesimals in solar systems. Our approach enables the evolution of such systems over millions to billions of years, achieving simulation runtimes of days to weeks on GPUs, tasks that would otherwise take prohibitively long on CPUs. Our implementation demonstrates speedups of over 10x when comparing top-tier CPUs to the leading NVIDIA GPU (B200).

title: Parameterized CUDA Graph Launch in PyTorch: CUDA Graphs Without the Pain
abstract: Modern GPUs have gotten so fast that CPUs cannot launch new work to the GPUs fast enough, which reduces overall efficiency. CUDA Graphs can eliminate CPU overhead completely, but have traditionally been hard to use, so let’s break down the problems and fixes for them, focusing on PyTorch in particular.

title: Performance Optimization of cuTile Kernels
abstract: This tutorial guides you through the implementation of Mixture of Experts (MoE) using cuTile, focusing on how to express complex dataflows—including matrix products, gather and scatter of tokens, dynamic routing—directly in terms of multidimensional tiles. Explore how the cuTile compiler automates the most difficult aspects of GPU optimization, such as asynchronous memory movement, software pipelining, and Tensor Core utilization. By the end of the session, you'll understand how to move from a high-level algorithm description to a production-quality kernel that achieves near-peak performance while remaining readable and maintainable.

title: Performance Without Compromise: How Memory Innovation is Central to AI System Effectiveness (Presented by Micron)
abstract: Artificial intelligence is driving unprecedented computational demands that rely on massive data access at low latency. Learn how advanced semiconductor technologies—high-bandwidth memory, low-power DRAM, and next-generation storage—have become critical to meeting these computing challenges in AI systems. We will spotlight how Micron’s innovations reduce power consumption in large-scale AI training and inference systems while delivering the bandwidth to advance next-generation generative AI.

title: Persona-Aware AI: Fine-Tuning Language Models With Reinforcement Learning From Human Feedback (RLHF)
abstract:
RLHF Persona Adaptation is a practical way to fine-tune foundational AI models so chatbots can perfectly match specific user personas and domains. 

From a cold start (or with some synthetic dialogues to get going), we tune the model using human feedback. Once it's good, we register it and put it into action. As people interact with it, we collect both explicit and implicit feedback. If we spot any changes, that triggers a scheduled retraining, creating a continuous loop of personalization. 

Accelerated by NVIDIA NIM, this system has a consistent tone, style, and content, while keeping everything governed through its model registry and data store. When tested on datasets for business, technical support, and customer service, our RLHF-tuned versions consistently beat the base model. They performed best in their specific target domains, improving response quality and user engagement.

title: Physical AI at Scale: Turn Real-World Intelligence Into Enterprise Advantage (Presented by Tata Consultancy Services)
abstract:
Physical AI moves enterprise intelligence into real-world systems that sense, reason, and act. Scaling from pilots to enterprise operations remains challenging. In this session, TCS and a global enterprise discuss how Physical AI is operationalized using NVIDIA accelerated computing to deliver measurable outcomes across physical operations.

The session walks through a representative use case integrating Physical AI assets—combining real-time sensing, simulation, and decisioning using NVIDIA Omniverse for digital twin simulation, Isaac Sim for robotics and physical system modeling, NVIDIA Metropolis VSS Blueprint for video analytics AI agents, and NVIDIA NIM for optimized inference. Speakers share production learnings on integrating physical data streams and sustaining performance and reliability at scale.

Attendees leave with a grounded view of how to scale Physical AI in production and turn real-world intelligence into enterprise advantage.

speakers.name: Sreenivasa Chakravarti
speakers.jobTitle: Vice President and Global Head - IoT & Digital Engineering

title: Physical AI for Healthcare Robotics—Part 1: Simulation-First Design and Accelerated Development
abstract: Join Part 1 of a two-part curated session on how simulation-first design is changing the way healthcare robots are built. You’ll see how teams are using NVIDIA Physical AI—spanning world foundation models, physics-based simulation, synthetic data, and real-time sensor-to-action deployment—to move faster with less risk. This session focuses on how simulation and digital twins are being used in practice to speed up development, test decisions earlier, and optimize systems long before they reach the operating room

title: Physical AI for Healthcare Robotics—Part 2: Closing the Sim-to-Real Gap and Real-World Deployment
abstract: Join Part 2 of this two-part curated session on what it takes to bring simulation-trained robotics into the real world. This session looks at how teams are translating behaviors developed in simulation into reliable clinical performance. We’ll cover practical approaches to closing the sim-to-real gap, along with the real challenges of deploying safe, robust, low-latency sensor-to-action systems in live healthcare environments.

title: Physical AI for the Real World: A Vision From NVIDIA Robotics Research
abstract: One of the hardest problems in physical AI is transforming robots from task-specific machines to general-purpose, intelligent collaborators. NVIDIA Research is pioneering a unified AI- and simulation-driven approach to solve this challenge, working from theoretical advances all the way to high-performance deployment. Join this session to learn about research breakthroughs in sim-to-real, real-world learning, model architecture, and model training, as well as key milestones on the path to generalist embodied intelligence for real-world applications. Gain perspectives about where robotics is headed, what technical hurdles remain, and how the next wave of research innovation could unlock entirely new markets.

title: Physical AI in Enterprises: What’s Real, What Scales, What’s Next (Presented by Deloitte)
abstract:
Top leaders from Siemens and Volkswagen come together to explore how physical AI, Siemens’ industrial digitalization portfolio, and digital twins built on OpenUSD and NVIDIA Omniverse libraries are redefining the future of automated virtual commissioning. This executive conversation highlights how physics-accurate digital twins, real-time interoperability, and closed-loop automation are enabling faster engineering cycles, higher production quality, and more resilient manufacturing operations. 

The panel will examine how combining Siemens’ end-to-end automation and life-cycle platforms with real-time simulation, powered by NVIDIA libraries and accelerated AI infrastructure, creates a unified environment where design, controls, and operations continuously learn from each other. Together, Siemens and Volkswagen will share strategic insights on how these technologies are accelerating the shift from simulation to scalable, AI-driven production systems across global manufacturing networks.

title: PinRec: Outcome-Conditioned, Multi-Token Generative Retrieval for Industry-Scale Recommendation Systems
abstract: Our poster introduces PinRec, a system that fundamentally redesigns recommendation retrieval using an LLM-like architecture to generate a sequence of items a Pinterest user is likely to engage with, unlike a more standard two-tower retrieval architecture. Deployed on NVIDIA L40S GPUs with innovations such as outcome-conditioning, multi-token generation, and a fully torch.compiled architecture, PinRec boosted key online metrics by 2-4% or more for hundreds of million users, making it one of the first industrial-scale production systems for generative retrieval.

title: Pioneering AI-Driven Engineering: Agentic Optimization for FEA-Driven ISO Standard Validation
abstract: New solutions for the time-consuming and error-prone process of safety-critical engineering validation are here: we demonstrate an agentic AI system that delivers a 7,200% engineering speedup and a 75% error rate reduction in validating complex ISO standards and finite element analysis (FEA) simulations. Explore how this pioneering, end-to-end system integrates AI agents with proprietary engineering workflows to enable faster iteration and increase the reliability of your high performance compute-driven design cycles.

title: Pioneering Autonomous Science: Toward a Unified Cross-Lab Workflow
abstract: This session brings together leading labs to showcase how they are using autonomous AI and high-performance computing to build scalable, reproducible data pipelines from edge to HPC. See real-world demonstrations of agentic AI automating complex, multi-facility workflows, optimizing resources, and accelerating scientific discovery with NVIDIA’s advanced frameworks. The featured labs will share insights on transparent resource management, rapid deployment, and collaborative experimentation that can be reproduced and scaled globally. Learn how these approaches unlock innovative science through seamless orchestration, portability, and reproducibility.

title: Pioneering NVIDIA DGX GB300 Liquid-Cooled Deployment in EMEA (Presented by PNY)
abstract: Join us to explore how innovative collaboration drives real-world impact. We’ll dive deep into how our joint work with WWT, VERTIV, and NVIDIA is redefining performance, efficiency, and scalability through one of the first NVIDIA DGX GB300 liquid-cooled deployments in Europe, the Middle East, or Africa (EMEA). You’ll gain valuable insights into how this partnership turned complex challenges into breakthrough results, improving AI infrastructure and accelerating outcomes. Through a customer testimonial journey, we’ll highlight practical lessons, measurable benefits, and how you can apply these insights to your own AI and data center strategies.

speakers.name: simon horton
speakers.jobTitle: EMEA DGX Sales Lead and AI Sales Specialist (North Europe)

title: PMPP Edition 5 Unveiled: Ask Dr. Wen Mei Your Questions
abstract:
Join Dr. Wen-Mei Hwu and NVIDIA experts for a live Q&A exploring key updates in the CUDA ecosystem since the last edition of his book Programming Massively Parallel Processors (PMPP). This session offers a rare opportunity to engage directly with Wen-Mei and his team of CUDA engineers, who will answer technical questions, share new features introduced in PMPP Edition 5, and discuss emerging topics under consideration for the sixth edition.

Important:Q&A with NVIDIA Experts are live, interactive sessions where attendees can ask questions directly to a panel of NVIDIA experts in a group setting. The experts address one question at a time for everyone in the room, so each answer is shared with all participants. This format is designed to deliver broadly relevant insights, turning every question into a chance for collective learning.

speakers.name: Wen-Mei Hwu
speakers.company: Senior Distinguished Research Scientist and Senior Research Director at NVIDIA

title: Polar Deep Learning on GPUs: Drone–Satellite Multispectral Fusion for Vegetation Mapping
abstract: We present a GPU-powered deep learning workflow that fuses drone and Sentinel-2 multispectral imagery to map Antarctic vegetation at 1 meter resolution. This approach improves large-scale ecosystem monitoring and reveals links between vegetation dynamics and hydrological change in polar environments.

title: Post-Training Nemotron With RL: Multi‑Environment Training for Reasoning and Tool-Using Agents
abstract:
How do we move beyond simple instruction-following to models that truly reason, plan, and use tools? The answer lies in the rigor of the post-training pipeline. In this session, we unveil the technology behind Nemotron’s reasoning and tool use capabilities. While we will cover the essentials of imitation learning and synthetic data generation, the spotlight will be on the frontier of reinforcement learning (RL).

We will demonstrate how multi-environment RL training serves as the catalyst for unlocking multi-step reasoning and robust tool use. By analyzing the interplay between reward modeling, reinforcement learning from human feedback, and environment-aware training, we will show how Nemotron achieves superior performance in complex agentic workflows. Join us to explore the specific techniques NVIDIA uses to align models not just for chat, but for cognitive action.

title: Power Solution for NVIDIA Vera Rubin and 800 VDC AI Rack Architecture (Presented by LITEON)
abstract: The great performance leap of the Vera Rubin servers also brings great challenges for power design. With the power increase of the rack versus GB300, the power shelf, after being upgraded, needs to stay precisely regulated under the increased load dynamics of AI workloads and be friendly to the utility. Under close cooperation with the NVIDIA team, LITEON jointly developed a 3RU 110kW power shelf with three-phase AC input PSUs and control system, which can meet the rigorous power management requirements at both the input and output ends. When pushing the IT rack power level to 1MW and beyond, the 800 VDC solutions prevail, and LITEON has several different power levels under developing (480Vac to 800 VDC,330kW, 660kW and 1.2MW). For the 800 VDC step-down, LITEON also provides options including DC/DC power shelf and power brick modules, with boosted power density.

title: Power-Efficient AI in Space: THOR-SR Mission NVIDIA Jetson Orin NX for Onboard Computing
abstract:
The THOR-SR (Terrestrial Gamma-ray Flash [TGF] and High-energy Astrophysics Observatory for gamma Rays on board Space Rider) is a high-energy astrophysics technology being developed for flight in low Earth orbit aboard the European Space Agency’s Space Rider vehicle during a two-month mission planned for Q4 2027. THOR-SR employs cutting-edge Timepix semiconductor detectors to enable high-resolution observations in gamma-ray astrophysics, TGFs, space radiation, and space-weather monitoring.

We address the integration of AI in space missions, which requires HPC under stringent power and radiation constraints, with a detailed characterization of the Jetson AI platform optimized for power-limited, non-radiation-hardened environments. Such edge devices can sustain full AI workloads within a 20–25 W power envelope, providing a viable alternative for next-generation space missions.

speakers.name: Gabriel Falcao
speakers.company: Instituto de Telecomunições, University of Coimbra, New York University Abu Dhabi
speakers.jobTitle: Associate Professor and Visiting Associate Professor

title: Powering Agentic AI in Automotive: From Enterprise and Manufacturing Operations to In-Vehicle Intelligence
abstract: Automotive AI is rapidly evolving from isolated pilots into end-to-end, production-scale deployments—spanning manufacturing operations, enterprise workflows, and in-vehicle intelligence. In this session, industry leaders from ServiceNow, Visteon, Lyft, and Tata Consultancy Services share how NVIDIA Nemotron models are being operationalized across the automotive value chain to deliver measurable business, operational, and engineering impact.

speakers.name: Sri Subramanian
speakers.jobTitle: Global Head of Generative AI, Automotive

speakers.name: Manoj Chathankulangara Rajan
speakers.jobTitle: Head, AI/Gen AI and Autonomous Driving COE , Industrial Autonomy and Engineering

title: Powering Agentic Commerce with Open-Source AI: Easy to Build, Train, and Deploy
abstract: PayPal is leveraging NVIDIA’s open-source AI models to accelerate the evolution of agentic commerce—a new era where intelligent digital agents autonomously handle commerce tasks across industries. By integrating NVIDIA’s Nemotron and reasoning AI frameworks, PayPal enables businesses to automate customer engagement, personalize financial recommendations, and optimize payment flows in real time.

speakers.name: Farhad Farahani
speakers.jobTitle: Head of Agentic Commerce and Personalization

title: Powering Research: Real Academic Use Cases With NVIDIA Accelerated Computing and AI
abstract:
Effective use of AI, accelerated computing, and advanced infrastructure is essential to drive research innovation and scientific breakthroughs. Researchers need to focus on sustainability, funding approaches, and strategic collaborations to maximize the impact of their work and fully leverage NVIDIA technologies.
  
This panel will provide successful research use cases of accelerated computing, showcasing how to better advocate and utilize research infrastructure available at academic institutions.

title: Powering Specialized Agents: Architecting Synthetic Data Pipelines with NVIDIA Data Designer
abstract:
Building a truly performant LLM agent means fine-tuning for your unique use-case. This requires training data that span multiple specialized domains or tools; for most real-world applications, the data you need simply don't exist. This hands-on workshop leverages NVIDIA Data Designer, our recently open-sourced, state-of-the-art framework, to bridge this gap. We'll cover the latest techniques developed at NVIDIA for producing high-quality reasoning data, including STEM and tool-calling workflows used to scale Nemotron models. By the end of this session, you will gain the skills to speed up data experimentation and drive innovation in your own AI projects.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Basic Python and Jupyter notebooks

  • Training or fine-tuning LLMs (conceptually)

  • Using datasets for supervised fine-tuning and evaluation (classification, generation, or instruction-following tasks)

  • Optional: prior exposure to NVIDIA NeMo, Hugging Face datasets/models, or other LLM fine-tuning stacks.

title: Powering the AI Era in Genomics With Advanced Next-Generation Sequencing Technologies
abstract: Learn how low-cost and high throughput next-generation sequencing from Ultima, combined with NVIDIA’s accelerated computing technologies, is shaping the future of genomics. Discover how these innovations enable research at an unprecedented scale across academic data generation, drug discovery, and diagnostics. We will discuss how NVIDIA’s accelerated computing technologies power Ultima’s roadmap and latest technology advancements, opening new applications areas like virtual cell modeling, population scale genomics, and cancer detection.

title: Powering the AI Factory: From Platform Integration to Scalable AI Data Center Solutions (Presented by Pegatron)
abstract:
As AI workloads continue to grow in size, power density, and complexity, data centers must evolve beyond traditional architectures. This session explores how PEGATRON is enabling next‑generation AI factories through automated manufacturing and tightly integrated server platforms.

From accelerated rack‑level deployment to end‑to‑end infrastructure integration, PEGATRON delivers a comprehensive solution spanning compute, networking, and storage—engineered to interconnect racks and clusters in modern AI data centers.

title: Practical Applications of NVIDIA 3 Computers in Vision, Robotics, and Edge (Presented by EDOM Technology)
abstract: This session explores how NVIDIA 3 Computers—Train, Simulate, and Deploy—enable enterprises to turn AI models into real-world applications across computer vision, robotics, and edge AI. Learn how this end-to-end architecture accelerates AI deployment, reduces risk, and helps organizations scale AI from experimentation to production.

title: Prioritize Accelerated Compute: Maximize Impact in a Supply-Constrained World (Presented by PwC)
abstract:
In today’s economy, accelerated computing is the engine behind AI, industrial optimization, and next-generation digital platforms. Yet demand for advanced chips continues to outpace supply, forcing enterprises to make sharper decisions about where their computing resources create the greatest impact. 

In this forward-looking conversation, sector leaders from industrial products, energy, and technology will explore how organizations can prioritize their accelerated computing resources across competing use cases. Rather than relying on static forecasts, the discussion will introduce a practical framework for aligning GPU deployment with business value, operational resilience, and broader societal needs. The goal is not simply to manage existing resources, but to ensure that compute capacity is directed toward the most transformative and economically meaningful applications. 

Designed to be timely and actionable, this session will help you move from reactive allocation to strategic prioritization in an environment defined by rapid technological change.

title: Private AI: Run Modern Models On-Prem Without Moving Sensitive Data (Presented by Cirrascale)
abstract: Explore why more enterprises are shifting AI workloads closer to protect sensitive company data and how to operationalize Private AI without sacrificing performance. In this session, we’ll break down the real-world drivers behind on-prem and private deployments (data sovereignty, IP protection, regulated workloads, and risk reduction), then walk through a practical blueprint for running modern models with full control of data. Attendees will leave with a clear framework for model selection, secure deployment design, and operational controls so teams can confidently deploy high-performance inference in private environments while keeping sensitive data in place and minimizing exposure.

title: Private Watch Party: An Introduction to Robot Simulation
abstract:
A GTC Session Watch Party is a replay of an original GTC talk hosted by our NVIDIA Team. This is an interactive session and we encourage you to join the discussion with any comments or questions. Please note that the original speakers of the talk listed below may not be in attendance.

Hosted by:

Somnath Jona,Solution Architect,NVIDIA

Physical AI is transforming how robots interact with the world. Building these advanced systems requires three computers: compute for training, advanced simulation for validation, and efficient edge deployment. With a simulation-first approach, robots can train across millions of scenarios, generalize faster, and adapt to real-world complexity. In this technical deep-dive session, learn core robotics simulation workflows, from building virtual environments to configuring robots, sensors, and physics, using NVIDIA Isaac Sim and Isaac Lab, built with NVIDIA Omniverse libraries. You'll also explore how to generate and leverage synthetic data with NVIDIA Cosmos world foundation models to train and validate robot perception and control policies before deploying them in the real world.

A “Join Watch Party Now” link will appear below, 15 minutes before the session start time. Click on the link to launch Microsoft Teams on your computer or from your web browser.

title: Private Watch Party: MLOps 101: Platforms and Processes for Building AI
abstract:
A GTC Session Watch Party is a replay of an original GTC talk hosted by our NVIDIA Team. This is an interactive session and we encourage you to join the discussion with any comments or questions. Please note that the original speakers of the talk listed below may not be in attendance.

Hosted by:

Cuong Vu, Enterprise Business Manager, NVIDIA

 As AI adoption accelerates across every industry, teams are discovering that model quality alone is not quite enough to deliver real value. Scalable and reliable AI systems depend on robust MLOps that streamline the entire life cycle, from data to deployment. At the same time, the field is undergoing a major transformation driven by agentic AI, advanced reasoning, multi-modality, and new patterns for large-scale, disaggregated inference. This session introduces the foundational patterns of MLOps while also exploring how these new capabilities reshape AI design and operational strategy. We'll examine several use cases to break down the process and ideal platform for MLOps in this new era. Whether you're launching your first AI initiative or modernizing your infrastructure, this session provides the technical blueprint and best practices you need to operationalize AI at scale. /p>

 A “Join Watch Party Now” link will appear below, 15 minutes before the session start time. Click on the link to launch Microsoft Teams on your computer or from your web browser.

title: Private Watch Party: NVIDIA Agentic AI solutions for Healthcare & Finance
abstract:
A GTC Session Watch Party is a replay of an original GTC talk hosted by our NVIDIA Team. This is an interactive session and we encourage you to join the discussion with any comments or questions. Please note that the original speakers of the talk listed below may not be in attendance.

Hosted by:

Ajeet Singh,Docker Community Leader,NVIDIA

In this session, we'll dive into designing an agentic system to support financial analysis and advisory. Inspired by NVIDIA AI-Q Research Assistant blueprint, and leveraging NVIDIA's multi-modal retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) pipeline, our system not only provides a tool that can take financial analysis—one of the core tasks in finance—to the next level by supporting internal experts, but also makes an important step toward building a sovereign agentic architecture.

A “Join Watch Party Now” link will appear below, 15 minutes before the session start time. Click on the link to launch Microsoft Teams on your computer or from your web browser.

title: Profiling Python and AI workloads with Nsight Compute
abstract:
Learn how to use the Nsight Compute tool to profile CUDA and AI GPU kernels in the Python framework of your choice: Numba, Triton or PyTorch. Understand the optimization workflows provided by NVIDIA Nsight Compute UI and its ncu command line interface. Use the new scoreboard dependency analysis view and source page improvements like opcode to metric pipeline associations. Learn how to use the Nsight Jupyterlab Extension for profiling and streaming reports directly in Jupyter. Try the latest Nsight Python decorators for profiling from within any Python script.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Intermediate knowledge of CUDA

title: ProFusion: 3D Reconstruction of Protein Complex Structures from Multi-View AFM Images
abstract: We present ProFusion, a framework that combines atomic force microscopy (AFM) with generative AI to predict 3D structures of proteins and protein complexes. While models like AlphaFold excel at individual proteins, they struggle with large complexes. Conversely, techniques like Cryo-EM offer high accuracy, but are costly and time-intensive. ProFusion bridges this gap by coupling AFM’s multi-view imaging with a diffusion-based neural radiance field (NeRF) model, trained on a virtual AFM dataset of ~542,000 proteins with synthetic multi-view images. This new paradigm for protein structure prediction bridges the gap between AI-based methods and experimental validation. By enabling accurate, rapid, and cost-effective 3D reconstruction of protein complexes, ProFusion opens opportunities for advancing structural biology, speeding drug discovery, and supporting iterative experimental design.

title: Provide the Best Care for Patients Using Foundation Models and Agents
abstract:
The next round of innovation in healthcare is being defined by the fusion of foundation models and intelligent agents, creating systems that learn, reason, and act alongside clinicians to deliver truly personalized medicine. In this session, we explore how AI is transforming the very fabric of healthcare delivery by embedding reasoning systems directly into the flow of care.
 
Agentic AI systems, built on these models, interpret, anticipate, and act—surfacing insights at the right moment, summarizing encounters, and coordinating care across disciplines. The result: care that is faster, safer, and more human.

This talk will show how Epic is using the NVIDIA AI platform in making this future real—combining accelerated computing and specialized medical AI models and agents to enable intelligent, closed-loop healthcare systems.

title: Puma’s Smart Stores: Agents and Digital Humans Redefine Customer Interaction
abstract: In this session, we explore how agents and digital humans are transforming customer in store experiences by improving the connection with customers, employees and store operations. From answering questions in multiple languages to providing product education and recommendations, and seamless integrating with key systems, PUMA is redefining the way retailers work and the way customers shop.

title: Push the Performance Frontier of Computer Vision Models With NVFP4 Reduced Precision
abstract: Reduced-precision formats like NVFP4 are shaping the future of AI by enabling faster training and inference without compromising model accuracy. In this talk, we share best practices, insights, and lessons learned from training and deploying convolutional neural network (CNN) and transformer models using advanced reduced-precision techniques while maintaining full accuracy relative to high-precision baselines. We'll explore how reduced precision, combined with optimized CUDA kernels, delivers significant speedups for both training and inference—pushing the performance frontier of computer vision workloads on modern GPUs. This methodology can be broadly applied across industrial use cases requiring highly accurate, low-latency computer vision models, including manufacturing, medical imaging, autonomous vehicles, robotics, and more.

title: Pushing the Boundaries of CAE and EDA With NVIDIA cuDSS, the CUDA Direct Sparse Solver
abstract: NVIDIA CUDA Direct Sparse Solver (cuDSS) is increasingly being adopted in large-scale simulations for engineering and design, such as those pertinent to electronic engineering automation (EDA) and computer-aided engineering (CAE). In this session, we will cover recent advances, including most recent performance number, highlights of key features, and new APIs in cuDSS that enable users across various workflows to leverage GPU acceleration for sparse solvers.

title: PyG-RAG: Double LLM RAG Accuracy by Adding GNN
abstract: This poster covers how graph neural networks (GNNs) can be used to enhance LLMs using PyG to improve accuracy for retrieval-augmented generation (RAG)-like tasks, across any kind of data domain, by up to 2x! This will include examples on real-world data. We'll also cover how LLMs can be used to enhance GNNs for graph machine learning tasks, including a link to a full one-hour talk given at Stanford University on the topic.

speakers.name: Rishi Puri
speakers.jobTitle: Sr. Deep Learning Engineer and Lead for PyTorch Geometric

title: Python All the Way Down: Speed-of-Light CUDA Without Leaving Python
abstract:
How would you write a Python library that uses CUDA today?

Most Python GPU libraries—PyTorch, CuPy, RAPIDS, and others—still build on CUDA C++ because many core CUDA building blocks have only ever existed in C++. That’s now changing. New Python-native CUDA libraries bring formerly C++-only capabilities directly into Python, enabling high-performance GPU functionality without bindings or C++ backends. This talk shows how to build modern GPU libraries with these tools, and what becomes possible when it’s Python all the way down.

To provide a real-world example of using these new tools to build accelerated Python libraries, we will present a case study of how the Awkward Array Python library used for high energy physics at CERN is replacing its custom CUDA C++ kernels with cuda.compute, delivering tremendous speedups while making the codebase vastly easier to evolve and maintain.

title: Quantum and HPC at Riken: The Sum is Greater Than Its Parts
abstract: Discover how we realize the vision in quantum-HPC integration that "the sum is greater than its parts" by integrating the Fugaku supercomputer and NVIDIA GB200 clusters with superconducting and ion-trap QPUs in our JHPC-Quantum project. Our tightly coupled software stack, including the low-latency RPC middleware, orchestrates heterogeneous resources, and when combined with our novel quantum-HPC hybrid algorithms, we achieve utility-scale results in realistic problems in areas such as advanced quantum chemistry and computational fluid dyamics using hybrid algorithms—not just those that are advantageous to quantum. With rapid advances in both quantum and HPC systems, these will likely lead to true quantum advantages in real applications in the very near future.

title: Quantum Computing and AI
abstract:
Join us to discuss how AI is driving key breakthroughs in quantum computing. From discovering new quantum algorithms with generative AI and building AI-based quantum error correction decoders to enabling the low-latency integration of QPUs and AI supercomputing with NVQLink — whatever your interest in quantum computing, we'll show you how AI can accelerate your work. We’ll answer your questions about realizing these capabilities by architecting hybrid workflows with CUDA-Q, and show you how to leverage CUDA-Q Academic to prepare the next generation of experts to drive this convergence.

Please note: Kevin Mato and Ester de Nicolas Benito will be joining us for the Tuesday session only. We encourage you to attend this session if you'd like to hear from them directly.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Monica Van-Dieren
speakers.jobTitle: Sr. Technical Marketing Engineer, Quantum and HPC

speakers.name: Jin-Sung Kim
speakers.jobTitle: Developer Relations Manager for Quantum Computing

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod A (L2)
Connect With the Experts Pod C (L2)

title: Quantum Computing at NERSC and Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory
abstract: In this talk, we highlight how emerging quantum computing technologies are poised to impact the National Energy Research Scientific Computing Center’s scientific workload. We show how NVIDIA’s hardware and software stack already enable next-generation quantum device and application simulations, and explain how NERSC’s future quantum roadmap will build on these capabilities.

title: Quantum-Inspired Kolmogorov–Arnold Networks for Scalable and Efficient LLMs
abstract: Discover how quantum-inspired Kolmogorov–Arnold networks (QKANs) unlock new levels of efficiency and scalability in large language models. By embedding Data Re-Uploading Activations (DARUANs) as quantum variational activation functions (QVAFs), QKANs achieve exponential parameter reduction and enhanced expressivity compared to classical multilayer perceptrons (MLPs). The resulting hybrid QKAN (HQKAN) architecture serves as a drop-in replacement for transformer feed-forward blocks, enabling smaller, faster, and more energy-efficient LLMs. Accelerated through cuQuantum-optimized Tensor contractions on NVIDIA GPUs, HQKANs compress GPT- and Gemma-class models with significant parameter reduction while maintaining strong generative performance, paving the sustainable path toward next-generation AI.

speakers.name: Hsi-Sheng Goan
speakers.company: Center for Quantum Science and Engineering, National Taiwan University

title: Quick Start to Accelerated Quantum Supercomputing
abstract: Get a hands-on introduction to NVIDIA CUDA-Q, the platform for seamlessly orchestrating both quantum and classical processors alongside each other in accelerated quantum supercomputers. Whether you are new to the field or an experienced developer, this session walks you through building a hybrid application to demonstrate the execution of quantum-classical workflows across NVIDIA GPUs and IQM’s quantum hardware.

speakers.name: Monica Van-Dieren
speakers.jobTitle: Sr. Technical Marketing Engineer, Quantum and HPC

title: Qwen3-Omni：驱动下一代智能座舱的全模态交互引擎
abstract: Qwen3-Omni 作为业界首个原生端到端全模态大模型，正重新定义智能座舱的人机交互范式。其核心突破在于将“被动响应”升级为“主动感知-理解-服务”闭环，实现真正拟人化、always online 的座舱智能体。

title: Qwen3-Swallow: Continual Pre-Training of Japanese–English Thinking LLMs With Megatron-LM
abstract:
While state-of-the-art LLMs achieve remarkable reasoning in English, their Japanese capabilities often lag. We present Qwen3-Swallow — a family of Japanese–English LLMs developed through continual pre-training (CPT) of open-weight Qwen3 models using LLM-refined corpora and FP8-accelerated training.

To construct CPT data, we employed TensorRT-LLM for large-scale, cost-efficient LLM inference, accelerating dataset rewriting and synthetic data generation for Japanese, mathematical, and code domains.
During training, TransformerEngine with FP8 blockwise scaling improved computational efficiency by 20% in FLOP/s per GPU with less than 0.4% loss deviation compared to BF16. The resulting models achieved +2.5-point average improvements in Japanese instruct and few-shot tasks while preserving English proficiency, providing a scalable blueprint for sovereign AI–aligned bilingual model adaptation.

title: Rapid VSOP Animation Generation via Vision‑Language Models and Trajectory Prediction
abstract: We demonstrate a vision‑language‑model‑driven pipeline that turns a few field image uploads and text instructions into accurate visual standard operating procedure (VSOP) animations in just minutes, achieving visual quality indistinguishable from expert‑crafted 3D animations. By eliminating hours to days of manual authoring and the need for specialist 3D animation skills, our system makes VSOP creation dramatically faster and accessible to anyone.

title: RAPIDS: Real-Time Accelerated Pipeline for Intelligent Dental Segmentation
abstract: We present RAPIDS, a GPU-accelerated dual-stage deep learning framework that eliminates the dental AI annotation bottleneck by transforming 3,000 polygon annotations into 18,131 pixel-wise training samples, reducing annotation costs. Using NVIDIA TensorRT optimization, we achieve 47ms inference latency on panoramic radiographs, which is 4x faster than baseline, enabling population-scale caries screening at 680 patients per hour on a single A100 GPU. Our framework achieves superior diagnostic accuracy with IoU of 0.9013 and Dice coefficient of 0.9482 while reducing false positive rates from 15-20% to just 2.26%. Mixed precision training on V100 GPUs completes in 4.5 hours, which is 6.2x faster than CPU, cutting research cycles from weeks to days. NVIDIA GPU acceleration transforms dental AI from expensive research into practical, affordable tools for resource-limited settings worldwide.

title: Real Cloud Infrastructure for Real AI Workloads: Training and Inference at Production Scale (Presented by CoreWeave)
abstract: As dense frontier and mixture-of-experts models move toward multi-trillion parameters, AI pioneers need real cloud infrastructure ready for real-world AI workloads spanning from training trillion-parameter models to long-context inference and reinforcement learning. In this session, we will provide a deep dive on how we optimized every layer of our full stack architecture from infrastructure and orchestration to observability to run large-scale training and inference workloads efficiently to power all AI workloads, especially agentic AI. We will examine the architectural breakthroughs, and new innovations that enable AI pioneers to engineer rack-scale systems with ultra-low latency and reliability for their training and inference workloads.

title: Real-Time 3D Video Generation in Neurosurgery and Future Directions for Physical AI
abstract: To convert a single monocular surgical feed into live stereoscopic video for assistant monitors and remote viewers, we train a depth-estimation network on paired stereo data, convert depth to point clouds, and synthesize the second-eye view for consistent depth cues. Explore new techniques for edge optimization on NVIDIA Holoscan/IGX using CUDA Graphs and GPUDirect to minimize latency. Dive deep into system-level trade-offs, safety-ready verification, and pathways to integrate stereo output with robot assistance, multi-modal sensing, and low-cost clinical deployment so you can improve teamwork, instrument hand-offs, and remote training.

speakers.name: Takahashi Junichiro
speakers.company: Brain Function Analysis & Digital Medicine Research Institute, Showa Medical University

title: Real-Time Generative Video Re-Styling for Gaming in the Next-Generation Datacenter
abstract: Decart’s Lucy Restyle is a World Transformation Model that reimagines any video, game, or camera feed into a new digital world in real time. We will dive into how Decart and NVIDIA optimized Lucy Restyle inference for low-latency, cost-efficient cloud deployment on NVIDIA B40 GPUs through GeForce NOW (GFN). Finally, we’ll demonstrate how to harness Lucy Restyle to Lucy Restyle completely restyle titles via the ultimate modding platform: RTX Remix.

title: Real-Time Science and Engineering With AI Physics and Kit-CAE
abstract: Physical engineering is moving from static reports and siloed workflows to real‑time, interactive insight. This session shows how AI physics meets simulation‑driven engineering by putting NVIDIA’s GPU‑accelerated libraries and NVIDIA Omniverse open libraries first. We’ll cover accessing common computer-aided engineering (CAE) formats via OpenUSD technologies in kit-cae; high‑fidelity field visualization with RTX and IndeX; Warp‑accelerated CAE/visualization algorithms; physics NeMo surrogates made from solver-generated ground truth; and application streaming for instant, hardware‑agnostic review and collaboration. We’ll highlight current industry use cases and advancements in the area of digital twins for science and engineering. You’ll leave today with an adoption path and a clear view of how AI physics and Omniverse libraries can accelerate your organization.

title: Real-Time Simulation for Real-Time Results: How AI Physics Can Accelerate Your AI Factory
abstract:
This hands-on lab introduces mechanical and simulation engineers to GPU-accelerated, real-time CFD workflows that combine interactive visualization with physics-AI models. Participants will use NVIDIA technologies to connect CFD simulations, PhysicsNeMo, and digital twin environments for faster iteration and more reliable design insights. We will configure and run real-time CFD cases, integrate PhysicsNeMo for accelerated prediction, and visualize results interactively for design exploration. The session will show how to move from traditional batch-style CFD to responsive, GPU-driven workflows that support rapid concept evaluation and collaborative design reviews.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Familiarity with CAE workflows and core computational fluid dynamics concepts.
  • Experience with engineering simulation principles, including mesh, boundary conditions, and solver workflows.
  • Understanding of 3D scene composition, transforms, and visualization in simulation or 3D environments.
  • Basic knowledge of physics-based simulation and design optimization processes.

title: Real-time Voice Transformation with GPU Accelerated AI
abstract: Modulate’s VoiceWear enables real-time voice transformation, demanding high-fidelity audio and near-zero latency. This session details how Modulate and NVIDIA migrated critical VoiceWear workloads from CPU to GPU, significantly reducing latency within their streaming pipeline. We’ll explore the process of identifying bottlenecks, the impact of precision and framework selection on performance, and how targeted CUDA and inference-stack optimizations delivered measurable gains. Attendees will gain practical strategies for accelerating sequential, real-time ML workloads for production.

title: Real-World Federated Learning With NVIDIA FLARE
abstract:
Federated learning is transforming data-centric industries such as healthcare, life sciences, finance, and transportation — domains where privacy, compliance, and data governance are essential. NVIDIA FLARE enables collaborative model training across institutions without moving sensitive data, unlocking insights from distributed datasets that would otherwise remain siloed. Today, organizations worldwide are deploying federated learning in production, using NVIDIA FLARE to build more accurate diagnostic models, accelerate drug discovery, improve financial risk modeling, and optimize transportation systems. With Confidential Computing on NVIDIA GPUs, FLARE supports secure, decentralized pipelines that maintain data protection and execution integrity across every site, even when running complex multi-party workflows. Meet the experts behind NVIDIA FLARE and join the discussion.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod B (L2)

title: Realizing the Promise of Quantum Computation
abstract: Quantum computers are fundamentally different than conventional computers. They promise to address certain problems that are practically prohibitive, and even impossible, to solve using today’s supercomputers. The challenge is building one that is large enough to be useful. In this talk, we will provide an overview of contemporary quantum computing at an intuitive level, including the technology, the promise, the hype, the importance of high-performance classical computing in this endeavor, and the challenges ahead associated with realizing useful quantum computers at scale.

title: Recon: A Real-Time Agentic AI Platform for Intelligent Traffic Incident Detection
abstract: Dive into Recon, a real-time agentic AI platform built on NVIDIA Jetson AGX Orin that fuses TAO-based multi-object detection, VILA vision-language reasoning, and NeMo LLM-driven alert synthesis for open-world traffic incident detection. We explore how integrating multi-modal reasoning pipelines enables dynamic, zero-rule anomaly understanding — pushing the boundaries of intelligent video analytics for autonomous infrastructure monitoring. All while being completely on the edge!

title: Recursive Transformer for Vision-Language Models
abstract: We propose a novel recursive transformer for vision-language models. The idea is to recursively reuse a certain part of parameters in the transformer to pursue better parameter efficiency and reduce memory footprint. Our methods use (1) a Multi-layer Connector that maps the output feature space to the input embedding distribution to alleviate misalignment; (2) a Multi-modal Expert — an independent connector expert for vision/language tokens; and (3) Multi-step Loss to enable a flexible number of recursions. This early attempt at a recursive vision-language model achieves a consistent 4% accuracy gain in vision-language tasks on multiple datasets under the same parameter size. It thus presents an effective way to boost the performance of a vision language model by reusing its parameters. It also shows potential for building more-efficient vision language model architectures.

title: Red Hat & NVIDIA: Driving Mainstream Enterprise AI Solutions to Unlock Wide-Scale Adoption of AI (Presented by Red Hat)
abstract: Delivering a mainstream enterprise AI solution requires an ecosystem that not only works, but is engineered as a cohesive set of components that fit seamlessly to drive success. To truly provide a seamless enterprise solution, you start in the open, where the components can be visualized, vetted, and cleanly integrated. Red Hat and NVIDIA have taken those steps to begin Day 0 support for Red Hat Enterprise Linux and Red Hat OpenShift on NVIDIA systems, starting with Vera Rubin. In this talk, we will look at the work being done behind the scenes to deliver what is a fundamental change in how open moves faster to deliver an enterprise experience.

speakers.name: Scott Herold
speakers.jobTitle: Director, Product Management - Red Hat Enterprise Linux

title: Redefining Scalable AI Performance: OCI Supercomputing in the Cloud (Presented by Oracle)
abstract: Organizations building or scaling frontier AI models can’t be constrained by infrastructure. In this session, discover how Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) connects hundreds of thousands of NVIDIA GPUs. Oracle’s AI superclusters deliver up to 16 zettaFLOPS of peak performance, giving you the scale, low-latency GPU-to-GPU communication, and price-performance required for the largest AI training and inference workloads. In this session you will learn how OCI’s Acceleron ultra-low-latency RoCE networking and NVIDIA’s next-generation GPU instances dramatically increase LLM throughput, accelerate multimodal and scientific workloads, and improve cluster utilization, so you can train faster, deploy sooner, and operate more efficiently. Walk away with a clear understanding of how to move from experimentation to industrialized AI at global scale, with the performance, reliability, and sovereignty controls enterprise AI demands.

title: Redefining Vector DB Management: Uniting Massive Scalability With GPU-Accelerated Indexing (Presented by KIOXIA America)
abstract: As generative AI reshapes enterprise workloads, the rapid growth of vector databases introduces new challenges for scalability and performance. Kioxia Corp. addresses these challenges with AiSAQ, an innovative approximate nearest neighbor search (ANNS) algorithm that breaks traditional DRAM limitations by leveraging SSDs for search operations.  Now, AiSAQ adds powerful GPU utilization to vector indexing, transforming a time-consuming bottleneck into a highly accelerated workflow. In this session, discover how AiSAQ’s combination of SSD scalability and NVIDIA cuVS dramatically boosts throughput for demanding generative AI applications such as retrieval-augmented generation and semantic search. Gain insights into the benefits of integrating SSDs and GPUs, enabling robust, cost-effective solutions to meet the demands of today's AI-driven workloads.

title: Reducing the Sim2Real Gap for Industrial Robotics (Presented by Synopsys)
abstract: Robotics and physical AI have reached their “ChatGPT” moment. The reliability for industrial use requires precision dexterity, beginning with precision simulation and high sim2real fidelity, where robots learn and reason in simulation before deployment. This session highlights the collaboration between Synopsys and Analog Devices (ADI) to augment the fidelity of simulation environments for dexterity in industrial robotics. The collaboration includes an industry-standard benchmark for tactile manipulation, led by ADI and enabled by Synopsys.

title: Reimagining Store Operations at Scale to Accelerate Retail ROI
abstract: SandStar, Lawson, and True Corp. will explain how to use both NVIDIA edge AI and cloud AI solutions to change thousands of retail stores' daily operations, and attain positive return on investment (ROI) within three to six months.

title: Reimagining the OR With Physical and Digital Robotics: Innovations, Challenges, and Opportunities
abstract: The integration of physical and digital robotics into operating rooms (ORs) is revolutionizing surgical care, driving precision, efficiency, and improved patient outcomes. Join us for an insightful panel discussion as we explore cutting-edge advancements in intelligent surgical robotics systems. We'll examine technological breakthroughs in digital robotic training through physics-based simulation, autonomous surgical assistance, and real-time edge AI deployment in the OR. We'll explore how these advancements empower surgeons with enhanced precision while reducing cognitive load. You'll hear unique perspectives on the future of the operating room and how physical and digital robotics enable unprecedented surgical excellence.

speakers.name: David Niewolny
speakers.jobTitle: Sr. Director of Business Development, Healthcare/Medical

title: Reinforcement Learning at Scale: Engineering the Next Generation of Intelligence
abstract: Reinforcement learning (RL) is entering a new phase—one defined as much by systems engineering as by algorithmic innovation. In this panel, we convene industry leading experts to discuss current challenges in scaling RL along with emerging RL paradigms, which together can unlock scientific discovery, multi-modal reasoning, collaborative agents, and continual learning—the vanguard of more adaptive and intelligent systems.

title: Reinforcement Learning for Follow-the-Leader Robotic Endoscopic Navigation via Synthetic Depth Data
abstract: Autonomous navigation is essential for both medical and industrial endoscopic robots, as it enables safe and efficient exploration of narrow, often tubular, environments without continuous human intervention. We propose a follow-the-leader endoscopic robot designed to minimize contact between the central body of the endoscope and the intestinal wall during navigation, thereby reducing patient discomfort. To achieve this, we construct a realistic intestine–robot simulation in NVIDIA Omniverse for training autonomous navigation policies. Furthermore, we generate thousands of synthetic intraluminal images within Omniverse to fine-tune the Depth Anything model, enabling dense 3D perception from a single monocular camera.

title: Reinforcement Learning in the Modern AI Stack
abstract:
Explore new solutions for navigating the expanding landscape of reinforcement learning (RL) — know when to use RL, which flavor fits your domain, and what tooling accelerates your path to production. Get practical guidance on stacks, environments, and implementation strategies from hands-on practitioners.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod F (L2)

title: Reinvent Omnichannel Retail With Physical AI: Connect Stores From Edge to Cloud
abstract: Retail is no longer a choice between online and in-store. Success now depends on physical and digital experiences operating as one. Instacart is reinventing retail with AI by connecting stores, shelves, customers, and digital experiences, from edge to cloud. This session explores how Instacart applies AI across the retail stack to build real-time understanding of physical stores—one of grocery's most valuable and least digitized assets. Instacart offers enterprise solutions designed to democratize AI for grocers of all sizes, including Kroger and Sprouts. Powered by NVIDIA Jetson at the edge and NVIDIA Dynamo-Triton in the cloud, signals are processed on GPUs to continuously build and update a real-time digital model of in-store reality and inventory.

speakers.name: Azita Martin
speakers.jobTitle: VP and General Manager, Retail and CPG

title: Reinvent Semiconductor Design With Microsoft Discovery and Synopsys Agent Engineers (Presented by Microsoft)
abstract: Semiconductor teams face exploding design complexity, shrinking windows, and increasing verification challenges. This session shows how the Microsoft Discovery AI for Science platform, combined with Synopsys Agent Engineers, introduces an agentic approach to electronic design automation (EDA) that automates routine steps, accelerates expert decision‑making, and keeps data secure on Azure. Discovery’s Science Copilot orchestrates multi‑agent plans leveraging Azure AI resources and a graph‑grounded knowledge layer, while Synopsys agents drive proven flows levering domain expertise—spanning spec‑to‑RTL, simulation, timing, and sign‑off—directly on Azure HPC infrastructure. See how novel methodology integrates with existing Synopsys tools, and how NVIDIA‑accelerated Azure AI infrastructure results in faster time‑to‑PPA, fewer iterations, and more predictable schedules.

speakers.name: Prashant Varshney
speakers.jobTitle: Head of Product, Physics and Silicon

title: Reinventing Clinical Decision Support With Agentic AI and a Data Flywheel
abstract: Clinical decision support is evolving from static, rule-based systems to adaptive, agentic AI that can reason, learn, and collaborate with clinicians. This talk explores how OpenEvidence is building an agentic system that combines real-world clinical data and clinical literature to deliver context-aware insights at the point of care. It'll also discuss the critical role of a continuously learning data flywheel—where every interaction enhances reasoning, accuracy, and safety—and how this dynamic feedback loop can redefine trust and performance in medical AI systems.

title: Reinventing Security for AI at Scale
abstract: As enterprises build AI factories, massive data flows, distributed compute, and real-time inferencing push infrastructure to new limits while introducing new security risks. An AI factory is a specialized data center built for intelligence at scale, yet traditional defenses struggle to secure dynamic AI pipelines and emergent model behavior without impacting performance. This session explores how a secure-by-design architecture powered by Palo Alto Networks AIRS and accelerated by NVIDIA BlueField delivers real-time, inline protection at the speed of AI. By embedding security controls at the infrastructure layer, organizations gain agentless visibility and enforcement across the AI life cycle. AIRS detects threats, blocks prompt-level attacks, and governs agent actions as systems scale. Together, these capabilities enable trustworthy, governed, high-throughput AI operations.

title: Reverse Pitches: VC Pitches for Startups
abstract: This isn't a typical pitch session. Join us for a fresh perspective, where investors from innovative venture capital firms pitch their firm and their vision for the future of their industries. Get a firsthand look at what the next wave of startups could look like.

title: Reverse Pitches: VC Pitches for Startups
abstract: This isn't a typical pitch session. Join us for a fresh perspective, where investors from innovative venture capital firms pitch their firm and their vision for the future of their industries. Get a firsthand look at what the next wave of startups could look like.

title: Revolutionize Automated Parcel Handling With Humanoid Robots
abstract: This session unpacks how Koerber is building a humanoid “robot brain” for parcel logistics using NVIDIA Omniverse, Isaac Sim, and Jetson across simulation and real-world deployments. We’ll walk through how Unitree class humanoid hardware is trained in Omniverse scenes with integrated physics and flow simulation, how Gr00t and Cosmos are evaluated as foundation models for control, and how the same policies are deployed on Jetson-powered lines for adaptive, vision-driven and safety-aware parcel handling.

title: Revolutionize Warehouse and Shipping Operations Through AI Optimization (Presented by EY)
abstract: Discover how to transform warehouse and shipping operations in collaboration with EY using NVIDIA’s advanced AI-driven optimization technologies. This session will explore how to reboot operational designs toward achieving measurable improvements in efficiency, cost, cashflow, and safety through an innovative solution powered by NVIDIA CuOpt. Get insights into the strategic objectives and scalable deployment model, and how this approach drives rapid innovation across global logistics operations.

speakers.name: Anand Ponnuswamy
speakers.jobTitle: Global Delivery Services Supply Chain and Operations Leader

title: Revolutionizing Car Manufacturing: Inside AUDI's Fully Automated Factory
abstract: The session highlights AUDI’s strategy for integrating AI-driven robotics into both brownfield and greenfield plants, anchored by digital twins of its production facilities. These digital replicas allow AUDI to augment existing industrial robots with advanced AI capabilities, increasing line efficiency, adaptability, and overall competitiveness in the face of rapidly evolving manufacturing technologies. By operating a continuous “digital twin-to-real” loop, AUDI can monitor factory operations in near-real time, simulate changes before deployment, and rapidly adapt to new product variants or process adjustments. NVIDIA’s industrial platforms—such as Omniverse for factory-scale digital twins, Isaac for robotics AI workflows, and NVIDIA’s edge and data center AI systems—provide the compute, simulation, and optimization capabilities making this production model feasible and scalable.

speakers.name: Henning Loeser
speakers.jobTitle: Sr. Manager and Head of Audi Production Lab

title: Robot Learning for Automated Electric Vehicle Battery Recycling: Sim-to-Real for Dexterous Manipulation
abstract: At SBTC, we aim to automate electric vehicle (EV) battery dismantling to boost recycling efficiency and support a sustainable circular economy. Conventional robotics struggles with these tasks, which demand dexterity, haptic feedback, and robustness to visual localization inaccuracies and diverse battery designs. To address this, we developed a sim-to-real pipeline where dismantling skills are trained in simulation using Isaac Lab and transferred to real robots with ROS2, CuRobo, and computer vision technologies.
